centers.english: space

examples.english: I wish we had more space in our office.
examples.chinese: 我希望我们的办公室地方更大一点。

examples.english: There's a space on the form where you write the name of your school.
examples.chinese: 表格上有个地方你可以写上你们学校的名字。

examples.english: We don't have enough space for all our furniture.
examples.chinese: 我们地方不够大，放不下所有的家具。

examples.english: Could you find me a space to store these boxes in?
examples.chinese: 你给我找个地方把这些盒子放进去，好吗?

examples.english: Our apartment is small, and doesn't have much storage space.
examples.chinese: 我们的公寓很小，储藏空间不大。

examples.english: Tens of thousands of acres of farmland are swallowed up each year by developers seeking living space for the city's fast-growing population.
examples.chinese: 城市人口快速增长，每年都有许许多多的农田被寻找居住空间的开发商所侵吞。

examples.english: We help corporations to relocate, and give them advice on how to maximize office space.
examples.chinese: 我们帮助公司搬迁，并为他们如何最有效地利用办公室空间提供咨询。

examples.english: Where Marion's photo had once been was now an empty space.
examples.chinese: 曾经摆放玛丽昂照片的地方，如今空空如也。

examples.english: The city would be unbearable in the summer without its green spaces.
examples.chinese: 要不是有点绿地，夏天城里就会热得受不了。

examples.english: London's parks and open spaces
examples.chinese: 伦敦的公园和空地

examples.english: the wide open spaces of the American West
examples.chinese: 美国西部宽旷的原野

examples.english: We can't sit there, there's not enough room.
examples.chinese: 我们不能坐那儿，地方不够。

examples.english: Do you have room for this in your bag?
examples.chinese: 你的袋子里可以放得下这个吗？

examples.english: Leave room for people to get by.
examples.chinese: 留个地方让人家通过。

examples.english: He didn't think he had room to pass the car in front.
examples.chinese: 他觉得要超过前面那辆车空间不够。

examples.english: They had no room to spare in their car, so we had to take a taxi.
examples.chinese: 他们车上没有空位了，所以我们只得坐出租车。

examples.english: I enjoy flying Air Canada, because they give you plenty of leg room.
examples.chinese: 我喜欢乘加拿大航空公司的飞机，因为他们的飞机座位有宽舒的腿部空间。

examples.english: They stood in the crowd, fighting for elbow room.
examples.chinese: 他们站在人群里，推挤着找转身的余地。

examples.english: In October the museums and art galleries are less crowded, and there's more elbow room in restaurants.
examples.chinese: 到了10月份，博物馆和画廊里不那么拥挤了，餐厅里的活动空间也宽余了。

examples.english: The workshop is quite big but there's not much floorspace.
examples.chinese: 车间很大，但实用面积不多。

examples.english: There was just enough floor space for a desk, a chair, and a filing cabinet.
examples.chinese: 建筑面积仅够放一张桌子、一把椅子和一个文件柜。

centers.english: to provide space for something

examples.english: The theater was torn down in the early '80s, to make room for the Horton Plaza Shopping Center.
examples.chinese: 剧院于80年代初拆毁，腾出地方建造了霍顿广场购物中心。

examples.english: The campers made room for us around the fire.
examples.chinese: 野营的人在篝火边给我们让出了些地方。

examples.english: There are two more people coming -- can you make room for them to sit down?
examples.chinese: 还有两个人要来—你能腾个地方给他们坐吗？

examples.english: She cleared a space on her desk to put her computer.
examples.chinese: 她在书桌上腾出个地方放电脑。

examples.english: The gardener was clearing a space so he could plant the young seedlings.
examples.chinese: 园艺工正在清理出一个地方来种植幼苗。

examples.english: If you can all make way please, so we can get through.
examples.chinese: 请大家让开，让我们过去。

examples.english: The onlookers stepped back to make way for the ambulance to pass.
examples.chinese: 旁观的人往后退了退，让救护车开过去。

centers.english: ways of saying how much space there is somewhere
centers.chinese: 表示某处有多大空间的说法

examples.english: This jug holds about a pint.
examples.chinese: 这壶可以装一品脱左右。

examples.english: The hotel dining room can hold up to 50 people.
examples.chinese: 宾馆的餐厅可容纳多达50人。

examples.english: The gas tank on a small car should hold at least six gallons.
examples.chinese: 小汽车上的油箱应该至少能装6加仑汽油。

expressions.gram: transitive verb not in progressive or passive

examples.english: My car can only take five people.
examples.chinese: 我的车只能坐五个人。

examples.english: The freezer will take about 50 litres of ice cream.
examples.chinese: 这个冰柜可以放50升冰淇淋。

examples.english: I'll have to throw out some clothes - the closet can't take any more.
examples.chinese: 我得扔掉一些衣服—衣橱里再也装不下了。

examples.english: The auditorium seats 500 people.
examples.chinese: 这个大礼堂可以坐500人。

examples.english: The Boeing 747 seats 400-425 passengers.
examples.chinese: 波音747可以乘坐400到425名乘客。

examples.english: You can rent a country cottage that sleeps six from as little as £300 a week.
examples.chinese: 你可以租一栋可睡六人的乡间小屋，租金可低至300英镑一星期。

centers.english: when there is not a lot of space

examples.english: It was a tight squeeze, but everything eventually fitted into my suitcase.
examples.chinese: 我的旅行箱已经塞得很满了，但最后还是全部都装进去了。

examples.english: Put the spare bed in here - it will be a tight squeeze but it's only temporary.
examples.chinese: 把备用床放这儿，地方很挤，但这只是暂时的。

examples.english: We sell furniture specially designed for homes where space is tight.
examples.chinese: 我们经销专为空间密集的居所设计的家具。

examples.english: I've never been very good at reversing into tight parking spaces.
examples.chinese: 我一向都不大会倒车停进狭小的车位。

examples.english: Conditions on board ship were extremely cramped and uncomfortable.
examples.chinese: 船上的活动空间非常局促，令人不舒服。

examples.english: I couldn't wait to move out of my cramped apartment.
examples.chinese: 我迫不及待地想从我那个狭小的公寓房里搬出去。

centers.english: a space between two things

examples.english: Plant cells contain liquid in spaces called vacuoles.
examples.chinese: 植物细胞在被称作液泡的地方含有液体。

examples.english: The children hid in the space between the wall and the sofa.
examples.chinese: 孩子们躲在墙壁和沙发之间的空隙里。

examples.english: The space between the old building and the Morgan mansion has been converted into a marble-paved court, with plantings and a fountain.
examples.chinese: 旧楼和摩根大厦之间的那块地方已经被改建成了一个庭院，地面上铺着大理石，有花草树木，有喷泉。

examples.english: Freddie managed to squeeze through a gap in the fence and run away.
examples.chinese: 弗雷迪设法从篱笆的一个缺口挤出去逃走了。

examples.english: Melanie's dentist says that as she gets older the gap between her two front teeth will disappear.
examples.chinese: 梅拉妮的牙医说，她长大之后两颗门牙之间的缺口会消失的。

examples.english: Melianthus is a good plant for filling in gaps in flower borders.
examples.chinese: 蜜花树这种植物很适合用来填补花坛中的空隙。

examples.english: Now there is just a gap where the buildings used to stand.
examples.chinese: 曾经高楼耸立的地方，现在只剩一个缺口了。

examples.english: ‘It's only Fred,’ said Joyce, looking through an opening in the curtains.
examples.chinese: “只是弗雷德。”乔伊斯通过窗帘的一个小孔看了看说。

examples.english: The dog darted through an opening in the hedge, chasing a rabbit.
examples.chinese: 那条狗从树篱的一个缺口里冲出来，追着一只兔子。

centers.english: the world

examples.english: In some parts of the world, clean drinking water is very scarce.
examples.chinese: 世界上有些地方干净的饮用水十分短缺。

examples.english: You can buy Coca-Cola all over the world.
examples.chinese: 世界各地都能买到可口可乐。

examples.english: Scientists around the world have been conducting similar experiments.
examples.chinese: 世界各地的科学家一直在进行类似的实验。

examples.english: The ceremony was seen on television around the world.
examples.chinese: 世界各地都在电视上看到这场典礼。

examples.english: For South Africa, and indeed for the whole world, 1990 was a year of great change.
examples.chinese: 对于南非，实际上对于全世界来说，1990年是巨变的一年。

examples.english: The two countries are trying to work out a peace deal with the whole world watching.
examples.chinese: 两国在全世界的关注下力图达成和平协议。

expressions.text: the best, fastest etc in the world/the world's best, fastest etc

examples.english: It's the world's tallest building.
examples.chinese: 这是全球最高的建筑。

examples.english: At age 116, she was believed to be one of the oldest people in the world.
examples.chinese: 她116岁，被认为是全世界年纪最大的人之

examples.english: Light from the stars can take millions of years to reach Earth.
examples.chinese: 恒星发出的光可能要经过数百万年才到达地球。

examples.english: The earth revolves around the sun.
examples.chinese: 地球绕着太阳公转。

examples.english: Water is one of the Earth's most important resources.
examples.chinese: 水是地球最重要的资源之一。

examples.english: Vietnam is among the poorest nations on Earth.
examples.chinese: 卢旺达是全球最贫穷的国家之一。

examples.english: The blue whale is the largest animal on Earth.
examples.chinese: 蓝鲸是地球上最大的动物。

examples.english: The space shuttle returned safely to earth on December 9th.
examples.chinese: 12月9日，航天飞机安全返回地球。

examples.english: The massive volcanic eruption could affect the climate of the whole planet.
examples.chinese: 大规模火山爆发可能影响整个地球的气候。

examples.english: Energy conservation is vital for the future of the planet.
examples.chinese: 节约能源对于地球的未来至关重要。

examples.english: Ozone shields the planet from the effects of the sun's ultraviolet radiation.
examples.chinese: 臭氧保护地球不受太阳紫外线辐射的影响。

examples.english: Water covers over half of the globe.
examples.chinese: 水占地球面积的一半以上。

examples.english: Using satellites, television pictures can be seen on the other side of the globe almost instantly.
examples.chinese: 通过人造卫星，世界另一边的人几乎立刻就能看到电视画面。

examples.english: This garden has exotic plants from all over the globe.
examples.chinese: 这个花园里有来自世界各地的奇花异草。

centers.english: things that affect or happen everywhere in the world
centers.chinese: 影响或发生在世界各地的事

examples.english: The top 50 multi-national companies control about 80% of world trade.
examples.chinese: 前50家跨国公司控制了80%左右的全球贸易。

examples.english: At that time Britain was a major world power.
examples.chinese: 当时，英国是世界大国。

examples.english: Islam is one of the great world religions.
examples.chinese: 伊斯兰教是世界上重要的宗教之一。

examples.english: The ice skating show features twelve Olympic and world champions.
examples.chinese: 滑冰表演有12位奥运会冠军和世界冠军参加。

examples.english: Jones is a world expert in genetics.
examples.chinese: 琼斯是国际著名的遗传学专家。

examples.english: Campaigners have called for a global ban on landmines.
examples.chinese: 倡导者呼吁全球禁止使用地雷。

examples.english: Multinational companies create, in effect, a global economy.
examples.chinese: 跨国公司实际上创造了全球经济。

examples.english: Scientists at an international conference have been discussing global warming and its possible effects.
examples.chinese: 科学家在一个国际会议上讨论全球气候变暖及其可能造成的影响。

examples.english: There is a worldwide shortage of oil.
examples.chinese: 全球石油短缺。

examples.english: The concert attracted a worldwide television audience of over a billion people.
examples.chinese: 这场音乐会吸引了全世界十亿多电视观众。

examples.english: Sea levels are expected to rise worldwide by 2100.
examples.chinese: 预计到2100年全球海平面会上升。

examples.english: Some see the spread of English as an international language as just another consequence of globalization.
examples.chinese: 有些人将英语作为国际语言的传播视为全球化的又一结果。

examples.english: Globalization often means that poorer countries become too dependent on foreign investment, with the result that their own development suffers.
examples.chinese: 全球化常常意味着较贫困的国家变得过于依赖外国投资，结果自身发展受到制约。

examples.english: There were widespread anti-globalization demonstrations at the World Environmental summit yesterday, leading to the arrest of dozens of protestors.
examples.chinese: 昨天世界环境峰会召开期间发生了大规模的反全球化示威游行，许多抗议者遭逮捕。

centers.english: the direction in which someone or something is moving, aimed etc
centers.chinese: 某人或某物移动的方向、目标等

examples.english: The moons all move around the planet in the same direction.
examples.chinese: 所有的卫星都沿着相同的方向绕该行星运行。

examples.english: Frightened by the sound of footsteps, the rabbits ran off in all directions.
examples.chinese: 兔子被脚步声惊吓得四处窜逃。

examples.english: I was hoping he wouldn't look in our direction and notice us sitting there.
examples.chinese: 我当时希望他不会朝我们看，不会注意到我们坐在那里。

examples.english: The two young men headed off in the direction of Central Park.
examples.chinese: 那两个年轻男人朝中央公园方向走去。

examples.english: The sound of shots came from the direction of the compound, a quarter of a mile away.
examples.chinese: 射击声从离这儿四分之一英里远的大院那边传来。

examples.english: We're going in the right direction now - I can see the main road up ahead.
examples.chinese: 我们现在走的方向是正确的—我看得见前面的大路了。

examples.english: Bill marched off angrily in the opposite direction.
examples.chinese: 比尔愤愤地大踏步往相反方向走去。

examples.english: Tornadoes usually form when rising warm, moist air rotates, as winds from opposite directions collide.
examples.chinese: 两股相对的气流相撞，上升的暖湿气流发生旋转，通常就会形成龙卷风。

examples.english: The plane was traveling in a northeasterly direction when it was hit by lightning.
examples.chinese: 飞机遭闪电击中时正朝东北方向飞行。

examples.english: The bear went that way - you can see its tracks in the snow.
examples.chinese: 熊朝那方向走了—你可以在雪地上看到它的足迹。

examples.english: It is important to consider which way the house faces, as that determines how much sun it gets.
examples.chinese: 考虑房子的朝向很重要，因为那决定着它能晒到多少日光。

examples.english: Are you sure we're going the right way? I don't remember seeing that church before.
examples.chinese: 你肯定我们走的是正确方向吗？我记得以前没见过这座教堂。

examples.english: The captain decided to change the ship's course to avoid the storm.
examples.chinese: 船长决定改变航向以避开风暴。

examples.english: Investigators say the plane was over 800 miles off course when it crashed.
examples.chinese: 调查人员说这架飞机撞毁时偏离航线超过800英里。

centers.english: ways of asking about direction

examples.english: Which way are you going? Maybe we can share a cab.
examples.chinese: 你要往哪个方向？也许我们能同乘一辆出租车。

examples.english: ‘Excuse me, which way is the Natural History Museum?’ ‘That way. Keep walking and you'll see it on the left.’
examples.chinese: “对不起，自然历史博物馆怎么走？”“往那个方向一直走，你会看到的，它在左边。”

examples.english: Which direction do we take once we reach the top of the hill?
examples.chinese: 我们到了山顶再朝哪个方向走？

expressions.text: ask somebody how to get to/ask somebody the way/ask the way

examples.english: He looked suspicious when we asked him how to get to the border.
examples.chinese: 我们问他去边境怎么走时，他看上去对我们有疑心。

examples.english: Maggie stopped the first person she saw and asked them the way to the hospital.
examples.chinese: 玛吉叫住她看到的第一个人，问去医院的路怎么走。

examples.english: I've got no idea where we are - we'll have to stop and ask the way.
examples.chinese: 我不清楚我们在哪里。我们得停下来问问路了。

examples.english: Some people stopped as they passed, asking for directions.
examples.chinese: 有几个人经过时停了下来问路。

examples.english: I asked for directions to the town hall and made my way there on foot.
examples.chinese: 我问明了去市政厅的路，然后步行去。

expressions.text: can you tell me the way to/do you know the way to

examples.english: Can you tell me the way to the harbor, please?
examples.chinese: 请问你能告诉我去港口怎么走吗？

examples.english: Excuse me, buddy - do you know the way to the beach?
examples.chinese: 对不起，朋友，你知道去海滩怎么走吗？

examples.english: How do I get to the station from here?
examples.chinese: 我从这儿去车站怎么走？

expressions.text: how do I get there/back/home etc

examples.english: I've got her address but how do I get there?
examples.chinese: 我有她的地址，可怎么到那儿去呢？

examples.english: ‘Is this the right way to the Science Museum?’ ‘No, you're going in the wrong direction.’
examples.chinese: “这是去科学博物馆的路吗？”“不对，你走错方向了。”

centers.english: to tell someone the way to a place

expressions.text: tell somebody how to get to/tell somebody the way

examples.english: He knows how to get here. I told him the way myself.
examples.chinese: 他知道怎么来这里，我亲自告诉他的。

expressions.text: tell somebody the way to something

examples.english: Graham can tell you the way to Yvonne's house.
examples.chinese: 格雷厄姆会告诉你去伊冯娜家的路。

expressions.text: tell somebody the way out/here/there etc

examples.english: Excuse me, could you tell me the way out, please?
examples.chinese: 对不起，你能告诉我出去的路怎么走吗？

examples.english: He got into the car and began to give directions.
examples.chinese: 他坐进汽车，然后开始指路。

expressions.text: give somebody directions/give directions to somebody

examples.english: I'm not surprised you couldn't find your way here if Peter gave you directions!
examples.chinese: 如果是彼得给你指路，也就难怪你找不到来这儿的路了！

examples.english: I'll send someone with you to show you the way.
examples.chinese: 我派个人给你带路吧。

examples.english: She asked a scruffy-looking little boy to show her the way to the police station.
examples.chinese: 她请一个邋遢相的小男孩带她去警察局。

examples.english: A steward directed us behind the stage and towards the dressing rooms.
examples.chinese: 一名服务员指引我们到后台并去往化装室。

examples.english: Go and ask the patrolman - he'll direct you to the freeway.
examples.chinese: 去问问巡警，他会告诉你往高速公路怎么走的。

centers.english: the ability to guess or find the right direction
centers.chinese: 猜测出或找到正确方向的能力

examples.english: When night came we lost all sense of direction and were soon completely lost.
examples.chinese: 入夜后我们完全失去了方向感，很快就彻底迷了路。

examples.english: I can't believe you got us back to the hotel - you've got a really good sense of direction.
examples.chinese: 我真不敢相信你竟把我们带回了旅馆，你的方向感真强。

centers.english: moving up to a higher place

examples.english: The car went slowly up the hill.
examples.chinese: 汽车慢慢地开上山。

examples.english: Lee gets out of breath just going up the stairs.
examples.chinese: 李上楼梯就会气喘吁吁。

examples.english: There's a great view from the top - you should go up and have a look.
examples.chinese: 顶上风景绝佳—你该上去看看。

examples.english: The fire sent clouds of smoke up into the sky.
examples.chinese: 大火冒出滚滚浓烟冲向天空。

examples.english: Don't let the cat jump up onto the table.
examples.chinese: 不要让猫跳到桌上去。

examples.english: We made our way up to the top of the mountain.
examples.chinese: 我们一路走上山顶。

examples.english: Serena was so scared she jumped straight up in the air.
examples.chinese: 塞蕾娜害怕得直跳了起来。

examples.english: Pain was running up and down both his legs.
examples.chinese: 痛楚传遍他的双腿。

examples.english: Alan grabbed hold of the ledge and began to climb upward.
examples.chinese: 艾伦抓住岩架开始往上爬。

examples.english: A few snowflakes fell toward the ground, then blew upward with the next gust of wind.
examples.chinese: 几片雪花飘落在地上，接着一阵风又将它们吹了起来。

examples.english: The lighter material floats upwards, carrying heat to the surface of the liquid.
examples.chinese: 较轻的材料向上浮，将热量带到液体表面。

examples.english: She massaged my back with a light upward movement.
examples.chinese: 她轻轻地向上推，给我的背部做按摩。

examples.english: I don't like cycling uphill.
examples.chinese: 我不喜欢骑车走上坡路。

examples.english: The children were running uphill towards the house.
examples.chinese: 孩子们朝山上那房子跑去。

examples.english: Our guide led us uphill along a steep trail.
examples.chinese: 导游领我们沿着一条陡峭的小路上山。

examples.english: Lucy came rushing upstairs after her sister.
examples.chinese: 露茜跟着她姐姐直奔楼上来。

examples.english: Don't go upstairs - Mom's still getting dressed.
examples.chinese: 别上楼—妈妈还在穿衣服。

examples.english: Flora watched Mrs Brown staggering upstairs with a heavy tray.
examples.chinese: 弗洛拉看着布朗夫人拿着一只沉甸甸的托盘蹒跚地走上楼。

examples.english: The moon rose higher and higher.
examples.chinese: 月亮升得越来越高。

examples.english: The kite went higher and higher into the sky.
examples.chinese: 风筝在空中越升越高。

examples.english: I watched as the birds flew higher and higher, grew smaller, and then disappeared.
examples.chinese: 我看着鸟儿越飞越高，变得越来越小，接着便消失了。

centers.english: looking, facing, or pointing upwards
centers.chinese: 往上看，面朝上，或指向上面

examples.english: He held the palms of his hands upward as if he were asking forgiveness.
examples.chinese: 他掌心朝上，似乎在请求原谅。

examples.english: A copy of the book lay on the table, its cover facing upwards.
examples.chinese: 桌上摆着一本书，封面朝上。

examples.english: All eyes were turned upward toward the man standing on the ledge.
examples.chinese: 所有的目光都朝上转向站在窗台上的那名男子。

examples.english: Hector gave her an upward glance and then continued reading the paper.
examples.chinese: 赫克托抬头看了她一眼，然后继续看报纸。

examples.english: Caroline looked up and laughed.
examples.chinese: 卡罗琳抬起头来笑了。

examples.english: The boy turned and stared up at her.
examples.chinese: 那男孩转过身来，仰着头盯着她看。

examples.english: The receptionist hardly looked up from her book when I came in the office.
examples.chinese: 我进办公室的时候，接待员在看书，头都没抬一下。

examples.english: We stood there for a moment, gazing up into the snow-covered branches of the tree.
examples.chinese: 我们在那儿站了一会，抬头凝望，那树上白雪满枝。

examples.english: He was pointing his rifle straight up in the air.
examples.chinese: 他举着枪，枪口直朝天空。

examples.english: Police found the body lying face-up in the hall.
examples.chinese: 警察发现尸体面朝上地倒在大厅里。

examples.english: He put all his cards face-up on the table.
examples.chinese: 他把自己所有的纸牌面朝上地放在桌上。

centers.english: to move upwards through the air

examples.english: Mervyn had never invited her to go up in his little plane.
examples.chinese: 默文从没邀请过她乘坐自己的小飞机。

examples.english: If you want to make the kite go up, pull the string hard, then release it slowly.
examples.chinese: 放风筝要用力拉线，然后慢慢松手。

examples.english: Hot air rises.
examples.chinese: 热空气上升。

examples.english: A stream of water rose into the air, arched smoothly, and fell back into the pool.
examples.chinese: 一股水流射向空中，形成一道平滑的弧线，然后落回池子里。

examples.english: Clouds of smoke rose up into the air.
examples.chinese: 团团烟雾升到了空中。

examples.english: A huge flock of red-wing blackbirds ascended from their nests along the side of the road.
examples.chinese: 一大群红翅黑鹂从路旁的巢里飞起来。

examples.english: He leaned out of an upstairs window and felt a current of warm air ascending from the street.
examples.chinese: 他从楼上的窗户探身出去，感到一股暖空气从街上升上来。

examples.english: As the plane began to climb, Karen started to feel ill.
examples.chinese: 飞机开始爬高，卡伦开始觉得不舒服了。

examples.english: The geese climbed high above us and set off on their long journey south.
examples.chinese: 一群大雁在我们头上高飞，开始了它们漫长的南徙之旅。

examples.english: Investigators are uncertain why the plane failed to gain height after takeoff.
examples.chinese: 调查人员不明白飞机起飞后为什么无法爬高。

examples.english: Gliders use thermal up-currents to gain height.
examples.chinese: 滑翔机利用上升的热气流逐渐升高。

examples.english: Flames shot up into the air and clouds of smoke poured out of the windows.
examples.chinese: 火焰窜向空中，窗户冒出团团浓烟。

examples.english: I saw a spray of white water shoot up into the sky and knew that there were whales nearby.
examples.chinese: 我看见白色的水花喷向空中，于是知道附近有鲸鱼。

examples.english: The ball soared high into the air.
examples.chinese: 球高高地飞向空中。

examples.english: The snow goose flew down low over the field and then soared back up gracefully.
examples.chinese: 那只雪雁俯冲下来在田野上低飞，然后又优雅地飞回空中。

centers.english: when something moves upwards into the air

examples.english: Gunmen started firing at the helicopter as it left the ground.
examples.chinese: 直升机离开地面时，持枪歹徒就开始朝它射击。

examples.english: The plane had barely left the ground when it began to experience engine trouble.
examples.chinese: 飞机才离开地面就出现了引擎故障。

examples.english: Some ducks took off and flew along the river.
examples.chinese: 一些鸭子腾空而起，沿河飞翔。

examples.english: We had to wait on the runway for a half an hour before we finally took off.
examples.chinese: 我们不得不在跑道上等了半小时之后才终于起飞。

examples.english: The president's plane took off from Andrews Air Force Base at 9:45 am.
examples.chinese: 总统专机在上午9点45分从安德鲁斯空军基地起飞。

examples.english: The takeoff and landing were a little rough, but the rest of the flight was very smooth.
examples.chinese: 起降的时候有点不稳，但是飞行过程中其他时间都很平稳。

examples.english: There was a burst of flame as the rocket lifted off into the sky.
examples.chinese: 火箭升空时喷发出一柱火焰。

examples.english: Thousands of people had gathered at Cape Canaveral to watch the rocket lift off.
examples.chinese: 数以千计的人聚集在卡纳维拉尔角观看火箭发射。

examples.english: This is Houston. We have lift-off the rocket is now lifting off.
examples.chinese: 这里是休斯顿，火箭正在升空。

examples.english: The space shuttle is set to blast off on a nine-day mission tomorrow at 4:18 a.m.
examples.chinese: 航天飞机定于明天凌晨4点18分发射，执行为期九天的任务。

examples.english: Minutes after blast-off the rocket exploded.
examples.chinese: 火箭发射升空后几分钟就爆炸了。

examples.english: China is planning to launch a space rocket later this month.
examples.chinese: 中国计划于本月稍后时间发射一枚太空火箭。

examples.english: On the first day of the war over 400 missiles were launched.
examples.chinese: 开战第一天发射了400多枚导弹。

examples.english: During the launch, two rockets boost the shuttle before separating and falling back into the sea.
examples.chinese: 发射过程中，由两枚火箭推进航天飞机，然后分离，落进大海。

centers.english: to move up a slope or upstairs

examples.english: You have to go up two flights of stairs, and then it's the second door on your right.
examples.chinese: 你得爬两段楼梯，右边第二扇门就是了。

examples.english: Hundreds of people lined the street, cheering the runners as they went up the hill.
examples.chinese: 数百人在街上夹道排列，为跑上山的选手喝彩。

examples.english: The old man slowly climbed up the stairs to his room.
examples.chinese: 老人慢慢地爬上楼到自己的房间去。

examples.english: We had to climb a pretty big hill to get to the temple.
examples.chinese: 我们得爬一座相当大的山才到寺庙。

examples.english: The tour begins with a steep climb up one of the city's many hills.
examples.chinese: 旅行一开始是攀爬该市众多小山中一座陡峭的山。

examples.english: He was turning to ascend the ladder to the engine room when the ship's fire alarm sounded.
examples.chinese: 他正转身爬梯子到发动机舱去，这时船上的火警响了。

examples.english: Bianca walked regally across the hall and ascended the marble staircase.
examples.chinese: 比安卡神态雍容地穿过大厅，登上大理石楼梯。

centers.english: when a road or path goes upwards

examples.english: The road goes up from the beach into the forest.
examples.chinese: 这条路从沙滩一直通到森林。

examples.english: I could see a tiny track going up ahead of us.
examples.chinese: 我看到我们前面有一条上坡的小径。

examples.english: The road climbs steadily, reaching 6,000 feet after 18 miles.
examples.chinese: 这条路逐渐变陡，18英里后上升到6,000英尺。

examples.english: The path climbs high into the hills above the village of Glenridding.
examples.chinese: 这条小径通往格伦里丁村上面的山上。

centers.english: when the level of water goes up

examples.english: The level of the water in the lake was rising fast.
examples.chinese: 湖的水位上升得很快。

examples.english: In 1956 the river rose to a height of more than 6 metres.
examples.chinese: 1956年，河水上涨到六米多的高度。

examples.english: The waves rose higher and higher till the rocks behind them were hidden.
examples.chinese: 海浪越升越高，直到礁石都被淹没了。

examples.english: Floodwaters continue to rise as the rain continues to fall.
examples.chinese: 雨不停地下，洪水继续上涨。

centers.english: when the sun or moon comes up into the sky

examples.english: A full moon rose over the valley.
examples.chinese: 一轮圆月在山谷的上空升起。

examples.english: What time does the sun rise tomorrow morning?
examples.chinese: 明天早晨太阳什么时候升起？

examples.english: The moon rises nearly an hour later each night.
examples.chinese: 月亮每天晚上都将近晚一小时升起。

examples.english: By midday the sun had risen high in the sky and was burning down on us.
examples.chinese: 到了中午，太阳高高挂在空中，火辣辣地照在我们头上。

examples.english: The moon came up slowly over the pine trees.
examples.chinese: 月亮慢慢地升到松树林上面。

examples.english: The sun was coming up and you could just see the tops of the mountains.
examples.chinese: 太阳冉冉升起，正好可以看到山顶。

centers.english: to move a part of your body upwards
centers.chinese: 身体的某部分向上移动

examples.english: She raised her head and looked at him.
examples.chinese: 她抬头看着他。

examples.english: If you want to ask a question, please raise your hand first.
examples.chinese: 想提问的话请先举手。

examples.english: ‘Oh really?’ Zack said, raising an eyebrow.
examples.chinese: “噢，真的吗？”扎克扬起一道眉毛说。

examples.english: Her shoulder muscles had become so weak that she could not lift her arms.
examples.chinese: 她肩部的肌肉软弱无力，连胳膊都无法举起来。

examples.english: It took him a great deal of effort just to lift up his arm a few inches.
examples.chinese: 他费了很大力气才把手臂举起了一点点。

examples.english: OK, now lift your right leg up as far as it will go.
examples.chinese: 好了，现在尽量抬高右腿。

examples.english: I gasped and put up a hand to cover my mouth.
examples.chinese: 我倒抽了一口气，然后用手捂住嘴巴。

examples.english: He swore at us and put up his fists as if he was going to punch one of us.
examples.chinese: 他对我们骂骂咧咧，举起拳头作势要打人。

examples.english: Rachel put both her hands up to shield her eyes from the sun.
examples.chinese: 雷切尔举起双手挡住眼睛以避开阳光。

centers.english: to move up in a list

examples.english: With this win Williams moves up to third place in the world rankings.
examples.chinese: 威廉斯这次获胜使他的世界排名上升至第三位。

examples.english: FC Roma are slowly moving up the league table.
examples.chinese: 罗马足球俱乐部在联赛排名表上的位置在慢慢上升。

examples.english: Hobson's novel has risen steadily up the bestseller list since it's release last August.
examples.chinese: 自去年8月发行以来，霍布森的小说在畅销书排行榜上稳步攀升。

examples.english: Borland rose to the top of the computer software industry by a mixture of innovation and good marketing.
examples.chinese: 宝兰公司大胆创新而且营销有方，成了电脑软件行业的巨头。

examples.english: Jennifer Lopez's new single has climbed to number two in the US charts.
examples.chinese: 珍妮弗·洛佩兹的新单曲已升至全美排行榜第二位。

examples.english: Towards the end of the season Benfica suddenly climbed the league table and finished third.
examples.chinese: 赛季临近尾声时，本菲卡队在联赛排名表上的名次突然上升，最后排名第三。

examples.english: Since the debate Robertson has shot up in the polls.
examples.chinese: 自辩论会后，罗伯逊在民意调查中位置迅速上升了。

examples.english: The new detective series quickly shot up the TV ratings.
examples.chinese: 这部新的侦探连续剧使电视的收视率飙升。

centers.english: moving down

examples.english: I told you not to climb on that table. Get down!
examples.chinese: 我跟你说过不要爬到那张桌子上去。下来！

examples.english: He's gone down to the basement to get some more beer.
examples.chinese: 他下到地窖里再拿些啤酒来。

examples.english: The accident happened when we were coming down off the mountain.
examples.chinese: 那事故是我们下山时发生的。

examples.english: Tears began to roll down her cheeks.
examples.chinese: 眼泪开始顺着她的脸颊往下流。!

examples.english: The heat of the fire brought ceilings crashing down onto the floor.
examples.chinese: 大火的高温使屋顶轰然塌落到地上。

examples.english: She gazed down at the ring on her finger.
examples.chinese: 她低头凝视着手指上的戒指。

examples.english: The doctor glanced down at the notepad on his desk.
examples.chinese: 医生低头扫了一眼桌上的记事本。

examples.english: We came to a path winding downwards through the trees.
examples.chinese: 我们来到一条向下盘绕穿过树林的小径。

examples.english: He was gazing downward into the pit.
examples.chinese: 他往下朝洞内张望。

examples.english: We set off downhill towards the lake.
examples.chinese: 我们开始下山朝湖的方向走去。

examples.english: After we get to the top it'll be downhill all the way to Kendal.
examples.chinese: 我们到顶上之后，去肯德尔的路就一直是下坡了。

examples.english: Moments later he tripped and was falling earthwards.
examples.chinese: 过了一会儿他失足掉了下来。

examples.english: Flight 427 suddenly rolled and plunged earthward as it approached Pittsburgh.
examples.chinese: 427号航班在近匹茨堡的上空突然翻滚着栽向地面。

examples.english: He managed to pull himself free with a sudden downward movement of his hands.
examples.chinese: 他双手猛地往下一按，终于设法使自己挣脱了出来。

examples.english: She gave the bell-rope a swift downward tug.
examples.chinese: 她把铃索迅速往下一拉。

examples.english: The downward pressure on my chest became more intense.
examples.chinese: 我胸口上向下的压力更重了。

examples.english: Douglas found her lying face down on the floor.
examples.chinese: 道格拉斯发现她脸朝下卧在地上。

examples.english: She was flung violently forward, to land face down in a heap of blankets.
examples.chinese: 她迅猛地往前扑，脸朝下倒在一堆毯子上。

centers.english: to go down

examples.english: You go down a steep slope, then turn left at the bottom of the hill.
examples.chinese: 你先走下一个陡峭的斜坡，然后在山脚下往左转弯。

examples.english: Right, here's the ladder. Who's going down first?
examples.chinese: 好，这儿有梯子。谁先下去?

examples.english: I'll go down downstairs to the kitchen and get you a glass of water.
examples.chinese: 我到楼下厨房去拿杯水给你。

examples.english: Airline officials believe that the plane came down somewhere in the Andes mountains.
examples.chinese: 航空公司的官员认为飞机是在安第斯山脉的某个地方坠落的。

examples.english: One of the missiles came down in a heavily populated suburb of Beirut.
examples.chinese: 其中一枚导弹落在贝鲁特一个人口密集的郊区。

examples.english: When I let go of her hand, it dropped like a stone.
examples.chinese: 我放开了她的手，那只手有气无力地垂落下来。

examples.english: The bottle rolled across the table, dropped onto the floor, and smashed.
examples.chinese: 瓶子滚过桌面，落到地上摔碎了。

examples.english: One of your buttons has dropped off.
examples.chinese: 你有个纽扣掉了。

examples.english: Just as we were about to leave the house, rain began to fall.
examples.chinese: 我们刚要离开屋子时开始下雨了。

examples.english: Leaves were falling from the trees.
examples.chinese: 树上开始掉叶子了。

examples.english: Bombs fell on the streets, destroying neighbouring homes, but leaving the school intact.
examples.chinese: 炸弹落到街上，炸毁了附近的住房，但学校完好无损。

examples.english: Slowly the two climbers descended the cliff face.
examples.chinese: 那两名登山者慢慢地从崖壁上爬下。

examples.english: We descended into the cave by a rope ladder.
examples.chinese: 我们用绳梯爬下洞穴。

examples.english: The engine did not re-start, and the plane dived to the ground.
examples.chinese: 引擎未能再启动，飞机朝地面俯冲下去。

examples.english: The hawk stopped in mid-flight before diving down on its prey.
examples.chinese: 老鹰在半空中停下来，然后往下向猎物扑去。

examples.english: Two BF109 planes flashed past in a steep dive.
examples.chinese: 两架BF109型飞机一闪而过，笔直向地面俯冲下去。

examples.english: He loves watching planes take off and land at the airport.
examples.chinese: 他喜欢看机场上飞机起降。

examples.english: We will be landing at Singapore airport at 3 am local time.
examples.chinese: 我们将于当地时间凌晨3点在新加坡机场着陆。

examples.english: A flock of Canada geese landed on the river in front of us.
examples.chinese: 一群加拿大鹅落在我们前面的河面上。

examples.english: Captain Edwards brought the plane in for a perfect landing.
examples.chinese: 爱德华兹机长驾着飞机顺利着陆。

examples.english: The King's private plane touched down at Heathrow airport at exactly 12.15 this afternoon.
examples.chinese: 国王的私人座机于今天下午12点15分在希思罗机场准时降落。

examples.english: We will be touching down in about an hour's time.
examples.chinese: 我们大约一个小时以后降落。

centers.english: when the sun goes down

examples.english: We sat on the balcony and watched the sun go down.
examples.chinese: 我们坐在阳台上看着太阳落下。

examples.english: The sun usually sets at about 6pm at this time of year.
examples.chinese: 一年中这个时候太阳一般于下午6点落下。

examples.english: The setting sun cast a deep red glow over the city's rosy stone.
examples.chinese: 夕阳在城市的粉红色石头上映出一片深红色的光芒。

examples.english: The sun sank and darkness fell on the island.
examples.chinese: 太阳落下了，黑暗笼罩了那座岛。

examples.english: As the sun sank lower and lower, the sky first turned pink and then orange.
examples.chinese: 太阳渐渐下沉，天空先是呈现出一片粉红色，接着又变成了橙红色。

examples.english: We sat and watched the glorious sunset from across the Mekong river.
examples.chinese: 我们坐在湄公河对岸观赏着壮丽的日落景象。

examples.english: You may prefer to sit on the terrace sipping a cocktail at sunset.
examples.chinese: 你也许更喜欢在日落时分坐在阳台上喝喝鸡尾酒。

centers.english: to go down under the surface of water, mud etc

examples.english: Hundreds of passengers tried desperately to escape from the boat as it sank.
examples.chinese: 船下沉时，数百名乘客拼命地想逃离。

examples.english: The guns sank deeper and deeper into the mud.
examples.chinese: 大炮在泥中越陷越深。

examples.english: With the car sinking into a marsh, there wasn't a moment to spare.
examples.chinese: 汽车在泥潭中不断下沉，要刻不容缓地采取行动。

examples.english: There was a sucking noise, and then the branch sank without trace.
examples.chinese: 树枝咕噜一声沉入水中不见了。

examples.english: He swam away from the sinking ship.
examples.chinese: 他从正在下沉的船边游开。

examples.english: She stood at the edge of the pool waiting to dive.
examples.chinese: 她站在水池边等着跳水。

examples.english: Ralph dived into the icy water.
examples.chinese: 拉尔夫跳入冰水中。

examples.english: A woman dived in to rescue the boy.
examples.chinese: 有个女人跳入水中去救那男孩。

examples.english: She did a perfect dive from the top board.
examples.chinese: 她从最高的跳板上完成了一次完美的跳水。

examples.english: Colin plunged into the icy water.
examples.chinese: 科林纵身跳入冰水中。

examples.english: I plunged in fully-clothed and pulled her to the river bank.
examples.chinese: 我没脱衣服就纵身跳入水中，把她拖到河岸上来。

examples.english: Peter pulled on the mask and submerged again.
examples.chinese: 彼得拉上面罩，再一次潜入了水中。

examples.english: elephants submerging themselves in cooling water
examples.chinese: 没入凉水中的象群

examples.english: The divers explored the submerged wreck.
examples.chinese: 潜水员探寻沉船的残骸。

examples.english: By the time the sea rescue service arrived, the ship had gone under.
examples.chinese: 海上救援队赶到时，船已沉没了。

examples.english: According to the only survivor, his comrades went under one by one.
examples.chinese: 根据唯一的幸存者描述，他的同伴一个接一个地沉了下去。

centers.english: to make a plane, boat, etc go down

examples.english: Submarines were used to sink the enemy's supply ships.
examples.chinese: 潜艇曾被用来击沉敌方的补给船。

examples.english: One battleship was sunk and two were badly damaged in last night's fighting.
examples.chinese: 在昨晚的战斗中一艘战舰被击沉，两艘遭到重创。

examples.english: In May 1960 the Russians shot down an American U2 spy plane.
examples.chinese: 1960年5月，苏联人击落了美国一架U2侦察机。

examples.english: He followed the dive-bomber round in a very tight turn and shot it down into the sea.
examples.chinese: 他紧追着那架俯冲轰炸机，一个急转弯后将它击落到海里。

examples.english: One helicopter gunship had been brought down by small-arms fire.
examples.chinese: 一架武装直升机被轻武器火力击落。

centers.english: to move something down or let it move down
centers.chinese: 把某物移下或让其降低

examples.english: You've dropped your handkerchief.
examples.chinese: 你的手帕掉了。

examples.english: Be careful not to drop that bowl, it's very valuable.
examples.chinese: 小心别把那只碗掉到地上，它很名贵的。

examples.english: I nearly dropped my glass on the floor when they said I'd won.
examples.chinese: 他们说我赢了时，我差点把杯子掉到地上。

examples.english: Put the gun down.
examples.chinese: 把枪放下。

examples.english: OK, you can all put your hands down now.
examples.chinese: 好，现在大家把手放下。

examples.english: We need to lower the mirror in the bathroom.
examples.chinese: 我们要把浴室中的镜子放低些。

examples.english: Lowering its head, the bull charged at him.
examples.chinese: 那头公牛低下头朝他冲了过去。

examples.english: The coffin was lowered slowly into the ground.
examples.chinese: 棺材被慢慢地放入地下。

examples.english: The old man lowered himself wearily into his chair.
examples.chinese: 老人疲惫地撑着坐进椅子里。

centers.english: when something hangs down

examples.english: He stood very still, his arms hanging loosely, his feet apart.
examples.chinese: 他一动不动地站着，双臂松垂，双脚分开。

examples.english: In the corner of the room was a large lamp, hanging from the ceiling.
examples.chinese: 房间一角的天花板上悬着一盏大吊灯。

examples.english: The watch was on a gold chain that hung from his belt.
examples.chinese: 表上系着金链子，挂在他的腰带上。

examples.english: Hold one end of the rope in your hand and let the other end hang down.
examples.chinese: 握住绳子一端，让另一端垂下。

examples.english: The keys are hanging on a nail by the door.
examples.chinese: 钥匙挂在门边的一枚钉子上。

examples.english: A picture of their parents hangs over the bedroom door.
examples.chinese: 卧室的门上挂着他们父母的一幅照片。

examples.english: Dangling from her ears were two large gold earrings.
examples.chinese: 她的双耳上悬垂着两只大金耳环。

examples.english: He dangled helplessly from the cliff, trying not to look down.
examples.chinese: 他无助地吊在悬崖上，尽量不往下看。

examples.english: We sat on the edge of the pool with our legs dangling in the water.
examples.chinese: 我们坐在池边，双腿荡在池水中。

examples.english: A row of hares were suspended by their feet outside the butcher's shop.
examples.chinese: 一排野兔腿朝上倒吊在肉铺外面。

examples.english: All sorts of artefacts were suspended from the ceiling.
examples.chinese: 屋顶下悬挂着各种各样的人工制品。

examples.english: A lantern swung from a hook in the roof.
examples.chinese: 灯笼在屋顶下的一个钩子上荡来荡去。

examples.english: A small jewelled cross swung from a gold chain around her neck.
examples.chinese: 一个镶了珠宝的小十字架在她脖子上的金链上晃荡。

centers.english: to move down to a lower position in a list
centers.chinese: 在排名表上降至较低位置

examples.english: The pound fell against the Euro again today.
examples.chinese: 今天英镑兑欧元的汇价又下跌了。

examples.english: Within the space of a few minutes, the British song fell from top to bottom position.
examples.chinese: 仅仅隔了几分钟，那首英国歌曲从榜首直落榜尾。

examples.english: Aston Villa fell 3 places in the league after their defeat by Barnsley.
examples.chinese: 阿斯顿维拉队被巴恩斯利队击败后在联赛中的排名下降了三位。

examples.english: Mansell has now slipped to third position.
examples.chinese: 曼塞尔现已退居第三位。

examples.english: The American slipped from second place to fourth.
examples.chinese: 这个美国人从第二位落到第四位。

examples.english: The team has been slipping down the league table and really needs some new players if it is to improve.
examples.chinese: 该队在联赛榜上已经落后了，如果要提高排名，实在需要引进一些新球员。

centers.english: light from the sun, a fire, an electric light etc

examples.english: Light was coming into the room through a crack in the door.
examples.chinese: 一道亮光从门缝里照进房间。

examples.english: a gas lamp that gives as much light as a 100 watt bulb
examples.chinese: 有100瓦灯泡那么亮的煤气灯

examples.english: The light was fading, and I was afraid we wouldn't be home before dark.
examples.chinese: 天色渐暗，恐怕天黑之前我们到不了家。

examples.english: The light isn't good enough to take a photograph.
examples.chinese: 这光线拍照不够明亮。

examples.english: In the fading light she could just make out the shape of a tractor.
examples.chinese: 灯光渐渐昏暗下去，她只能看到一辆拖拉机的影子。

examples.english: The valley was bathed in the soft light of dawn.
examples.chinese: 山谷沐浴在柔和的晨曦中。

examples.english: the cold blue light of the Arctic
examples.chinese: 北极的蓝色冷光

examples.english: a sudden flash of blinding light
examples.chinese: 突然一道炫目的光

expressions.text: by the light of the moon/the fire/a candle

examples.english: She sat reading by the light of the fire.
examples.chinese: 她坐在那里借着炉火的光看书。

examples.english: Let's go now while it's still light.
examples.chinese: 趁天还亮我们现在就走吧。

examples.english: It's not light enough to play outside.
examples.chinese: 现在出去玩天不够亮。

examples.english: I'd like to look at the house again in daylight.
examples.chinese: 我想白天再看看这房子。

examples.english: The park is open during daylight hours.
examples.chinese: 这公园白天开放。

examples.english: Her long blonde hair was shining in the sunlight.
examples.chinese: 她金黄色的长发在阳光下闪闪发亮。

examples.english: We emerged from the dark forest into the sunlight.
examples.chinese: 我们从黑暗的森林里走到明亮的阳光下。

examples.english: Keep the plant out of direct sunlight.
examples.chinese: 这种植物要避免阳光直接照射。

examples.english: The trees looked strangely white in the moonlight.
examples.chinese: 树木在月色中白得出奇。

examples.english: Moonlight came in through the curtains, lighting up the children's sleeping faces.
examples.chinese: 月光透过窗帘，照在孩子们熟睡的脸上。

examples.english: the glare of the car's headlights
examples.chinese: 车头灯打出的强光

examples.english: The heat and glare of the furnace is immense.
examples.chinese: 炉火烧得极热，火光非常刺眼。

examples.english: Candles give a warm glow to the room.
examples.chinese: 烛光融融，照亮着房间。

examples.english: the orange glow of the sunset
examples.chinese: 落日的红霞

examples.english: We could see the beams of searchlights scanning the sky.
examples.chinese: 我们看到探照灯的光束扫射着天空。

examples.english: Maggie stumbled across the field with only a narrow beam of light from her flashlight to help her.
examples.chinese: 玛吉跌跌撞撞地在田野里走，只有她手电筒的一小束光给她照路。

examples.english: Use a sunscreen to protect your skin against the sun's harmful rays.
examples.chinese: 使用防晒霜保护你的肌肤，使肌肤免受太阳有害射线的伤害。

examples.english: The first rays of the sun pierced the canopy of leaves above us, and the forest began to wake up.
examples.chinese: 第一缕阳光透过我们头顶茂密的树叶照射下来，森林开始苏醒了。

centers.english: to make a place light

examples.english: Fireworks lit up the night sky.
examples.chinese: 烟花照亮了夜空。

examples.english: Their garden was lit up by dozens of coloured lamps.
examples.chinese: 他们的花园里点亮着许多彩灯。

examples.english: What are you going to use to light the stage?
examples.chinese: 你打算用什么给舞台照明呢？

examples.english: The room was lit by dozens of candles.
examples.chinese: 房间里给许多蜡烛照亮着。

examples.english: Can you put the light on? I can't see anything!
examples.chinese: 你把灯打开好吗？我什么也看不见！

examples.english: When I turned on the light, I realized the room was in chaos.
examples.chinese: 我开了灯，发现房间乱七八糟。

examples.english: Small lights illuminate different points on the map.
examples.chinese: 一盏盏小灯照亮着地图上不同的点。

examples.english: The blazing fire illuminated the china ornaments above the hearth.
examples.chinese: 熊熊的火光照亮着壁炉上方的瓷器饰品。

examples.english: A small path was illuminated by low orange lamps concealed in the flower beds.
examples.chinese: 一条小路由隐藏在花坛里的橘黄色的低灯照亮着。

centers.english: when the lights in a place are on

examples.english: Although the lights were on, nobody answered the door.
examples.chinese: 尽管灯亮着，但没人来开门。

examples.english: The lights are still on in a couple of the offices.
examples.chinese: 有几间办公室里仍然亮着灯。

examples.english: People were getting ready for dinner, and the house was all lit up.
examples.chinese: 人们正准备吃晚餐，房子里灯火通明。

examples.english: In the town centre, the streets are all lit up for Christmas.
examples.chinese: 市中心的街道上灯火齐明，准备过圣诞节了。

examples.english: The mosque is lit up by floodlights at night.
examples.chinese: 夜里清真寺被泛光灯照得通亮。

examples.english: The yacht passed us, its cabin lights ablaze.
examples.chinese: 游艇从我们身旁驶过，它的船舱里灯火辉煌。

examples.english: Every shop window is ablaze with bright Christmas lights.
examples.chinese: 家家商店的橱窗里都被圣诞节的灯饰照得通明透亮。

centers.english: not heavy

examples.english: You can carry this bag -- it's fairly light.
examples.chinese: 你可以拎这个包—它很轻。

examples.english: Modern tennis rackets are much lighter than old-fashioned wooden ones.
examples.chinese: 现代的网球拍比以前的木拍子轻多了。

examples.english: Heat rises because hot air is lighter than cold air.
examples.chinese: 热气往上升是因为热空气比冷空气轻。

examples.english: She was light as a feather to carry, and her hands were cold as ice.
examples.chinese: 把她背在身上轻如无物，她的双手也冷冰冰的。

examples.english: a lightweight summer suit
examples.chinese: 轻便的夏服

examples.english: a strong, lightweight material such as titanium
examples.chinese: 一种质硬而轻的材料，如钛

examples.english: Today's baby buggies are lightweight, compact, and easy to fold.
examples.chinese: 现在的婴儿车轻便小巧，容易折叠。

examples.english: Astronauts have problems moving around in the spacecraft because they are virtually weightless.
examples.chinese: 宇航员在太空船里活动有困难，因为他们基本上处于失重状态。

examples.english: It takes a shuttle crew around a day to get used to the weightlessness of space.
examples.chinese: 航天飞机机组人员要花一天左右才能习惯太空里的失重状态。

centers.english: to make something lighter

examples.english: They had taken out all the drawers to make it lighter, before carrying it down the stairs.
examples.chinese: 他们把它搬下楼之前，取掉所有的抽屉，好让它轻一些。

examples.english: Many parts are now made from plastic instead of steel, making cars lighter and more fuel-efficient.
examples.chinese: 现在许多汽车部件都用塑料取代了钢，使汽车更轻更省油。

examples.english: I'm sure you could lighten that suitcase a little if you only packed what you need.
examples.chinese: 我相信，如果你只装些必需的东西，手提箱就会轻一些的。

examples.english: As weather conditions got worse, Watts and Peters abandoned their photographic equipment in order to lighten the load.
examples.chinese: 天气变得越来越糟，沃茨和彼得斯为了减轻负担，丢弃了他们的摄影器材。

centers.english: dark and with little or no light
centers.chinese: 暗的，只有一点点光或没有光

examples.english: Thick curtains covered the windows and the room was very dark.
examples.chinese: 厚厚的窗帘遮住了窗，房间很暗。

examples.english: I shrank back into the darkest corner of the room, and prayed that the soldiers would not see me.
examples.chinese: 我后退躲进房间最阴暗的角落里，祈求这些士兵不会看到我。

examples.english: No, you can't play outside, it's too dark.
examples.chinese: 不行，你不能出去玩，外面太黑了。

examples.english: It was a dark night and he was afraid they might get lost if they went across the fields.
examples.chinese: 那是个漆黑的夜晚，他担心如果他们穿越田野也许会迷路的。

examples.english: Anyone who disobeyed him ran the risk of getting beaten up in a dark alley, or even killed.
examples.chinese: 谁敢不听命于他，就有在黑暗的小巷里被痛揍一顿甚至被杀死的危险。

examples.english: It's pitch dark in there. I can't see a thing.
examples.chinese: 那里一片漆黑，我什么都看不见。

examples.english: Inside the cellar it was pitch black.
examples.chinese: 地窖里伸手不见五指的。

examples.english: The bar was gloomy and smelled of stale cigar smoke.
examples.chinese: 酒吧里昏暗阴沉，有股浑浊的雪茄味。

examples.english: I never liked visiting Dr Allen in his gloomy old study.
examples.chinese: 我向来不喜欢到艾伦博士那阴暗的旧书房里去。

examples.english: He ate lunch in a dingy little cafe next to the station.
examples.chinese: 他在火车站隔壁一家昏暗肮脏的小餐馆里吃了午饭。

examples.english: The room was damp and dingy.
examples.chinese: 这房间又湿又暗。

examples.english: The prisoner lay in a darkened room.
examples.chinese: 囚犯躺在一间黑暗的房间里。

examples.english: The production opens with a darkened stage, and the sound of a woman singing softly.
examples.chinese: 这出戏开场时是昏黑的舞台，一个女声在柔声轻唱。

examples.english: a long, dimly-lit corridor
examples.chinese: 一条灯光昏暗的长走廊

examples.english: Madame Gloriana led the way into a dimly lit back room.
examples.chinese: 格罗丽亚娜夫人带路走进一间灯光昏暗的密室里。

examples.english: The path was unlit, and she needed a torch to find her way.
examples.chinese: 路上暗无灯光，她需要一只手电筒才能看清方向。

examples.english: Behind the gasoline pumps the unlit garage stood like a huge black shadow.
examples.chinese: 汽油泵后面暗无灯光的车库像一个巨大的黑影。

centers.english: to become dark

examples.english: It was getting dark, and we were worried that we wouldn't make it back to the village before nightfall.
examples.chinese: 天色变暗了，我们担心黑夜到来之前赶不回村庄。

examples.english: When we were camping we used to go to sleep as soon as it got dark.
examples.chinese: 以前我们野营时总是天一黑就睡觉。

examples.english: It's getting very dark out there -- there's going to be a storm.
examples.chinese: 外面天很黑了—暴风雨就要来了。

examples.english: The light slowly began to fade and the trees became mere shadows.
examples.chinese: 天色渐暗，树木成了一株株的暗影。

examples.english: I want to take some photographs before the light fades.
examples.chinese: 光线暗下来之前我要拍几张照片。

examples.english: In a few minutes the sky darkened and heavy rain began to fall.
examples.chinese: 过了几分钟，天色就暗了下来，并开始下起大雨。

examples.english: We walked along the shore as the sun's last rays winked over the darkening sea, then headed for home.
examples.chinese: 落日最后的余晖映照在渐暗的海面上时，我们沿海岸散着步，随后就回家去。

examples.english: We got back home just as night was falling.
examples.chinese: 夜幕渐渐降临时我们刚好回到了家。

examples.english: Darkness fell on the town and the streetlights came on one by one.
examples.chinese: 夜幕降临了，镇上的路灯一盏一盏地亮了起来。

examples.english: Suddenly the light went out and the narrow stairs were plunged into darkness.
examples.chinese: 灯突然熄灭了，狭窄的楼梯陷入一片黑暗之中。

examples.english: Lightning struck the power lines, plunging half the city into darkness.
examples.chinese: 雷电击中了电线，半个城市突然陷入了黑暗之中。

centers.english: darkness

examples.english: A voice came from out of the darkness, but she couldn't see anyone.
examples.chinese: 一个声音从暗处传来，可她看不见任何人。

examples.english: As my eyes became used to the darkness I could make out a bed in the corner of the room.
examples.chinese: 当我的眼睛适应了黑暗时，我看到房间的角落里有一张床。

examples.english: The city was a violent place at that time, and it was not safe to walk the streets during the hours of darkness.
examples.chinese: 那时候这座城市里充斥着暴力，天黑以后在街上走很不安全。

examples.english: Colour films must be developed in complete darkness.
examples.chinese: 彩色胶卷一定要在漆黑的地方冲洗。

examples.english: Children who are afraid of the dark need to be reassured.
examples.chinese: 对怕黑的小孩子要给予安慰鼓励。

examples.english: Why are you sitting there in the dark? Put the light on.
examples.chinese: 你为什么坐在那一片黑暗里？把灯打开。

examples.english: Someone was hiding in the shadows at the end of the garden.
examples.chinese: 有人躲在花园尽头的阴影处。

examples.english: Two figures moved out of the shadows into the moonlit street.
examples.chinese: 两个人影从阴暗处出来，走到月光下的街道上。

examples.english: He urged the mule forward through the half-light of the forest.
examples.chinese: 他赶着骡子穿过光线灰暗的树林。

examples.english: It was difficult to see who was standing there in the dim half-light of the hall.
examples.chinese: 大厅里昏暗不明，很难看清谁站在那里。

examples.english: I stepped through the doorway and peered into the gloom.
examples.chinese: 我走过门道，眼前一片黑暗。

examples.english: Jon could hear her voice but was unable to see anything in the gloom.
examples.chinese: 乔恩听得到她的声音，但在幽暗中什么也看不见。

centers.english: when light comes from the sun, a lamp, a surface etc
centers.chinese: 光从太阳、灯、表面反射等而来

examples.english: It wasn't very warm, but at least the sun was shining.
examples.chinese: 天气不是很暖和，但起码还有太阳照耀。

examples.english: She could see the lights of Hong Kong shining in the distance.
examples.chinese: 她能看到远处的香港灯火闪耀。

examples.english: A light shone in a window of one of the houses.
examples.chinese: 其中有一幢房子窗户里亮着灯。

examples.english: Could you move that lamp? It's shining right in my eyes.
examples.chinese: 你把那盏灯移一下好吗？它正好照着我的眼睛。

examples.english: The streetlights shone brightly and the sidewalks were filled with people.
examples.chinese: 街灯闪亮，人行道上游人如织。

examples.english: The evening sun glowed in the sky.
examples.chinese: 夕阳映红了天空。

examples.english: A few lumps of coal still glowed in the fire.
examples.chinese: 一些煤块仍然在火堆里灼灼发光。

examples.english: The windows were glowing with a warm, yellow light.
examples.chinese: 窗户里映着融融的黄色灯光。

examples.english: There faces were lit by the warm glow of the fire.
examples.chinese: 他们的脸被融融的火光照亮了。

examples.english: A Rolls Royce was parked outside, gleaming in the sunshine.
examples.chinese: 一辆劳斯莱斯车停在外面，在阳光下熠熠生辉。

examples.english: The floors gleamed, and the house smelled sweetly of soap and fresh air.
examples.chinese: 地板光滑发亮，屋子里空气新鲜，散发着肥皂的清香。

examples.english: The old walnut dining table gleamed under the chandelier.
examples.chinese: 古旧的胡桃木餐桌在枝形大吊灯下面发着亮光。

examples.english: On his left was the galley, a tiny kitchen gleaming with stainless steel.
examples.chinese: 他的左边是厨房，厨房小小的，不锈钢餐具闪闪发亮。

examples.english: In the candlelight, Nula's gleaming hair fell like silk on her shoulders.
examples.chinese: 烛光下，努拉亮闪闪的秀发丝一般地垂到她肩上。

examples.english: I heard the back door of the house open, and saw the gleam of a lantern.
examples.chinese: 我听见屋子的后门开了，接着看到了灯笼的亮光。

examples.english: The midday sun blazed down on us.
examples.chinese: 正午的太阳火辣辣地照在我们身上。

examples.english: The windows of the cathedral were blazing with coloured light.
examples.chinese: 教堂的窗户里透出灿烂多彩的灯光。

examples.english: Lights blazed in every room in the house.
examples.chinese: 屋子里每个房间都灯火通明。

examples.english: A line of camels moved across the dunes under a blazing sky.
examples.chinese: 在耀眼的天空下，一个驼队越过沙丘在沙漠上行进。

examples.english: The rabbit stopped, caught in the blaze of the car's headlights.
examples.chinese: 兔子停了下来，在车头灯的照耀下被捉住了。

examples.english: Lightning flashed across the sky.
examples.chinese: 一道闪电划过天空。

examples.english: A police car sped through the intersection, lights flashing.
examples.chinese: 一辆警车闪着警灯在十字路口疾驰而过。

examples.english: Why did that guy flash his headlights at me?
examples.chinese: 那个家伙为什么朝我闪车头灯?

examples.english: There was a bright flash of light as the bomb exploded.
examples.chinese: 炸弹爆炸时发出一道强光。

examples.english: The candle flickered a few times and then went out.
examples.chinese: 烛光闪烁了几下就熄灭了。

examples.english: The lights flickered; I wondered if we were about to lose our power.
examples.chinese: 灯一闪一闪的，我就想，我们是不是要没电了呢。

examples.english: When I got in, the message light on my answering machine was blinking.
examples.chinese: 我进门时，我的电话答录机上的留言指示灯在闪烁。

examples.english: The neon lights on the theater blinked red and blue.
examples.chinese: 剧院上面的霓虹灯忽红忽蓝，闪烁不停。

centers.english: to shine with small bright points of light
centers.chinese: 发出一闪一闪的小小亮光

examples.english: She wore a diamond necklace, which sparkled in the light of the fire.
examples.chinese: 她戴着一条钻石项链，在火光下闪闪发亮。

examples.english: Dwayne's eyes sparkled as he laughed.
examples.chinese: 德伟恩笑起来两眼闪出光彩。

examples.english: When the sun came up, the snow sparkled as if it were studded with millions of diamonds.
examples.chinese: 太阳升起的时候，白雪晶莹透亮，犹如镶嵌了无数的宝石。

examples.english: a pair of beautiful sparkling earrings
examples.chinese: 一副亮闪闪的漂亮耳环

examples.english: The lights of the town twinkled faintly in the distance.
examples.chinese: 远处小镇灯火幽幽，忽明忽暗。

examples.english: Over her shoulder, the stars twinkled in the black sky.
examples.chinese: 她的头顶上，黑色的夜空繁星闪烁。

examples.english: The frost glittered on the ground.
examples.chinese: 地上的霜晶莹闪亮。

examples.english: Jewels glittered in the dim light of the cave.
examples.chinese: 珠宝在山洞里昏暗的光线下闪闪发光。

examples.english: The chandelier glittered, its crystal teardrops like small golden suns.
examples.chinese: 大吊灯晶莹璀璨，它的水晶坠子犹如一个个金色的小太阳。

examples.english: When he moved, his silk green shirt shimmered.
examples.chinese: 他走动的时候，他的绿色真丝衬衫一闪一闪的。

examples.english: The lake shimmered in the moonlight.
examples.chinese: 月光下，湖面波光粼粼。

examples.english: The grey roofs glistened after the rain.
examples.chinese: 雨后，灰色的屋顶黑得发亮。

examples.english: When we finished the set, Katie's face was red and glistening with sweat.
examples.chinese: 我们打完这盘网球，凯蒂的脸红红的，汗珠闪闪发亮。

centers.english: when something shines in the dark

examples.english: It's a good idea to paint your bike with luminous paint so that you are more visible to motorists.
examples.chinese: 用发光漆漆自行车这个办法很好，这样机动车司机就能更清楚地看到你。

examples.english: He couldn't tell what time it was since his watch didn't have a luminous dial.
examples.chinese: 他不知道几点了，因为他的表盘上没有夜光。

examples.english: If you plan to jog along roadsides, it's a good idea to wear at least one article of fluorescent clothing.
examples.chinese: 如果你打算在路边慢跑，身上起码要穿一件有荧光的衣服。

examples.english: I'm sorry, but fluorescent green socks are just not suitable for a job interview.
examples.chinese: 荧光的绿色袜子恐怕不适合在面试的时候穿。

examples.english: One of the children was playing with a yo-yo which glowed in the dark.
examples.chinese: 其中的一个孩子在玩溜溜球，那溜溜球在黑暗中发着光。

examples.english: Outside the cathedral, vendors were selling small statues of the Virgin which glowed in the dark.
examples.chinese: 教堂外面，小贩们在卖圣母像，小小的圣母像在黑暗中幽幽发光。

examples.english: Dickie was dressed in a Day-Glo orange vest, jeans and running shoes.
examples.chinese: 迪基穿着一件橙色的荧光背心、一条牛仔裤和一双跑鞋。

examples.english: Outside the club, a woman was handing out day-glo green fliers.
examples.chinese: 俱乐部外面，一名妇女在分发荧光绿色的广告传单。

centers.english: having a surface that shines

examples.english: She wore a fashionable jacket and high shiny boots.
examples.chinese: 她穿着一件时髦的夹克衫，一双擦得光亮的高统靴子。

examples.english: At 7:30 p.m. sharp, a shiny limousine pulled in front of the building.
examples.chinese: 晚上7点30分正，一辆闪亮的豪华轿车准时停在大楼的前面。

examples.english: To keep the surface shiny, apply a clear, high-gloss varnish.
examples.chinese: 要保持表面光滑发亮，涂一层高亮度的透明罩光漆。

examples.english: She stroked the horse's long glossy neck.
examples.chinese: 她抚摸着那匹马光滑发亮的长脖子。

examples.english: There was a stack of glossy magazines on the coffee table.
examples.chinese: 咖啡桌上有一叠用有光纸印刷的杂志。

examples.english: The cat purred as Ben stroked its sleek fur.
examples.chinese: 本抚摸着猫咪光滑发亮的毛，猫咪发出呼噜呼噜的声音。

examples.english: Linda looked wonderful at the party; her hair was long and sleek, her make-up perfect.
examples.chinese: 聚会上，琳达看上去美极了。她一头长发光滑亮泽，化妆无可挑剔。

examples.english: You're so lucky to have such lovely, silky hair.
examples.chinese: 你真幸运，有这样美丽光滑的秀发。

examples.english: Her skirt and jacket were made of smooth, silky fabric.
examples.chinese: 她的裙子和上衣是用一种像真丝一样柔滑的面料做的。

examples.english: Her nails were beautifully shaped and polished.
examples.chinese: 她的指甲修剪得很漂亮，擦得亮亮的很好看。

examples.english: a polished oak floor
examples.chinese: 擦得亮亮的橡木地板

examples.english: Every surface in the kitchen was polished and gleaming.
examples.chinese: 厨房里所有的台面都擦得亮光光的。

examples.english: A gleaming Harley Davidson motorcycle stood parked outside the bar.
examples.chinese: 一辆闪闪发亮的哈雷-戴维森牌摩托车停在酒吧的外面。

examples.english: They looked out across the shimmering water of the lagoon.
examples.chinese: 他们望过去，潟湖湖面波光粼粼。

examples.english: Gabby pulled a shimmering gold evening dress out of a box and held it in front of her.
examples.chinese: 加比从箱子里拉出一件金光闪闪的晚礼服，把它握在胸前。

examples.english: His glistening bald head moved from side to side in time to the music.
examples.chinese: 他那发亮的光头随着音乐的节奏左右摆动。

examples.english: The harpoon sank into the whale's glistening skin.
examples.chinese: 捕鲸叉一下子插入了鲸鱼闪亮的表皮。

centers.english: having a surface that shines like metal
centers.chinese: 表面像金属一样发亮的

examples.english: There was a metallic grey VW Golf parked outside cabin fifteen.
examples.chinese: 15号小屋的外面停着一辆金属灰色的大众高尔夫汽车。

examples.english: Small silvery fish darted through the shallow water of the creek.
examples.chinese: 银色的小鱼在溪涧浅水的地方穿梭。

examples.english: The hills looked silvery in the moonlight.
examples.chinese: 群山在月光下银光闪亮。

centers.english: the shiny appearance of something

examples.english: They polished their boots to a dazzling shine.
examples.chinese: 他们把靴子擦得锃亮。

examples.english: Linseed oil helps restore the shine to a dull surface.
examples.chinese: 亚麻籽油可以有助恢复暗淡表面的光泽。

examples.english: Her elegant dress had a silver sheen.
examples.chinese: 她那件优雅的礼服有一种银色光泽。

examples.english: A light sheen of perspiration covered his upper lip.
examples.chinese: 他的上唇隐隐地有一抹汗光。

examples.english: The gel is guaranteed to add gloss even to the dullest hair.
examples.chinese: 这种发胶能令即使是最没有光泽的头发也亮泽起来。

examples.english: Stephanie did not look well. The gloss had gone from her blond hair and her skin was splotchy looking.
examples.chinese: 斯蒂芬妮看来身体不好，她的金发失去了光泽，肌肤上也出现了色斑。

examples.english: The silverware had been polished to a high gloss.
examples.chinese: 这些银器擦得铮亮。

examples.english: Autumn had given the trees that extra golden lustre.
examples.chinese: 秋天给树木添加了一层金色光泽。

examples.english: The herb can be used as a hair rinse to add luster.
examples.chinese: 这种药草可用作洗发剂，增加头发的光泽。

examples.english: Wax is sprayed on the apples to give them more luster.
examples.chinese: 在苹果上喷蜡可增加其光泽。

centers.english: not shiny

examples.english: Her hair was a dull, darkish brown.
examples.chinese: 她的头发是暗淡的褐色。

examples.english: They chose a red clay pot decorated with patterns in dull white paint.
examples.chinese: 他们选了一个红色的陶罐，上面饰有用没有光泽的白漆描绘的图案。

examples.english: Do you want matt paint or gloss paint?
examples.chinese: 你要亚光漆还是亮光漆？

examples.english: When you drop off the photos, be sure to request matte finish.
examples.chinese: 你送去冲洗照片的时候，一定要他们洗绒面的。

examples.english: He wore a tarnished watch chain across his waistcoat.
examples.chinese: 他的背心上挂着一条已经失去光泽的表链。

examples.english: You shouldn't let the silverware get so tarnished.
examples.chinese: 你不应该让这些银器变得那么没有光泽。

centers.english: bright light

examples.english: From the top of the hill they could see the bright lights of the city below them.
examples.chinese: 从山顶上他们可以看到下面五光十色的城市。

examples.english: After so long indoors the bright sunshine hurt Jack's eyes.
examples.chinese: 杰克躲在室内这么长时间才出来，强烈的阳光刺疼了他的眼睛。

examples.english: There was a flash of bright light beyond the forest and the thunder exploded again.
examples.chinese: 树林那边一道明亮的闪电划过后，又响起一阵雷声。

examples.english: The fire was burning brightly now.
examples.chinese: 火现在燃烧得很旺。

examples.english: She closed her eyes against the brightness of the sun.
examples.chinese: 面对太阳的光芒，她闭起双目。

examples.english: The light from the flashlight wasn't strong enough to read by.
examples.chinese: 用手电筒的光来读书不够亮。

examples.english: The colors had faded after years of being exposed to strong sunlight.
examples.chinese: 被强烈的阳光照射多年后，色彩已褪了。

examples.english: Daylight shone strongly through the cracks in the blinds.
examples.chinese: 猛烈的阳光从百叶窗的缝中照了进来。

examples.english: The windows in the roof gave us a good light to work by.
examples.chinese: 屋顶上的窗户使我们工作时光线充足。

examples.english: The light isn't good here. Go stand by the window.
examples.chinese: 这儿光线不足，站到窗边去。

examples.english: In the harsh light of the street lamps Michelle looked tired and old.
examples.chinese: 在刺目的街灯下，米歇尔看上去又疲倦又苍老。

examples.english: The lighting in these offices is so harsh, it gives me a headache.
examples.chinese: 这些办公室里的灯光太刺眼，让我觉得头疼。

centers.english: extremely bright

examples.english: All of a sudden the stage was flooded with brilliant light.
examples.chinese: 突然间，舞台被照得雪亮。

examples.english: A shaft of brilliant sunlight shone through the dusty attic window.
examples.chinese: 一束灿烂的阳光穿过了满是灰尘的阁楼窗户。

examples.english: Suddenly, I looked up and saw a point of light that was more brilliant than any star I had ever seen.
examples.chinese: 猛然间我往上看去，看到一点亮光。那亮光比我以前看到过的星星都更明亮。

examples.english: The brilliance of the sun on the lake was quite breathtaking.
examples.chinese: 灿烂的阳光照在湖面上，这一美景令人惊叹。

examples.english: There was a blinding flash and then a loud bang.
examples.chinese: 一道眩目的光划过，随后是一声巨响。

examples.english: The sun on the snow is blinding.
examples.chinese: 阳光照在雪上非常刺眼。

examples.english: The blinding glare of our headlights frightened the deer.
examples.chinese: 我们车前灯的眩目亮光使那头鹿受到惊吓。

examples.english: We walked out of the cinema into dazzling sunshine.
examples.chinese: 我们出了影院走到耀眼的阳光下。

examples.english: The sun was so dazzling that it was impossible to even look at its reflection in the water.
examples.chinese: 阳光耀眼得甚至无法睁开眼看它在水面上的反光。

examples.english: The room was so dazzlingly bright that we had to look away.
examples.chinese: 房间亮得刺眼，我们只得移开目光。

examples.english: The blazing lights of the casino shone out across the bay.
examples.chinese: 赌场明亮的灯光在海湾对面闪耀。

examples.english: At twilight, the blazing orange sunset turned into a muted pink.
examples.chinese: 黄昏时，落日那耀眼的金黄色光芒变成了柔和的粉红色。

centers.english: when a place has plenty of light

examples.english: The big windows in this room make it nice and bright.
examples.chinese: 大玻璃窗使这个房间既舒适又明亮。

examples.english: Claire had a lovely bright bedroom which was decorated in yellow and white.
examples.chinese: 克莱尔有一间舒适明亮的卧室，室内粉刷成黄、白两种颜色。

examples.english: We emerged from a dark corridor into a bright, airy courtyard.
examples.chinese: 我们从昏暗的走廊走到明亮而通风的院子里。

examples.english: The colored lights in the distance grew in brightness as I got closer.
examples.chinese: 当我走近时，远处的彩色灯光变得更亮了。

examples.english: The kitchen is light and airy, with a fantastic view.
examples.chinese: 厨房明亮通风，望出去景致迷人。

examples.english: The hallway led to a light and spacious studio.
examples.chinese: 这条走廊通往一间明亮宽敞的工作室。

examples.english: I always try to park in a well-lit area at night.
examples.chinese: 晚上我总是尽量把车停在灯光明亮的地方。

examples.english: To avoid eye problems, make sure that your desk is well-lit.
examples.chinese: 要避免眼睛出现毛病，就要确保书桌上有充足的光线。

centers.english: not bright

examples.english: I couldn't get to sleep until I saw the first pale light of dawn.
examples.chinese: 我一直等看到第一线暗淡的晨光才睡着的。

examples.english: The sunlight through the thick clouds was pale and cool that morning.
examples.chinese: 那天早晨，阳光穿过厚厚的云层，显得既暗淡又清凉。

examples.english: The banks of the river are bathed in pale moonlight.
examples.chinese: 河岸沐浴在淡淡的月光之中。

examples.english: It was impossible to read by the dim light of the fire.
examples.chinese: 靠昏暗的火光是无法阅读的。

examples.english: There was nothing in the room but a table, a chair, and a dim lamp.
examples.chinese: 房间内除了一张桌子、一把椅子和一盏昏暗的灯以外，什么都没有。

examples.english: Dying embers gave out a dim glow in the hearth.
examples.chinese: 壁炉中即将烧尽的余火微微发着光。

examples.english: a dimly-lit corridor
examples.chinese: 一条灯光暗淡的走廊

examples.english: The lights dimmed, and the audience went quiet as the curtain rose.
examples.chinese: 灯光暗了下来，幕升起时观众都安静了。

examples.english: She dimmed the lights to create a more romantic atmosphere.
examples.chinese: 她把灯光调暗以营造出更浪漫的氛围。

examples.english: In the weak light inside the bus Tom couldn't see to read.
examples.chinese: 公共汽车内灯光很暗，使汤姆无法看书。

examples.english: the weak glow of the dashboard lights
examples.chinese: 仪表盘指示灯所发出的微光

examples.english: A candle flickered weakly at the end of the table.
examples.chinese: 一支蜡烛在桌子的一端微微闪烁着。

examples.english: Reading in poor light is very bad for the eyes.
examples.chinese: 在光线不佳的地方看书对眼睛伤害极大。

examples.english: It was difficult to find our way down the mountain in the mist and bad light.
examples.chinese: 由于薄雾笼罩、光线很差，我们难以找到下山的路。

examples.english: In the soft evening light Sonya looked ten years younger.
examples.chinese: 在柔和的夜光下，索尼娅看上去仿佛年轻了十岁。

examples.english: The restaurant has a romantic atmosphere with soft lights and background music.
examples.chinese: 那家餐馆内光线柔和，放着悠扬的背景音乐，有一种浪漫的情调。

examples.english: Coloured lanterns shone softly in the trees and bushes.
examples.chinese: 彩色灯笼在树丛灌木间发出柔和的光。

examples.english: The softness of candlelight added atmosphere to the evening.
examples.chinese: 柔和的烛光为那个晚上增添了气氛。

examples.english: For our anniversary, let's go to a restaurant with low lights and soft music.
examples.chinese: 为庆祝我们的周年纪念日，我们去一家有幽暗灯光和轻柔音乐的餐厅吧。

examples.english: It was a while before Samuel's eyes got used to the low lighting of the intensive care unit.
examples.chinese: 萨缪尔的双眼过了好一会才适应重症监护病房内幽暗的灯光。

centers.english: sharp

examples.english: Be careful. That knife's very sharp.
examples.chinese: 当心，那把刀很锋利。

examples.english: You'll need some sharp scissors.
examples.chinese: 你需要一把锋利的剪刀。

examples.english: Puppies mean to be playful, but their sharp teeth can give you a nasty bite.
examples.chinese: 养小狗是为了好玩，但是小狗牙齿尖利，咬你一口就不得了。

examples.english: His sword was a wicked weapon, three feet in length and razor-sharp.
examples.chinese: 他那把剑绝对是一把好剑，有三英尺长，锋利无比。

examples.english: Over millions of years, some of the reptiles developed razor-sharp teeth.
examples.chinese: 有些爬行动物经过数百万年的进化长出了尖利的牙齿。

examples.english: He picked up a pointed stick and began drawing in the sand.
examples.chinese: 他捡起一根尖尖的棍子，在沙地上画了起来。

examples.english: a plant with long pointed leaves
examples.chinese: 叶子长而尖的一种植物

examples.english: Many ships have been torn apart on the jagged rocks that ring the shoreline.
examples.chinese: 围绕海岸线上的岩石犬牙交错，许多船只都被撞坏了。

examples.english: The window had gone, and the floor was covered with jagged pieces of glass.
examples.chinese: 窗户没有了，地板上全是尖锐的玻璃碎片。

examples.english: He had a leather jacket and short, spiky hair.
examples.chinese: 他穿了一件皮夹克，短短的头发尖尖地竖着。

examples.english: Some corals are quite smooth, others are sharp and spiky.
examples.chinese: 有些珊瑚很光滑，有些则是锋利多刺。

examples.english: a spiky cactus plant
examples.chinese: 带刺的仙人掌

examples.english: Keep prickly plants and bushes away from any paths and seats in the garden.
examples.chinese: 有刺的植物和灌木不要种植在花园里的人行小径或座椅旁边。

examples.english: Sea urchins and starfish feel prickly to the touch.
examples.chinese: 海胆和海星摸上去有刺。

centers.english: to make something sharp

examples.english: Nick sat down at his desk, sharpened his pencil and began to draw.
examples.chinese: 尼克在他桌子旁边坐下来，削好铅笔开始画画。

examples.english: My mother used a special stone to sharpen kitchen knives.
examples.chinese: 我母亲用一块特殊的石头磨菜刀。

centers.english: not sharp

examples.english: I cut myself shaving with a blunt razor.
examples.chinese: 我用一把钝剃刀刮胡子，刮破了脸。

examples.english: It's difficult to achieve a good result if you use blunt tools.
examples.chinese: 如果使用的工具不锋利，就很难达到好的效果。

examples.english: a blunt pencil
examples.chinese: 一支写钝了的铅笔

examples.english: Police say the victim was hit with a blunt instrument, possibly a hammer.
examples.chinese: 警方说被害者是被钝器击中的，有可能是锤子。

centers.english: a colour

examples.english: What's your favourite colour?
examples.chinese: 你最喜欢什么颜色?

examples.english: I like nice bright colours like yellow and pink
examples.chinese: 我喜欢黄、粉红等好看鲜艳的颜色。

examples.english: What colour is his hair?
examples.chinese: 他的头发是什么颜色？

examples.english: Look carefully at the rear door. It's not quite the same colour as the rest of the car.
examples.chinese: 仔细看一下后车门，它与车子其他地方的颜色不太一样。

examples.english: The chameleon is able to change colour to protect itself.
examples.chinese: 变色龙能够变换肤色保护自己。

examples.english: When buying make-up, choose the right shade to match your skin.
examples.chinese: 购买化妆品时；要选择适合自己皮肤的色调。

examples.english: Valerie's eyes are a beautiful shade of blue.
examples.chinese: 瓦莱丽的双眼是好看的蓝色。

examples.english: In the fall, the woods are full of countless shades of brown, yellow and orange.
examples.chinese: 秋天，树林里到处是深浅不一的褐色、黄色和橙色，多得无穷无尽。

examples.english: The sun shining on her head gave her hair an attractive red tint.
examples.chinese: 太阳照在她头上，使她的头发呈现出一种迷人的红色。

examples.english: The glass had a bluish tint to it, like ice.
examples.chinese: 那块玻璃带点儿蓝色，像冰一样。

examples.english: It was October, and the leaves had begun to take on their warm autumn tints.
examples.chinese: 10月了，叶子开始呈现秋季的暖色调。

examples.english: a vivid blue sky
examples.chinese: 碧蓝的天空

examples.english: a cloud of vivid yellow butterflies
examples.chinese: 一大群鲜黄色的蝴蝶

examples.english: The Prince wore majestic robes of a rich purple hue.
examples.chinese: 王子穿着色彩浓重的华贵紫袍。

examples.english: This lipstick is perfect for your coloring.
examples.chinese: 这支口红很配你的肤色。

examples.english: People with very light colouring need to protect themselves from the sun as they burn easily.
examples.chinese: 肤色很浅的人容易被太阳灼伤，所以要保护自己免受太阳直射。

examples.english: Many poisonous animals have distinctive coloring that is easily recognized.
examples.chinese: 许多有毒的动物颜色独特，易于识别。

centers.english: to be a particular colour

examples.english: The Japanese flag is white with a red circle in the middle.
examples.chinese: 日本的国旗为白底红日图案。

examples.english: Frogs are green, toads are brown. That's how you tell the difference.
examples.chinese: 青蛙是绿色的，蟾蜍是棕色的。你可以借此来区分。

examples.english: ‘What colour is your car?’ ‘It's black.’
examples.chinese: “你的车子是什么颜色的？”“黑色。”

examples.english: The front door was painted blue and had a coloured glass panel in it.
examples.chinese: 前门被漆成蓝色，上面镶着一块彩色玻璃。

examples.english: You can make Christmas decorations from almost anything -- beads, colored ribbons, sea shells -- anything.
examples.chinese: 你几乎可以用任何东西来做圣诞装饰品—珠子、彩带、海贝壳—什么都行。

examples.english: The cages were full of brightly- coloured tropical birds.
examples.chinese: 那些笼子里有很多颜色鲜艳的热带鸟。

examples.english: The limousine had tinted windows, so we couldn't see who was inside.
examples.chinese: 那辆豪华大轿车装有浅色玻璃，因此我们无法看见谁在里面。

examples.english: She wore a pair of pink-tinted glasses.
examples.chinese: 她戴着一副粉红色的眼镜。

centers.english: brightly coloured

examples.english: The artist clearly loved bright colours.
examples.chinese: 那位画家显然喜欢鲜艳的颜色。

examples.english: If you are cycling at night, always wear something bright.
examples.chinese: 晚间骑车务必穿颜色鲜艳的衣服。

examples.english: The front door was painted bright red.
examples.chinese: 前门漆成了鲜红色。

examples.english: a bright yellow van
examples.chinese: 一辆鲜黄色的小货车

examples.english: a brightly painted boat
examples.chinese: 一条色彩鲜艳的小船

examples.english: brightly coloured balloons
examples.chinese: 色彩鲜艳的气球

examples.english: The garden was full of colourful and fascinating flowers.
examples.chinese: 花园里盛开着许多色彩鲜艳的美丽花朵。

examples.english: That's a really colourful tie you're wearing.
examples.chinese: 你戴的那条领带真是色彩鲜艳。

examples.english: Stunning tropical fish swim in and out of the colorful rock formations.
examples.chinese: 漂亮的热带鱼在色彩绚丽的岩石中间游进游出。

examples.english: a brilliant blue sky
examples.chinese: 碧蓝的天空

examples.english: The room was painted a brilliant white.
examples.chinese: 房间被粉刷成了刺眼的白色。

examples.english: A giant multicoloured flag waved in the midday sun.
examples.chinese: 在中午的阳光下，一面巨大的彩旗迎风招展。

examples.english: A group of women sat around talking, their multi-coloured dresses radiant.
examples.chinese: 一群女士坐着聊天，她们穿的衣服色彩缤纷，令人眼花缭乱。

examples.english: I didn't like the decorations - they looked rather gaudy.
examples.chinese: 我不喜欢那种装饰—看上去相当俗艳。

examples.english: She smelled of cheap perfume and wore gaudy clothing and fake costume jewellery.
examples.chinese: 她身上有廉价香水的味道，穿着俗艳的衣服，戴着仿真首饰。

examples.english: Her hair had been dyed a garish shade of red.
examples.chinese: 她的头发染成了一种过于鲜艳的红色。

examples.english: The streets are lined with garish neon signs.
examples.chinese: 街道两旁全是炫目的霓虹标志。

examples.english: The traditional designs have been replaced by much more garish colours.
examples.chinese: 传统的图案已经被花哨的颜色所替代。

examples.english: the vibrant reds and oranges of the leaves in autumn
examples.chinese: 秋季树叶明亮的红色和橘色

examples.english: The streets of the capital are vibrant with color.
examples.chinese: 首都的街道色彩鲜明。

examples.english: The carpets were a lurid shade of green.
examples.chinese: 地毯是俗艳的绿色。

centers.english: light colours

examples.english: They both have brown hair, but Tina's is slightly lighter.
examples.chinese: 他们俩都是棕色头发，但是蒂娜发色稍淡些。

examples.english: This is a nice jacket and we also do it in a light green.
examples.chinese: 这是件漂亮的夹克。这种式样的我们还有淡绿色的。

examples.english: There were dark rings under her eyes and her skin was paler than usual.
examples.chinese: 她有黑眼袋，肤色比平时苍白。

examples.english: Her dress is pale pink, with a small flowery pattern.
examples.chinese: 她的裙子是淡粉红色的，有小花卉图案。

examples.english: The old man's pale blue eyes moved from the dartboard to the bar and then back again.
examples.chinese: 那老人浅蓝色的眼睛在圆靶和吧台之间来回扫视着。

examples.english: Someone with fair skin like you should probably use a stronger sunscreen.
examples.chinese: 皮肤像你那样白的人应该用更强效的防晒霜。

examples.english: The Indians were at first frightened of the fair-skinned Europeans.
examples.chinese: 起初，印第安人对白皮肤的欧洲人感到害怕。

examples.english: Before the baby was born Jenny bought some pretty, pastel baby clothes.
examples.chinese: 孩子出生以前，珍妮买了一些色彩柔和的漂亮婴儿服。

examples.english: Mrs Singh preferred saris in pastel colours, such as salmon pink.
examples.chinese: 辛格太太偏爱淡色的莎丽，比如浅橙色。

examples.english: White, cream, and pastels suit me better than dark colours.
examples.chinese: 白色、米色等柔和的色彩比深色更适合我。

examples.english: The woman wore a faded blue dress and old brown sandals.
examples.chinese: 那女人穿着一件褪了色的蓝裙子和一双棕色的旧凉鞋。

examples.english: Joe dropped a faded newspaper picture on Woodward's desk.
examples.chinese: 乔把一张褪了色的报纸图片扔在伍德沃德的桌子上。

centers.english: dark colours

examples.english: She has beautiful dark brown eyes.
examples.chinese: 她长着漂亮的深褐色眼睛。

examples.english: If you're going to have such dark walls I really think you should have a pale carpet.
examples.chinese: 如果你想把墙壁刷成这样的深色，我真的认为你的地毯应该铺浅色的。

examples.english: ‘What do you think of this blouse?’ ‘It's a bit dark -- navy doesn't really suit you.’
examples.chinese: “你觉得这件衬衫怎么样？”“颜色稍深了一点儿—藏青色并不太适合你。”

examples.english: I'm looking for a deeper shade of purple to paint the bedroom.
examples.chinese: 我在找一种更深的紫色来粉刷卧室。

examples.english: She looked into his eyes. They were deep blue.
examples.chinese: 她凝视着他的双眼，那是一双深蓝色的眼睛。

examples.english: In the lounge hung long curtains of luxurious deep red velvet.
examples.chinese: 休息室里挂着豪华的深红色丝绒长窗帘。

examples.english: I admired the warm, rich colors of her Persian rugs.
examples.chinese: 我喜欢她的波斯小地毯那种富丽的暖色。

examples.english: The horse had a rich chestnut coat.
examples.chinese: 那匹马长着深栗色的皮毛。

centers.english: having no colour

examples.english: Carbon monoxide is a colourless, odourless gas.
examples.chinese: 一氧化碳是一种无色无味的气体。

examples.english: He stared out the window at the colorless sky.
examples.chinese: 他凝视着窗外苍白的天空。

centers.english: photograph/film/television

examples.english: You need a colour television to fully appreciate nature programmes.
examples.chinese: 你需要一台彩色电视来尽情观赏描述大自然的节目。

examples.english: Does your new computer have a colour monitor?
examples.chinese: 你的新电脑配有彩色显示器吗?

examples.english: The outstanding feature of this book is its 45 full-page color photographs.
examples.chinese: 这本书的出众之处在于其45页全页彩照。

examples.english: All the pictures are in color.
examples.chinese: 所有图片都是彩色的。

examples.english: Black and white TV sets are now very cheap to buy.
examples.chinese: 现在购买黑白电视机非常便宜。

examples.english: I love the old black-and-white movies.
examples.chinese: 我喜欢古老的黑白影片。

examples.english: an exhibition of black and white photos
examples.chinese: 黑白照片展览！

examples.english: ‘Is the film ‘Casablanca’ in colour?’ ‘No, it's in black and white.’
examples.chinese: “《卡萨布兰卡》这部影片是彩色的吗？”“不，是黑白片。”

examples.english: There were some beautiful monochrome pictures of Yosemite.
examples.chinese: 有一些约塞米蒂国家公园的黑白照片，很漂亮。

examples.english: McCullin still prefers to shoot in monochrome.
examples.chinese: 麦库林还是喜欢拍黑白照片。

centers.english: to change the colour of something

examples.english: Last time I tried to colour my hair it turned red!
examples.chinese: 上次我想染头发，结果弄成红色的了！

examples.english: Marigold petals were once used for colouring butter and cheese.
examples.chinese: 万寿菊的花瓣曾被用来给黄油和乳酪染色。

examples.english: Josie sat at the table coloring in her picture while her father made the dinner.
examples.chinese: 乔茜坐在桌子旁给画涂色，她爸爸则做晚饭。

examples.english: I'm bored with this skirt. I'm going to dye it.
examples.chinese: 我穿腻了这条裙子。我想给它染色。

examples.english: Wool and silk are the materials that are easiest to dye.
examples.chinese: 羊毛与丝绸是最易染色的料子。
