examples.english: Opportunities for men and women, though especially the latter, are limited to employment in agriculture or domestic service.
examples.chinese: 男女的就业机会，尤其是后者，局限于农业或家务方面。

examples.english: There were two candidates for the presidency, Lyndon B Johnson and Senator Barry Goldwater. The latter was known to hold extreme right-wing views.
examples.chinese: 总统候选人有两位，林登·B.约翰逊和巴里·戈德华特参议员。后者因持有极右观点而出名。

examples.english: The classes consist of immigrants, and students who will return to their home countries at the end of the course. The latter group are preparing for the Cambridge Proficiency Certificate.
examples.chinese: 这些班级由移民学生和学成后要回国的学生组成。后者正在准备剑桥英语能力证书考试。

centers.english: to be the last one in a line, group, race etc
centers.chinese: 在队伍里、小组中、比赛中为最后一名

examples.english: There's no point in trying, because I know I'm going to be last.
examples.chinese: 没有必要试，因为我知道我会是最后一名。

examples.english: We were last in a very long queue, so I knew we'd have a long wait.
examples.chinese: 队伍很长，我们排在最后，所以我知道我们要等很长时间。

examples.english: Sally was last to arrive.
examples.chinese: 萨莉最后一个到达。

examples.english: His plane was always the first to take off, and the last to land.
examples.chinese: 他的飞机总是第一个起飞，最后一个降落。

examples.english: I was the youngest in a family of four, so I was always last in line for clothes and toys.
examples.chinese: 我是家里四个孩子中最小的，所以衣服和玩具我总是排到最后一个。

examples.english: Let's just say I don't want to be last in line for promotion.
examples.chinese: 我就这样说吧，我可不想排在最后才得到提升。

expressions.text: come in last/finish last/ come last

examples.english: Our school always comes in last in the regional competition.
examples.chinese: 在地区性比赛中，我们学校总是得最后一名。

examples.english: Bakatin came last with just 3.4% of the vote.
examples.chinese: 巴克丁排在最后，只有3.4%的得票率。

examples.english: Despite finishing last of six, he was only beaten by two lengths.
examples.chinese: 尽管六匹马中它跑了最后一名，但是它只是差两个马位而落败。

examples.english: We all followed our guide up the path, Marcus and I bringing up the rear.
examples.chinese: 我们大家都跟着导游沿小路前行，马库斯和我殿后。

examples.english: The funeral hearse was followed by cars full of friends, and a company of Life Guards brought up the rear.
examples.chinese: 灵车后面跟随着一辆辆坐满亲朋好友的小汽车，一个近卫骑兵团殿后。

examples.english: A few stragglers got lost in the fog.
examples.chinese: 几个掉队的人在大雾里迷失了方向。

examples.english: After three and a half hours, the stragglers were still coming through.
examples.chinese: 三个半小时以后，还有一些落在后面的人陆续到达。

centers.english: the one just before the last one

examples.english: I've nearly finished this test. I'm on the next to last question.
examples.chinese: 我快做完这份试卷了。我在答倒数第二题。

examples.english: ‘How much have you read?’ ‘I'm on the second to last chapter.’
examples.chinese: “你看了多少了？”“我在看倒数第二章。”

examples.english: It wasn't until the next to last day of the vacation that she dared to go to the beach.
examples.chinese: 直到假期结束前一天她才敢去海边。

examples.english: She had several husbands. Garry Burton, the last but one, was a real alcoholic.
examples.chinese: 她有过好几位丈夫。加里·伯顿是倒数第二任，他是个十足的酒鬼。

examples.english: He and his family escaped on the last but one ship to leave Hamburg.
examples.chinese: 他和家人乘坐倒数第二班轮船逃离汉堡。

examples.english: There's a long row of houses and I live in the last but one.
examples.chinese: 那里有一长排的房子，我住在倒数第二幢。

examples.english: We booked a moped for the penultimate day of the holiday.
examples.chinese: 我们订了一辆摩托自行车在假期的倒数第二天使用。

examples.english: Particularly impressive is the horn section on the penultimate track, ‘Just Jivin’ Around'.
examples.chinese: 尤其好听的是倒数第二首“来跳摇摆舞吧”的小号乐组。

examples.english: These trials are actually the penultimate step toward choosing the Olympic team for this summer's games.
examples.chinese: 这几场预赛实际上是为今年夏季奥运会选拔参赛球队所做的倒数第二步。

examples.english: Eric scored a timely win in the penultimate round of the Formula 1 championship.
examples.chinese: 埃里克在一级方程式大奖赛的倒数第二圈中及时追上并胜出。

examples.english: His horse slipped and fell at the penultimate fence.
examples.chinese: 他的马在倒数第二道障碍物前失足滑倒。

centers.english: what you say to introduce the last thing in a speech etc
centers.chinese: 演讲等中要说最后一件事时说的话

examples.english: Finally, I'd like to thank all those people who helped make the conference such a success.
examples.chinese: 最后，我想对所有帮助这次会议成功召开的人表示感谢。

examples.english: And finally, don't miss Albuquerque's amazing punk band, playing tonight at the Lunar Club.
examples.chinese: 最后，请不要错过阿尔布开克基了不起的朋克乐队，今晚他们将在月球俱乐部演出。

examples.english: Now lastly, before you all leave, I want to mention some tips for the interview stage.
examples.chinese: 最后，在大家离开之前，我想就面试这个阶段提一些小建议。

examples.english: And lastly, Delia, do you see the movement towards more and more computerization continuing in the future?
examples.chinese: 最后，迪莉娅，你有没有发现今后会越来越电脑化的这种倾向?

examples.english: Firstly it's too big, secondly we can't afford it, and lastly we don't really need it.
examples.chinese: 首先，这东西太大了；第二，我们买不起；最后，我们实在不需要。

examples.english: Last but not least, let me introduce Jane, our new accountant.
examples.chinese: 最后，但也同样重要的是，我要介绍一下我们新来的会计简。

examples.english: And last but not least, I thank Begona Canup for her interest in the book.
examples.chinese: 最后，但也一样重要的是，我要感谢贝贡娜·坎纳普对这本书的关心。

expressions.text: last but certainly not/by no means least

examples.english: Social Security has reduced poverty, and last, but by no means least, it has been a good deal for participants.
examples.chinese: 社会保障制度减少了贫困，最后同样重要的一点是，对于参加者来说它是合算的。

centers.english: to continue for a particular period of time

examples.english: Mexico achieved a remarkable 8% annual growth rate, but the new prosperity did not last.
examples.chinese: 墨西哥实现了令人瞩目的8%年增长率，但是新的繁荣并不持久。

examples.english: Ours was a happy marriage, but I always feared it wouldn't last.
examples.chinese: 我们的婚姻是幸福的，但我总是担心不会长久。

expressions.text: last 2 hours/6 months/a long time etc

examples.english: The whole opera lasts about four hours.
examples.chinese: 整部歌剧大约要演四小时。

examples.english: The earthquake lasted 30 seconds and caused 12,000 casualties.
examples.chinese: 地震持续了30秒钟，造成12，000人伤亡。

examples.english: The silence in the room seemed to last a very long time before she spoke.
examples.chinese: 屋子里好像沉默了好长时间她才开口说话。

examples.english: rainstorms lasting all night long
examples.chinese: 持续了一整晚的暴雨

examples.english: Each consultation can last between 10 minutes and half an hour.
examples.chinese: 每场咨询为十分钟到半小时不等。

expressions.text: last for 2 hours/a long time/years etc

examples.english: The performance is expected to last for approximately 3 hours.
examples.chinese: 这场演出估计约三小时左右。

examples.english: The effects of Josie's experience could last for years, doctors say.
examples.chinese: 乔茜的经历要影响她多年，医生是这么说的。

examples.english: The talks lasted until the early hours of the morning.
examples.chinese: 会谈一直持续到凌晨。

examples.english: The drug produces a powerful cocaine-type high that lasts for days.
examples.chinese: 这种毒品能产生强烈的类可卡因兴奋状态，可持续好几天时间。

examples.english: The Sung dynasty lasted from 960 to 1278.
examples.chinese: 宋朝始于960年，至1279年结束。

expressions.text: last into the 19th century/1980s/next decade etc

examples.english: This phase of the educational campaign lasted into the 1960s.
examples.chinese: 这一阶段的教育运动持续到了20世纪60年代。

examples.english: Analysts are confident the downturn in share prices will not last long.
examples.chinese: 分析人士很有信心，这次股价下跌不会持久。

examples.english: It's the worst cold I've ever had, but luckily it didn't last very long.
examples.chinese: 那是我最厉害的一次感冒，幸好持续的时间不是很长。

examples.english: I wanted the weekend to last forever.
examples.chinese: 我真希望那个周末永远不会结束。

examples.english: The pilots say the walkout could last as long as two months.
examples.chinese: 飞行员说这次罢工可能要持续两个月之久。

examples.english: the profound and long-lasting consequences of the war
examples.chinese: 战争深远和持久的影响

examples.english: The bridge was closed, so the journey took much longer than usual.
examples.chinese: 桥封闭了，所以路上所花的时间比平时多很多。

examples.english: Can you give me an idea how long this is going to take?
examples.chinese: 你可以大概告诉我一下这需要多长时间吗？

examples.english: It took two years for Eddie to learn how to walk again.
examples.chinese: 埃迪花了两年时间才重新学会走路。

expressions.text: take 2 hours/6 months etc to do

examples.english: The software will take a few minutes to load.
examples.chinese: 载入这个软件需要几分钟时间。

examples.english: They're just fun books, they don't take very long to read.
examples.chinese: 这些只是娱乐性的书，不需要花很长时间就能看完。

expressions.text: take somebody 2 hours/6 months etc to do

examples.english: Doing the painting alone will take him all day.
examples.chinese: 他一个人刷油漆要一整天的时间。

expressions.text: it takes (somebody) 2 hours/six months etc to do something

examples.english: It takes me about half an hour to get ready in the mornings.
examples.chinese: 早上我需要大约半小时做准备。

examples.english: It didn't take Susie long to find out what was going on between us.
examples.chinese: 苏茜不久就发现了我们之间的问题。

examples.english: It took three and a half hours to come back from Swansea.
examples.chinese: 从斯旺西回来路上花了三个半小时。

examples.english: The chairman's speech went on for what seemed like hours.
examples.chinese: 主席的发言像是持续了几个小时。

examples.english: It sounds like the sort of meeting that could go on forever.
examples.chinese: 这听起来好像是那种没完没了的会议。

examples.english: It starts at seven, but I don't know how long it goes on for.
examples.chinese: 这在7点钟开始，但我不知道要进行多长时间。

examples.english: It was a big lawsuit, and it went on for quite a while.
examples.chinese: 这是一宗很大的官司，打了很长时间。

examples.english: The siege dragged on into its second month.
examples.chinese: 这次围困历时很久，已踏进第二个月。

examples.english: I can't afford to let the case drag on for months.
examples.chinese: 我不能让这个案子拖好几个月。

examples.english: The Ideal Homes exhibition will run until 6 p.m. each day over the weekend.
examples.chinese: “理想之家”展览会在周末每天一直开到晚上6点。

examples.english: The play ran for two months on Broadway.
examples.chinese: 这出戏在百老汇连演了两个月。

examples.english: The media campaign features four commercials that will run for eight weeks.
examples.chinese: 这次在媒体上的宣传活动将推出四个商业广告，为期八周。

examples.english: One of the top sit-coms was ‘On the Buses’, which ran for sixty episodes
examples.chinese: 其中一部最佳情景喜剧是《在巴士上》，演了60集。

examples.english: This is one that will, I believe, run and run.
examples.chinese: 我相信这部戏会演了又演。

examples.english: The show moves to London's West End after a month's run in Leicester's Gala Theatre.
examples.chinese: 这个节目在莱斯特大剧院连演一个月之后移师伦敦西区上演。

examples.english: Britain's longest-running chart show, Top of the Pops, looks set to close.
examples.chinese: 英国播出时间最长的排行榜节目《流行音乐榜中榜》看来准备结束了。

examples.english: We had a marathon recording session extending over eighteen hours.
examples.chinese: 我们进行了一次马拉松式的录音，历时18个小时。

examples.english: If the investigation is a sustained piece of work extending over a period of weeks, a diary will help to track events.
examples.chinese: 如果调查是一项长久的工作，要进行好几个星期，那么日记可以帮助把事情记录下来。

centers.english: to stay in good condition for a particular period of time
centers.chinese: 在某一段时间里保持良好的状态

examples.english: Cheap saucepans can't really be expected to last.
examples.chinese: 便宜的平底锅实在不能指望它耐用。

examples.english: It's amazing how long this car has lasted, really.
examples.chinese: 真的很难相信这辆汽车已经用了多久。

examples.english: You can get washable plastic lace by the yard, which lasts much better than the real thing.
examples.chinese: 你可以论码买可用水洗的塑料花边，它比真正的花边耐用许多。

expressions.text: last (for) 7 years/2 months/a long time etc

examples.english: Some wine-makers will tell you that a cask lasts only for four years.
examples.chinese: 有些酿酒商会告诉你一个酒桶只能用四年。

examples.english: When the houses were put up in 1946, they really weren't meant to last more than ten years.
examples.chinese: 1946年建造这些房子的时候，并没有打算要用十年以上。

examples.english: With care, a mahogany dining table will last you a lifetime.
examples.chinese: 红木餐桌如果小心使用可以用上一辈子。

examples.english: Well-made golf clubs ought to last a lifetime, so it's worth paying the extra.
examples.chinese: 好的高尔夫球杆可以用一辈子，所以多花点钱也是值得的。

examples.english: You'd better eat these cakes today -- they won't keep.
examples.chinese: 你最好今天就把这些蛋糕吃了—它们放不久。

examples.english: The yoghurt will keep for about a week in a refrigerator.
examples.chinese: 酸奶可以在冰箱里放一个星期左右。

examples.english: Do you think this bacon will keep till tomorrow?
examples.chinese: 你说这块熏猪肉能放到明天吗？

examples.english: Eat celery as soon as possible -- it does not keep well for long periods.
examples.chinese: 把芹菜尽快吃了一放久了不新鲜。

examples.english: Homemade bread never stays as fresh as the stuff you buy in the supermarket.
examples.chinese: 家里做的面包不可能像超市里买的能保持新鲜。

expressions.text: stay fresh for 2 weeks/2 days/a long time etc

examples.english: Strain the stock and put in a sealed container. It should stay fresh for up to three days.
examples.chinese: 把高汤滤出来放在一个密封的容器里，这应该可以保鲜三天。

examples.english: Bend the stems, as this will make the blooms stay fresh for longer.
examples.chinese: 把茎压弯，因为这样可以使花的保鲜期更长。

centers.english: when a supply of something continues to be enough

examples.english: He knew they only had enough food to last another three days.
examples.chinese: 他知道他们的食物只能再维持三天。

examples.english: $400 won't last you long in Chicago.
examples.chinese: 400美元在芝加哥用不了多长时间。

examples.english: I sent my sister $500, with some advice on how to make it last.
examples.chinese: 我给妹妹寄了500美元，并附了一些建议，告诉她钱怎么花才经用。

expressions.text: make something last longer/a long time etc

examples.english: You can add ice or additional mixer to alcoholic drinks make them last longer.
examples.chinese: 酒精饮料加冰或加别的调酒饮料可以喝得更久一些。

examples.english: One group delayed spending the grant in order to make its funds last as long as possible.
examples.chinese: 有一个组没有立即动用拨款，他们想尽量让经费维持更长的时间。

examples.english: She only had a small amount of food left, and knew she'd have to make it last the week.
examples.chinese: 她只剩下很少的一些食物了，她知道这个星期只能靠这些来维持了。

examples.english: Dealers are mixing the drug with heroin and cocaine to eke out their supplies.
examples.chinese: 毒贩在这种毒品中掺入海洛因和可卡因，这使他们的毒品供应可以持久。

examples.english: She lived on a shoestring during these years, eking out the pennies as best she could.
examples.chinese: 这些年她就靠一点点钱生活，因此花钱尽量精打细算。

examples.english: I'm not ashamed to eke food out.
examples.chinese: 吃省一点，我并不觉得丢脸。

centers.english: continuing or existing for only a limited period of time
centers.chinese: 只持续或存在有限的时间

examples.english: I'm sure this is only a temporary problem.
examples.chinese: 我肯定这只是个暂时的问题。

examples.english: These arrangements are only intended to be temporary until an alternative is found.
examples.chinese: 这些安排只是临时性的，直至找到另一个方案。

examples.english: Temporary shelters were hastily constructed as the refugees started to pour in.
examples.chinese: 难民开始蜂拥而至，临时避难所被匆匆搭建起来。

examples.english: I've got a temporary secretarial job, but I'm hoping to find something more permanent.
examples.chinese: 我找到了一份临时的秘书工作，但我希望能找到更固定的职业。

examples.english: There are always plenty of temporary workers available during the summer months.
examples.chinese: 夏季的几个月里总是有大量的临时工可供选择。

examples.english: Because of damage to their homes, many people had to stay in temporary accommodation for a few months.
examples.chinese: 由于房屋遭损，许多人只好在临时住处内暂住几个月。

examples.english: Quite a lot of our staff are employed on short term contracts.
examples.chinese: 我们的员工中有许多人签的是短期合同。

examples.english: The development programme was established on a short-term basis, just over a year ago.
examples.chinese: 这项发展计划的设立是短期性的，一年前才刚定下来。

examples.english: The Prime Minister emphasized the need to look to the future, rather than only considering the short-term results.
examples.chinese: 首相强调要放眼未来，不能仅仅考虑短期结果。

examples.english: She can still remember things that happened fifty years ago, but her short-term memory is terrible!
examples.chinese: 她仍记得50年前发生的事，但她的短期记忆却糟得很！

examples.english: In the short term, interest rates are likely to remain the same.
examples.chinese: 短期内利率很可能维持不变。

examples.english: The management has made a provisional pay offer of 7%.
examples.chinese: 管理层暂时定出7%的加薪幅度。

examples.english: Following a series of provisional governments, the Dominican people elected Juan Bosch as their President.
examples.chinese: 经过一连串临时政府执政后，多米尼加人民选出了胡安·博什为他们的总统。

examples.english: I've made a booking at the hotel, but it's only provisional - I'll have to confirm it soon.
examples.chinese: 我在旅馆订了房间，但只是暂定—我得马上确认一下。

examples.english: You have to get a provisional licence until you pass your test.
examples.chinese: 在通过考试之前，你要去办一张临时驾驶执照。

examples.english: Two more series of the show are provisionally planned for next year.
examples.chinese: 这个节目暂定于来年多推出两个系列。

examples.english: Eventually an interim agreement on arms control was reached.
examples.chinese: 最终达成了一项临时军备控制协议。

examples.english: The actual sales figures were 20% higher than those estimated in the interim report in July.
examples.chinese: 实际的销售数字比在7月份的中期报告中估计的要高出20%。

examples.english: The government will announce interim measures to tackle fuel shortages.
examples.chinese: 政府将宣布推出临时措施以解决燃料紧缺问题。

examples.english: The first house we bought was only a stopgap, until we could afford something we really liked.
examples.chinese: 我们买的第一套房子只是暂时的，直到我们能够买得起真正喜欢的房子。

examples.english: There is a trend towards favoring stopgap measures, instead of seeking long-term solutions to socioeconomic problems.
examples.chinese: 现时的趋势是喜欢推行临时措施，而非寻找能解决社会经济问题的长远方法。

centers.english: for a limited time only

examples.english: The library is temporarily closed for repairs.
examples.chinese: 图书馆因维修暂时关闭。

examples.english: Well, I suppose she can stay here temporarily, while she's looking for an apartment.
examples.chinese: 嗯，我想她在找房子时可以暂时住在这里。

examples.english: For the moment, I'm quite happy in the job I'm doing.
examples.chinese: 目前我对自己的工作很满意。

examples.english: Such popular programmes will go on being broadcast on Channel 3 - for now.
examples.chinese: 这么受欢迎的节目暂时将继续在第三频道播出。

examples.english: ‘The negotiations are continuing for the moment,’ a spokesman said.
examples.chinese: “谈判目前还在继续。”一位发言人说。

examples.english: For the time being, Mrs Gilman's classes will be taken by other teachers.
examples.chinese: 吉尔曼太太教的班级暂时由其他老师接手。

examples.english: Although the government aims to encourage private enterprise, around one third of the economy will remain under state control, for the time being.
examples.chinese: 尽管政府致力鼓励私营企业，暂时仍约有三分之一的经济由国家控制。

centers.english: to say what will happen in the future

examples.english: Most of the papers are predicting an easy victory for the Dallas Cowboys.
examples.chinese: 多数报纸预言达拉斯牛仔队会轻易获胜。

examples.english: a major earthquake that no-one had predicted
examples.chinese: 一场没有预测到的大地震

examples.english: Some scientists predict that the Earth's temperature will rise by as much as 5° over the next 20 years.
examples.chinese: 一些科学家预言，今后20年里地球温度会上升5度之多。

examples.english: Property analysts forecast a fall in house prices.
examples.chinese: 房地产分析人士预测房价会下跌。

examples.english: Rain is forecast for all parts of southern England tomorrow.
examples.chinese: 预报说，明天英格兰南部全境有雨。

examples.english: Hardly anyone had forecast that the drought would last so long.
examples.chinese: 几乎没人预测到干旱会持续那么长时间。

examples.english: It is claimed that Ebba prophesied her own death from the plague.
examples.chinese: 据称埃巴预言自己会死于鼠疫。

examples.english: Jesus prophesied that one of his disciples would betray him.
examples.chinese: 耶稣预言，他的门徒中有一人将出卖他。

examples.english: Her ability to prophesy about the future made many people think she was a witch.
examples.chinese: 她预知未来的能力使得许多人认为她是个巫婆。

examples.english: Nostradamus is said to have foretold the rise of Hitler.
examples.chinese: 据说诺查丹玛斯曾预言希特勒会崛起。

examples.english: Everything happened as Merlin foretold.
examples.chinese: 一切正像默林预言的那样。

examples.english: I just couldn't get the ball past him; he second-guessed me every time.
examples.chinese: 我无法把球绕过他；每次他都猜出我的意图。

examples.english: Second-guessing the bank's next move in the takeover bid proved very difficult.
examples.chinese: 预测该银行在此次收购出价中下一步会怎么做非常困难。

centers.english: to think you know what is going to happen in the future
centers.chinese: 觉得自己预先知道将要发生什么事

examples.english: No one foresaw the Great Depression of the thirties.
examples.chinese: 没有人预见到30年代的经济大萧条。

examples.english: Businesses are alarmed at the costs they foresee in complying with the new rules.
examples.chinese: 企业预见到遵循新规定将要付出的代价，感到很忧虑。

examples.english: Ten years ago she could not have foreseen that her marriage would end in divorce.
examples.chinese: 十年前，她无法预料到自己的婚姻会以离婚告终。

examples.english: I cannot envisage what the circumstances will be in twenty years’ time.
examples.chinese: 我无法想象二十年后的情形会是什么样。

examples.english: Most of those who voted for independence did not envision war as the eventual outcome.
examples.chinese: 大多数投票选择独立的人没料到最后的结局竟会是战争。

examples.english: We do not envisage a general election for at least another two years.
examples.chinese: 我们预计，至少今后两年内不会举行大选。

examples.english: Jason saw the stock market crash coming and sold most of his shares.
examples.chinese: 贾森预计股市会暴跌，所以把名下大多数的股票都卖了。

examples.english: Then one day she just walked out -- I suppose I should have seen it coming really.
examples.chinese: 后来有一天她出走了—我想我本应该能够估计到。

examples.english: The trip's going to be a disaster - I can feel it in my bones.
examples.chinese: 这次旅行将是一场灾难。我凭直觉预感到这一点。

examples.english: When Paola failed to phone, John had a horrible premonition that she was in danger.
examples.chinese: 葆拉没有打电话来，于是约翰有一种可怕的预感：她有危险。

examples.english: She shivered suddenly, and I wondered whether she had had a premonition of her own death.
examples.chinese: 她突然颤抖起来，我怀疑她是不是预感到死亡来临。

examples.english: If I could only see into the future and know how this would all end.
examples.chinese: 要是我能未卜先知，知道这一切如何了结就好了。

examples.english: Nobody can see into the future, and all stock exchange investment is a gamble.
examples.chinese: 没有人能未卜先知，证券投资都是赌博。

centers.english: something that someone predicts will happen
centers.chinese: 某人预言将会发生的某事

examples.english: Despite their confident predictions, sales of the new car have not been very good.
examples.chinese: 尽管他们的预言充满信心，但是新车销售并不十分理想。

examples.english: It's too early to make any predictions about the election results.
examples.chinese: 预言选举结果还为时过早。

examples.english: According to the weather forecast, it's going to stay hot for the rest of the week.
examples.chinese: 天气预报说本周以后几天持续高温。

examples.english: It is impossible to give an accurate forecast of company sales 10 years from now.
examples.chinese: 精确预测公司十年后的营业额是不可能的。

examples.english: The old woman's prophecies of disaster were soon fulfilled.
examples.chinese: 老妇人关于灾难的预言很快就变成了现实。

examples.english: Lij Yasu was never crowned, possibly because he believed a prophecy that if he became king he would die.
examples.chinese: 伊亚苏五世从没登上王位，可能是因为他相信自己当上国王后就会死去的预言吧。

examples.english: Amazingly, the manager's prophecy that the team would get into the first division seems to be coming true.
examples.chinese: 令人惊讶的是，教练关于球队将进入甲级的预言似乎要变成现实了。

examples.english: Well, doctor, what's the prognosis?
examples.chinese: 那么，医生，预后情况怎么样？

examples.english: There were several arrests for disorderly behaviour, but for the most part the much-predicted violence did not materialize.
examples.chinese: 一些人因破坏治安遭逮捕，但很多人预料发生的暴力事件基本上没有发生。

examples.english: Next year's projected sales are 5% higher than this year's.
examples.chinese: 明年的预计销售额要比今年的高出5%。

examples.english: The company's losses look likely to wipe out the projected profits on the ECR90 project.
examples.chinese: 该公司的损失可能会抵消掉ECR90项目的预期利润。

centers.english: able to be predicted

examples.english: The drug is usually effective but unfortunately the side effects are not always predictable.
examples.chinese: 那种药物通常是有效的，但不幸的是其副作用不一定能够预测出来。

examples.english: There are few predictable elements to this conflict -- the only certainty is that the situation will worsen before it gets better.
examples.chinese: 这次冲突的因素大多难以预料——唯一可以肯定的是，情势变好之前必先恶化。

examples.english: In the current economic climate it is fairly predictable that unemployment will continue to rise.
examples.chinese: 在当前的经济环境里，可以肯定的是，失业率将会持续上升。

examples.english: Due to rising costs and delays in the delivery of equipment, losses are already foreseeable on the new tunnel project.
examples.chinese: 由于成本上升、设备运送延误，人们已经预见到新隧道工程必将出现亏损。

examples.english: There will not be any redundancies in the foreseeable future.
examples.chinese: 在可以预见的未来里不会裁员。

examples.english: Your insurance policy should take into account all foreseeable circumstances.
examples.chinese: 你们的保险单应该把所有可以预见的情况考虑在内。

centers.english: not able to be predicted

examples.english: The doctors can't say whether he will recover at this stage.
examples.chinese: 医生在目前阶段不知道他能否康复。

examples.english: I don't know if we'll stay together or not. I can't tell how I'll be feeling in a month's time.
examples.chinese: 我不知道我们能否在一起生活。我说不准一个月后自己的感受会是什么样。

examples.english: Britain is well known for its unpredictable weather.
examples.chinese: 英国的天气变幻莫测是广为人知的。

examples.english: Mayoral elections are usually unpredictable, highly dependent on the particular appeal of personalities and the ability to form coalitions.
examples.chinese: 一般来说，市长选举的结果难以预测，很大程度上取决于独特的性格魅力和建立联盟的能力。

examples.english: The situation in the region's poorest country remains volatile and highly unpredictable.
examples.chinese: 该地区最贫困的国家的形势依然多变，很难预测。

examples.english: What happened the following weekend was as unforeseeable as a plane falling on your house.
examples.chinese: 下个周末发生的事就像飞机掉到你家那样无法预料。

examples.english: The circumstances which combined to cause this accident were unforeseeable.
examples.chinese: 导致这起事故的几种情况交织在一起，不可能预料得到。

examples.english: ‘What is your next film going to be about?’ ‘Well, that remains to be seen.’
examples.chinese: “你下一部片子是有关什么内容的？”“哦，还不知道呐。”

examples.english: What remains to be seen now is whether it is too late to save the rainforests.
examples.chinese: 现在未知的问题是抢救雨林是否已经太迟。

expressions.text: it remains to be seen how/when/if etc

examples.english: It remains to be seen how many senior citizens will actually benefit from this new plan.
examples.chinese: 到底有多少老年人实际上会从此新方案中受益还不知道。

centers.english: someone with special powers to predict

examples.english: I went to see a fortune teller, and she told me that I would meet the man of my dreams and have three children.
examples.chinese: 我去见了一位算命的，她告诉我说我会遇见梦寐以求的男子，并生下三个孩子。

examples.english: A clairvoyant predicted that something terrible would happen to the President.
examples.chinese: 一位据说未卜先知的人预言总统会遭遇可怕的事。

examples.english: Local legends told of a family in which the women were all cursed with clairvoyance.
examples.chinese: 当地传说中有一家人，其中的女性成员都能预知未来，深受其苦。

examples.english: How was I supposed to know she'd react like that? I'm not psychic!
examples.chinese: 我怎么知道她会有那样的反应？我又不能未卜先知！

examples.english: How did you know I'd be here? You must be psychic!
examples.chinese: 你怎么知道我会在这里？你肯定是个先知！

centers.english: to start doing something

examples.english: We can't start until Carol gets here.
examples.chinese: 等卡罗尔到了我们才可以开始。

examples.english: I'm starting a new job next week.
examples.chinese: 下个星期我要开始一份新的工作。

examples.english: Have you started that book yet? started reading it
examples.chinese: 你开始看那本书了吗？

examples.english: I've just started learning German.
examples.chinese: 我刚刚开始学德语。

examples.english: It was getting dark so we started looking for a place to stay the night.
examples.chinese: 天慢慢黑下来，我们开始找晚上住的地方。

examples.english: Halfway through the performance, she started to feel a little faint.
examples.chinese: 演出进行了一半，她开始感到有点头晕。

examples.english: Outside, it was starting to rain.
examples.chinese: 外面开始下雨了。

examples.english: Once the children were quiet, the teacher began.
examples.chinese: 孩子们一静下来，老师就开始上课了。

examples.english: They began their holiday in Italy, and then went on to Greece.
examples.chinese: 他们的假期从意大利开始，后来又去了希腊。

examples.english: More and more people are beginning to do their shopping on-line.
examples.chinese: 越来越多的人开始网上购物。

examples.english: ‘What do you mean?’ she said, beginning to laugh.
examples.chinese: “你什么意思呀？”她说着笑了起来。

examples.english: The audience suddenly began shouting and cheering.
examples.chinese: 观众顿时大叫着欢呼起来。

examples.english: Come on Sam - it's time you got down to some homework.
examples.chinese: 来吧，山姆—你该开始做家庭作业了。

expressions.text: get down to to doing something

examples.english: When the summer comes, we must get down to painting the outside of the house.
examples.chinese: 等到夏天来临，我们一定要把房子的外墙刷一刷。

examples.english: OK, can everyone take a seat now, and we'll get down to business.
examples.chinese: 好了，请大家都坐好，我们要开始了。

examples.english: The exam was almost over and I hadn't even started on question 3.
examples.chinese: 考试都快结束了，我还没开始做第三道题。

examples.english: It was time to start on all those little jobs around the house that he'd been putting off.
examples.chinese: 应该动手去做家里这些零零碎碎的活儿了，他一直拖着不干。

examples.english: We can't start on the building work until planning permission comes through.
examples.chinese: 要取得建筑许可证我们才可以开工。

examples.english: Philip set about the task with a great deal of energy and enthusiasm.
examples.chinese: 菲利普以无穷的精力和热情开始了这项任务。

examples.english: Why don't we set to work really early, and try to get it finished in one day?
examples.chinese: 我们为什么不一早就开始，在一天之内把它干完呢?

expressions.text: set about/set to work doing something

examples.english: If there is a serious problem, it's far better to admit it and set about tackling it.
examples.chinese: 如果出现严重的问题，最好是承认问题的存在，并着手去解决它。

expressions.text: set to work to do something

examples.english: Workmen had already set to work to clear the fallen trees.
examples.chinese: 工人们已经动手清理倒下的树木。

examples.english: Once in power, the government set to work on major reforms to the tax system.
examples.chinese: 政府一上台就开始进行重大的税制改革。

examples.english: Martin marched into the shop and proceeded to hurl abuse at the girl behind the counter.
examples.chinese: 马丁大步走进店里，对柜台后面的那个女孩破口大骂起来。

examples.english: After listening carefully to my advice, she proceeded to do the exact opposite!
examples.chinese: 她仔细听了我的劝告之后，开始做起完全相反的事情来！

examples.english: After lunch the children settled down to finish their science projects.
examples.chinese: 午饭后，孩子们定下心来把科学专题研究做完。

expressions.text: settle down to to doing something

examples.english: It was two o'clock before I could finally settle down to writing the next chapter.
examples.chinese: 等我最后坐下来写下一章的时候已是两点钟了。

examples.english: Annie arrived late and immediately launched into a lengthy description of the terrible traffic on the roads.
examples.chinese: 安妮迟到了，一进来就啰啰唆唆地讲路上交通如何如何糟糕。

examples.english: At that point the young man stood up and launched into a passionate party-political speech.
examples.chinese: 就在那个关头，这位年轻人站起来发表了一篇慷慨激昂的党政演说。

examples.english: In the 1950s China embarked on a massive program of industrialization and mechanization.
examples.chinese: 20世纪50年代，中国开始实施一项宏大的工业化和机械化的计划。

examples.english: After leaving his office job, he embarked upon a highly successful writing career.
examples.chinese: 他辞去办公室工作以后，开始了他的写作生涯，非常成功。

examples.english: Rowling's latest book is full of all the magic and excitement that her young readers have come to expect.
examples.chinese: 罗琳的最新小说里充满了她的年轻读者所期待的魔幻与激情。

examples.english: At first I thought he was a bit strange, but I grew to like and respect him over the years.
examples.chinese: 起初我觉得他有点怪，但是多年下来，我逐渐喜欢他，而且尊敬他了。

examples.english: My teacher said that this wasn't the kind of work that she'd come to expect of me.
examples.chinese: 老师说她没想到我能写出这样的文章。

examples.english: Come on! - get cracking. I want this whole house clean by the time I get back.
examples.chinese: 快点！赶紧动手，我要你们在我回来的时候把屋子全部打扫干净。

examples.english: You'd better get cracking if you want to get to the airport by ten.
examples.chinese: 如果你想在10点之前赶到机场，就得赶快了。

centers.english: to start an activity, job, speech etc by doing the first part of it
centers.chinese: 以做第一部分开始某项活动、工作、讲话等

examples.english: There were two interviewers and they began by asking me questions about my last job.
examples.chinese: 给我面试的有两个人，他们一开始就问我有关上一份工作的事情。

examples.english: When you're drawing a face, you should start by trying to imagine the bones underneath.
examples.chinese: 画脸的时候，应该首先想象一下脸部的骨架。

expressions.gram: transitive phrasal verb not in passive

examples.english: If I were you, I'd start with the easy questions.
examples.chinese: 要是我的话，就从简单的问题开始做。

examples.english: The lecturer began with a short account of the history of the UN.
examples.chinese: 讲师先对联合国的历史进行了简短的介绍。

examples.english: Decorating the place was a huge job, and we started with the kitchen and the hall.
examples.chinese: 装修工程浩大，我们决定从厨房和门厅开始干起。

expressions.gram: intransitive/transitive phrasal verb not in passive

examples.english: Is there anyone here who would like to lead off the debate?
examples.chinese: 在座有哪位愿意带头开始讨论?

examples.english: The Chief Executive led off by pointing out that a merger was only one option.
examples.chinese: 总经理首先指出，合并只是其中的一个选择方案。

examples.english: We asked the sales director to lead off with the latest sales figures.
examples.chinese: 我们让销售部经理先发言，报告一下最近的销售数字。

expressions.gram: transitive phrasal verb not in passive

examples.english: Let's kick off with an Indian meal somewhere, and go on to a club after that.
examples.chinese: 我们先到哪里去吃顿印度菜，然后再去找一家夜总会吧。

examples.english: Phelps kicked off an outstanding night's music with a beautifully played Mozart Symphony.
examples.chinese: 费尔普斯以一曲演奏得很动听的莫扎特交响乐开始了一个精彩的音乐之夜。

centers.english: to be the person who starts something

examples.english: I'd always been attracted to her, but I was too shy to make the first move.
examples.chinese: 我一直喜欢她，可是我胆子太小，不敢采取主动。

expressions.text: make the first move to do something

examples.english: The employees made the first move to end the strike.
examples.chinese: 雇员采取主动，结束了罢工。

examples.english: Everyone was standing around in silence, so I took the initiative and tried to explain why we had come.
examples.chinese: 大家都站着默不作声，于是我主动站出来解释我们为什么要到这里来。

examples.english: The disarmament talks failed because neither side was prepared to risk taking the initiative.
examples.chinese: 裁军谈判告吹，因为双方都不愿意冒险采取主动。

centers.english: to start a journey

examples.english: What time do you have to set off in the morning?
examples.chinese: 你明天早上得什么时候动身？

examples.english: We had meant to set out before lunch but nobody was ready to leave.
examples.chinese: 我们本来打算午饭前动身，可是大家都没准备好。

expressions.text: set off/set out towards/along/in the direction of etc

examples.english: Packing herself a couple of sandwiches, she set off along the cliff path.
examples.chinese: 她给自己装了几份三明治，就沿着山崖的小路出发了。！

examples.english: The weather had been fine on the morning that the climbers set out up the last part of the mountain.
examples.chinese: 那天早上天气很好，登山者就出发去攀登那座山的最后一段。

examples.english: My mother was only twenty when she married my father and set off for Addis Ababa.
examples.chinese: 母亲只有20岁的时候嫁给父亲并前往亚的斯亚贝巴。

examples.english: The Royal Navy set out from Portsmouth on July 13th.
examples.chinese: 7月13日,皇家海军从朴次茅斯启程。

examples.english: Columbus set out to discover America in the fifteenth century.
examples.chinese: 哥伦布在15世纪出海航行，发现了美洲大陆。

examples.english: When are you starting for Seattle?
examples.chinese: 你什么时候动身去西雅图？

examples.english: It was already dark by the time we started for home.
examples.chinese: 我们起程回家的时候，天已经黑了。

examples.english: Before starting off you should check that your seat and mirrors are properly adjusted.
examples.chinese: 出发前，你应该检查一下座位和后视镜的位置是否调节妥当。

examples.english: The riders got back on their horses and started off along the track again.
examples.chinese: 骑手重新跨上马，又沿着小道出发了。

examples.english: It's time we hit the road.
examples.chinese: 我们该动身了。

examples.english: The group will be hitting the road again in the new year, in order to promote their new album.
examples.chinese: 新年里，这个组合将要再次出发宣传他们的新专辑。

examples.english: Let's get going now or we'll miss the train.
examples.chinese: 我们走吧，否则赶不上火车了。

examples.english: The coach was supposed to leave at 10:30 but we eventually got going at 3 o'clock.
examples.chinese: 长途汽车本应该是10点半开的，但是我们最后3点钟才出发。

examples.english: Get going, you two! Didn't you hear the school bell?
examples.chinese: 走吧，你们俩！没听到上课铃响了吗？

centers.english: to start doing something regularly

examples.english: I started going to the gym two years ago.
examples.chinese: 我两年前开始去健身房。

examples.english: She was only 16 when she began seeing Alan.
examples.chinese: 她和艾伦开始交往的时候只有16岁。

examples.english: His parents got divorced last year - that's when he started to take drugs.
examples.chinese: 他父母去年离婚了—他就是那个时候开始吸毒的。

examples.english: When did Bryan take up golf?
examples.chinese: 布赖恩是什么时候开始喜欢上高尔夫球的？

examples.english: Your pictures are so good - you could take up painting as a profession.
examples.chinese: 你的画画得非常好——你可以以此为职业了。

examples.english: Hal turned to drinking after his wife and kids were killed in a car crash.
examples.chinese: 妻子和孩子在车祸中丧生以后，哈尔就开始酗酒了。

examples.english: Research shows that young people without jobs are most likely to turn to crime.
examples.chinese: 研究显示，无业的年轻人更易走上犯罪道路。

examples.english: There's a big ginger cat that's taken to coming in our house at night.
examples.chinese: 有一只姜黄色的大猫到了晚上就会走进我们家里来。

examples.english: My daughter took to spending hours alone in her room, only coming downstairs for meals.
examples.chinese: 我女儿开始整天待在自己的房间里，只有吃饭才下楼来。

examples.english: I only used to have one or two cigarettes, but then I got into the habit of it.
examples.chinese: 我以前只抽一两根香烟，可后来就成为习惯了。

expressions.text: get into the habit of of doing something

examples.english: Try to get into the habit of planning your work at the beginning of the day.
examples.chinese: 要养成习惯，每天一起来就要安排好你的工作。

centers.english: to help or encourage someone to start doing something new
centers.chinese: 帮助或鼓励某人开始做新的事情

examples.english: Her father introduced her to rock ‘n’ roll when she was a little girl.
examples.chinese: 她很小的时候，她父亲就引导她接触摇滚乐。

examples.english: It was my sports teacher who first introduced me to skiing.
examples.chinese: 滑雪是我的体育老师领我入门的。

examples.english: People come to me to be initiated into meditation, as a way of handling stress.
examples.chinese: 人们都来找我学冥想，用这种方法来缓解压力。

examples.english: He tried to initiate her into the mysteries of Chinese cooking.
examples.chinese: 他尝试让她领略到中国烹饪的奇妙之处。

examples.english: Most parents start their babies on solid foods when they are about 4 months old.
examples.chinese: 多数父母在孩子四个月大的时候就开始喂他们吃固体食物了。

examples.english: The doctor said that he wants to start Dad on a special low-cholesterol diet.
examples.chinese: 医生说，他要让爸爸按特别规定进食，吃低胆固醇的东西。

centers.english: to start doing something again in a better way
centers.chinese: 用更好的办法重新开始做某事

expressions.text: start afresh/make a fresh start/make a new start

examples.english: I see the new job as a chance to start afresh.
examples.chinese: 我把这份新的工作当作是重新开始的一个机会。

examples.english: The money we won made it possible for us to pay off all our debts and make a new start.
examples.chinese: 我们中奖得来的钱让我们偿还了所有的债务，并开始新的生活。

examples.english: He's determined to make a fresh start when he gets out of prison.
examples.chinese: 他决定出狱后重新做人。

examples.english: If you make a keying error, just delete it and start over.
examples.chinese: 如果你输入有误，就删除它，重新来一遍。

examples.english: In 1960 the family fled the island of Cuba and, like many others, started over in Miami.
examples.chinese: 1960年，这家人和许多其他家庭一样逃离古巴岛，在迈阿密开始新的生活。

centers.english: to start doing something successfully

expressions.text: make a good start/get off to a good start

examples.english: I haven't finished all my Christmas shopping yet but I've made a good start.
examples.chinese: 圣诞节采购我还没有全部完成，但是开始得很顺利。

examples.english: Chelsea got off to a good start with a victory over Southampton on the first day of the season.
examples.chinese: 切尔西队在赛季的第一天就开局顺利，战胜了南安普顿队。

expressions.text: get off to a flying start

examples.english: Kate's got off to a flying start. She was promoted twice in the first six months.
examples.chinese: 凯特一开始就平步青云，头六个月里提升了两次。

centers.english: to start doing something unsuccessfully

expressions.text: get off to a bad start

examples.english: I got off to a bad start at the interview by spilling my coffee all over my notes.
examples.chinese: 面试的时候我一开头就不顺利，我把咖啡都洒到笔记上了。

examples.english: The senator got off to a bad start, twice forgetting the name of the town he was in.
examples.chinese: 参议员出师不利，两次忘了他所在小镇的名字。

expressions.text: get/start off on the wrong foot

examples.english: What should I wear on my first day? I don't want to start off on the wrong foot.
examples.chinese: 我第一天穿什么衣服好呢？我可不想一开始就给人留下不好的印象。

examples.english: John seems to have got off on the wrong foot with Angela -- she won't even speak to him.
examples.chinese: 约翰似乎一开始就和安杰拉合不来—她连话也不跟他说。

examples.english: After a number of false starts, the Channel Tunnel between England and France finally went ahead in the late 80s.
examples.chinese: 经过几次失败的尝试以后，英法之间的海峡隧道终于在80年代末动工了。

centers.english: to start happening

examples.english: Do you know what time our first class starts?
examples.chinese: 你知道我们第一节课什么时候开始上吗?

examples.english: My day starts at 5 or 6 o'clock, when the baby wakes up.
examples.chinese: 早上五六点钟，宝宝醒了，我的一天也就开始了。

examples.english: The movie was just beginning when Richard and James arrived.
examples.chinese: 理查德和詹姆斯到来的时候，电影刚开始播放。

examples.english: Work on the new bridge will begin next year.
examples.chinese: 这座新桥将于明年动工。

examples.english: Andrew Lloyd Webber's new musical will open later this year.
examples.chinese: 安德鲁·劳埃德·韦伯的新音乐剧将于今年稍后时间开演。

examples.english: A permanent exhibition of Moore's work will open next year.
examples.chinese: 莫尔的作品常设展将于明年开幕。

examples.english: If the meeting kicks off on time, we should be finished by 12 o'clock.
examples.chinese: 如果会议准时开始，12点钟之前应该能够结束。

examples.english: The carnival kicked off with a wonderful firework display.
examples.chinese: 狂欢节以精彩的烟花表演作为开场。

centers.english: to start to exist

examples.english: Pakistan came into existence as an independent country in 1947.
examples.chinese: 巴基斯坦于1947年建国，成为一个独立的国家。

examples.english: Darwin's theory of evolution explains how different species came into being.
examples.chinese: 达尔文的进化论阐述了不同物种是如何出现的。

examples.english: Dozens of websites have sprung up to provide information for travelers.
examples.chinese: 一时间冒出了许多为旅行人士提供信息的网站。

examples.english: New dot.com companies are springing up all the time.
examples.chinese: 新的网络公司不断涌现。

examples.english: When a conflict arises in the workplace, you should aim to repair the relationship as quickly as possible.
examples.chinese: 工作场所里发生冲突，就应该尽快努力修复关系。

examples.english: Low achievement at school often arises from poverty and bad social conditions.
examples.chinese: 在学校里成绩不好往往是贫困和不良社会环境造成的。

examples.english: All staff are expected to do some overtime, if the need arises.
examples.chinese: 如有必要的话，所有员工都要加班。

examples.english: With the invention of the electric guitar, rock ‘n’ roll was born.
examples.chinese: 随着电吉他的发明，摇滚乐诞生了。

examples.english: Picasso was painting pictures in a Cubist style long before the Cubist movement was born.
examples.chinese: 早在立体主义运动诞生之前，毕加索就用立体派风格作画了。

examples.english: The arrival of the railroads after the Civil War produced a huge building boom in California.
examples.chinese: 内战以后铁路的出现，在加州掀起了一股巨大的建筑热潮。

examples.english: the arrival of gene technology
examples.chinese: 基因技术的兴起

centers.english: when something bad starts to happen

examples.english: A fire broke out on the top floor of the building.
examples.chinese: 大楼的顶层突然发生火灾。

examples.english: Late last night, fighting broke out between gangs of rival football fans.
examples.chinese: 昨天深夜，对立的足球迷帮派之间发生了打斗。

examples.english: Thousands of people died as the result of this latest cholera outbreak.
examples.chinese: 最近的这次霍乱爆发造成了成千上万人死亡。

examples.english: There's been an outbreak of food poisoning at the hotel.
examples.chinese: 宾馆里发生了食物中毒事件。

examples.english: The system started to operate in late 1914, a few months after the outbreak of war in Europe.
examples.chinese: 这套体制在1914年末战争在欧洲爆发几个月以后开始运作。

examples.english: A fight over a game of cards had erupted in the corner of the bar.
examples.chinese: 在酒吧的角落里，有人因为玩纸牌突然打了起来。

examples.english: Massive and often violent protests erupted across the country.
examples.chinese: 全国各地都爆发了大规模的抗议活动，其中许多都非常激烈。

examples.english: Gang violence can erupt for no apparent reason.
examples.chinese: 黑帮暴力事件有时无缘无故就会爆发。

examples.english: It looks as if the rain has set in for the day.
examples.chinese: 看来天要下雨了。

examples.english: The doctors operated immediately to prevent any infection setting in.
examples.chinese: 医生们立即进行了手术，以防止发生感染。

examples.english: Worldwide economic recession set in during the early 1980s.
examples.chinese: 20世纪80年代初出现了全球性的经济衰退。

centers.english: to make something start happening

examples.english: The police have already started an investigation.
examples.chinese: 警方已经开始调查了。

examples.english: The referee couldn't start the game because there were fans on the field.
examples.chinese: 裁判没法宣布比赛开始，因为球场上还有球迷。

examples.english: A ‘safe neighbourhood’ campaign has been started by local residents.
examples.chinese: 当地居民发起了“安全社区”活动。

examples.english: Rebel forces launched an attack on the capital.
examples.chinese: 叛军向首都发动了攻击。

examples.english: Police are launching a major murder inquiry.
examples.chinese: 警方正在为谋杀案展开一场大规模的调查。

examples.english: The local hospital has launched a campaign to raise money for new X-ray equipment.
examples.chinese: 当地医院正在为购买新的X光设备发起一场筹款活动。

examples.english: Police have opened an investigation into the girl's disappearance.
examples.chinese: 警方已经对女孩的失踪展开调查。

examples.english: The Football Association are to open an inquiry into recent crowd trouble.
examples.chinese: 足球协会将对最近观众闹事一事展开调查。

examples.english: Peace talks have been initiated in an attempt to avert full scale war.
examples.chinese: 为避免战争全面爆发，和平谈判已经开始。

examples.english: The couple plan to initiate legal proceedings against the police.
examples.chinese: 这对夫妇打算对警方提出起诉。

examples.english: The murder sparked off a wave of protests in the city.
examples.chinese: 这桩命案在市里引发了一波抗议浪潮。

examples.english: Recent freak weather conditions have sparked off renewed fears about the effects of global warming.
examples.chinese: 近来的反常天气再次引起人们对全球变暖所造成的影响产生忧虑。

expressions.text: set in motion/ set into motion

examples.english: A few months later the divorce procedure was set in motion.
examples.chinese: 几个月以后，离婚程序启动了。

examples.english: The government had already set into motion a series of reforms.
examples.chinese: 政府已经开始了一连串的改革。

examples.english: Wait's actions had set in motion a chain of events that would eventually result in his dismissal.
examples.chinese: 韦特的行为引发了一连串的事件，这些事件最后将导致他被解雇。

examples.english: Mark stood up and asked the first question to get the ball rolling.
examples.chinese: 马克站起来，问了第一个问题作为开始。

examples.english: To start the ball rolling, the government was asked to contribute £50,000 to the new charity.
examples.chinese: 政府被要求带头给这个新的慈善机构捐赠50,000英镑。

examples.english: Change is certainly needed and the new headteacher needs to get things moving quickly.
examples.chinese: 改革肯定是需要的，新校长必须尽快取得进展。

examples.english: Once we got things moving, the deal went through very quickly.
examples.chinese: 我们一旦启动，协议很快就达成了。

expressions.text: (let's) get this show on the road

examples.english: Are you all packed and ready? Right, let's get this show on the road.
examples.chinese: 你们都收拾好行李了吗？好了，我们出发吧。

examples.english: We're having another meeting next week, hopefully to really get this show on the road.
examples.chinese: 我们下星期还要开一次会，希望能把这事真正落实。

centers.english: to officially start a new system, method, rule etc
centers.chinese: 正式开始使用新的制度、方法、规定等

examples.english: The company is thinking of introducing medical tests for all employees.
examples.chinese: 公司正考虑为所有员工安排体检。

examples.english: New safety measures will be introduced next month.
examples.chinese: 新的安全措施将于下个月实施。

examples.english: The city authorities are bringing in new parking regulations next month.
examples.chinese: 市政当局将于下个月推行新的停车规定。

examples.english: The new technology will be phased in over a five year period.
examples.chinese: 这项新技术将在五年内逐步采用。

examples.english: In an attempt to reduce opposition to its tax reforms, the government plans to phase them in gradually.
examples.chinese: 为了缓解人们对这次税务改革的反对，政府打算分阶段逐步实施。

examples.english: The new law came into effect in 1991.
examples.chinese: 这项新法律于1991年开始生效。

examples.english: Eventually a ban on the sale of fireworks to children came into operation.
examples.chinese: 禁止向儿童出售烟花的法令终于开始实施了。

centers.english: to start a new business or organization

examples.english: Luigi's family came here in 1966 and started up a chain of restaurants.
examples.chinese: 1966年，卢贾一家人来到这里开了几家连锁餐厅。

examples.english: John decided to start his own textile business shortly after the war.
examples.chinese: 战后不久，约翰决定创办自己的纺织企业。

examples.english: They just opened a new supermarket on Van Nuys Boulevard.
examples.chinese: 他们刚刚在范纽斯大街开了一家新的超级市场。

examples.english: The rail company plans to open several new lines over the next five years.
examples.chinese: 铁路公司计划在未来五年再开几条新线路。

examples.english: Kate and her partner are setting up their own printing business.
examples.chinese: 凯特和她的合伙人正要开办自己的印刷公司。

examples.english: The Enterprise Center runs courses for people who want to set up in business on their own.
examples.chinese: 创业中心为希望自己创业的人举办培训课程。

examples.english: Dad set up as a builder in 1990 and now he employs over twenty men.
examples.chinese: 1990年爸爸成立了一家建筑公司，现在手下雇有二十多个人。

examples.english: The company was established in 1899.
examples.chinese: 这家公司创立于1899年。

examples.english: Most of the money will be used to establish local industries and mobilize the work-force.
examples.chinese: 这笔钱大部分将用于创办地方工业和调用劳动力。

examples.english: The establishment of NATO in 1949 gave the US a leading role in the defence of Europe.
examples.chinese: 1949年北约的成立确立了美国在欧洲防御事务上的主导地位。

examples.english: Who originally founded the college?
examples.chinese: 这所学院最初是谁创办的?

examples.english: The bank was founded 60 years ago in Munich.
examples.chinese: 这家银行60年前于慕尼黑成立。

examples.english: The hospital has served the needs of the local community since its foundation in 1863.
examples.chinese: 这家医院自1863年创建以来满足了当地社区的需要。

examples.english: Within a few years of its inception, the charity was involved in aid projects all around the world.
examples.chinese: 该慈善机构成立了短短几年便参与了世界各地的援助项目。

examples.english: Not long after their inception, the welfare programs were under attack.
examples.chinese: 这个福利计划实施没多久就遭到了抨击。

centers.english: when something starts a natural process
centers.chinese: 某事物开始一个自然过程

examples.english: Investigators still aren't sure what started the fire.
examples.chinese: 调查人员仍然不能确定起火的原因。

examples.english: It is thought that the avalanche was started by a small rock-fall on the higher slopes.
examples.chinese: 据认为，这次雪崩是由高处山坡上一次小小的岩崩引起的。

examples.english: Adding acid to the test tube starts a chemical process which leads to the formation of crystals.
examples.chinese: 把酸加入试管就会产生化学反应，最后形成晶体。

examples.english: The process is activated by sunlight.
examples.chinese: 这个过程是靠阳光激活的。

examples.english: In certain rare circumstances, these vaccines could activate disease.
examples.chinese: 在极少数的情况下，这些疫苗会激活疾病。

examples.english: In 1992, tidal waves set off by a strong earthquake killed around 2000 people.
examples.chinese: 1992年，一场强烈的地震引发海啸，造成约2,000人死亡。

examples.english: The splitting of an atom sets off an explosive chain reaction.
examples.chinese: 原子分裂可造成一触即发的连锁反应。

centers.english: to start an old law, custom, system etc after a long time
centers.chinese: 很久以后重新启用一种旧的法律、习俗、制度等

examples.english: Do you think they should bring back the death penalty?
examples.chinese: 你觉得他们应该恢复死刑吗?

examples.english: They're talking about bringing back formal grammar teaching.
examples.chinese: 他们在讨论恢复正规的语法教学。

examples.english: Many people think that student grants should be reintroduced.
examples.chinese: 许多人认为应该恢复奖学金制度。

examples.english: They are reintroducing English as the official language in schools throughout the country.
examples.chinese: 他们打算在全国各地的学校恢复英语作为官方语言的地位。

examples.english: the reintroduction of compulsory military service
examples.chinese: 义务兵役制的重新引入

examples.english: a campaign to revive the tradition of holding a two-minute silence on Armistice Day
examples.chinese: 恢复停战日静默两分钟的传统的运动

examples.english: They are planning to revive the old Saint's Day parades through town.
examples.chinese: 他们打算恢复圣徒节在镇上游行的古老传统。

examples.english: a revival of interest in sixties music and style
examples.chinese: 对60年代音乐和风格的兴趣的复兴

examples.english: The earlier restrictions on currency exchange have now been restored.
examples.chinese: 早前对货币汇兑的限制现在又恢复了。

examples.english: Next week there will be a debate on the restoration of capital punishment.
examples.chinese: 下个星期将举行一次有关恢复死刑的辩论会。

examples.english: Old theories about the origin of the universe have recently been resurrected.
examples.chinese: 过去关于宇宙起源的理论最近又被重新提出来。

examples.english: There's a growing drive to resurrect the ancient woodland tradition of charcoal burning.
examples.chinese: 恢复古代山林里烧碳传统的需求不断增长。

centers.english: to switch something on

examples.english: Will you switch on the television?
examples.chinese: 你把电视机打开好吗？

examples.english: I switched on the radio to listen to the news.
examples.chinese: 我打开收音机听新闻。

examples.english: Do you mind if I switch the light on?
examples.chinese: 我开灯你介意吗？

examples.english: Exhaust fumes come into the car when I switch on the air conditioner.
examples.chinese: 我打开空调，废气就进车里面来了。

examples.english: He went into the bathroom and turned on the shower.
examples.chinese: 他走进浴室，打开淋浴器。

examples.english: Use timers to turn on indoor lights while you are away.
examples.chinese: 你不在的时候就用定时器打开屋里的灯。

examples.english: Do you want me to turn the lights on?
examples.chinese: 要我把灯打开吗？

examples.english: Eva put the kettle on to make a cup of coffee.
examples.chinese: 伊娃打开水壶的电源冲咖啡。

examples.english: Put the light on, then we can see what we're doing.
examples.chinese: 把灯打开，这样我们做事情就能看见了。

examples.english: It was so cold I put the heating back on.
examples.chinese: 我冷得把暖气又重新开启。

examples.english: She started the car and backed slowly out of the garage.
examples.chinese: 她发动汽车，慢慢地从车库里倒车出来。

examples.english: Clean or replace the air filter before attempting to start the engine.
examples.chinese: 发动引擎前，清洁或更换空气过滤器。

examples.english: I couldn't get my car started this morning.
examples.chinese: 今天早上我的车发动不起来。

examples.english: He rebuilt the engine and finally got it going.
examples.chinese: 他重新装配了引擎，终于把它发动起来了。

examples.english: It took the repairman an hour to get the washing machine going again.
examples.chinese: 修理工花了一个小时才把洗衣机重新启动起来。

examples.english: Someone accidentally set off the fire alarm.
examples.chinese: 有人不小心碰响了火警报警器。

examples.english: Smoke alarms can be set off by smoke from cigarettes, cooking, and fireplaces.
examples.chinese: 香烟、烹调的油烟，以及壁炉的烟都可以触发烟雾报警器。

examples.english: The alarm is so sensitive that the slightest movement will set it off.
examples.chinese: 这个警报器非常敏感，稍有动静便会响起。

examples.english: The bomb's firing mechanism is activated by a time-clock.
examples.chinese: 炸弹的引爆装置由定时器启动。

examples.english: The smoke activated the sprinkler system.
examples.chinese: 烟雾启动了喷洒灭火系统。

examples.english: Mr Carey wound up the old clock and gently set the pendulum going.
examples.chinese: 凯里先生给这个旧时钟上好发条，轻轻地拨动钟摆。

examples.english: She pulled the lever that set the pump going and waited for the water.
examples.chinese: 她拉了一下启动水泵的控制杆，然后等着水流出来。

examples.english: What happens when you press this button?
examples.chinese: 按下这个按钮会怎么样?

examples.english: Somebody pushed the alarm button.
examples.chinese: 有人按了警报的按钮。

examples.english: The Speaker of the House threw the switch for the Christmas tree lights in front of the Capitol building.
examples.chinese: 议院议长扳动开关，点亮了国会大楼前圣诞树上的彩灯。

centers.english: to switch something off

examples.english: Always switch off your computer when you've finished.
examples.chinese: 电脑用完之后一定要关掉。

examples.english: It's OK - I switched the TV off before I went out.
examples.chinese: 没问题，我出门之前把电视机关掉了。

examples.english: Would you turn off the heater before you go to bed?
examples.chinese: 你上床之前把取暖器关掉好吗？

examples.english: You forgot to turn the oven off!
examples.chinese: 你忘记关炉子了！

examples.english: George stopped the engine and got out of the car.
examples.chinese: 乔治关掉引擎下了车。

examples.english: We had to stop the pump and unblock it.
examples.chinese: 我们只能把水泵关掉进行疏通。

examples.english: If you want it to stop, just press the red button.
examples.chinese: 如果你想把它停下来，就按下这个红色的按钮。

examples.english: Norma put out the light and went to sleep.
examples.chinese: 诺尔玛关了灯睡觉了。

examples.english: Could you put the lamp out in the bedroom, please?
examples.chinese: 请你把卧室的灯关了，好吗？

examples.english: Don't forget to turn out the lights when you go to bed, okay Annie?
examples.chinese: 你上床的时候别忘了把灯关掉好吗，安妮？

examples.english: The computer automatically shuts off the pump when no water is flowing.
examples.chinese: 如果没有水在流动，电脑会自动关掉水泵。

examples.english: The Department of Energy shut down the reactor because of safety considerations.
examples.chinese: 能源部出于安全考虑关闭了反应堆。

examples.english: Did you remember to unplug the kettle?
examples.chinese: 你记得把电水壶的插头拔了吗？

examples.english: I was getting a lot of crank calls, so I started unplugging my phone at night.
examples.chinese: 我经常收到古怪的电话，所以晚上我就把电话插头拔掉。

examples.english: Once a car has started, the engine would continue to run even if you disconnected the battery.
examples.chinese: 汽车一旦起动，即使你切断电池的电源，引擎还是会继续运转的。

centers.english: when something is switched on

examples.english: Is the heating on? I'm freezing.
examples.chinese: 暖气开着吗？我冷死了。

examples.english: Did you leave the kitchen light on?
examples.chinese: 你没有关厨房的灯吗?

examples.english: In the winter, I keep the gas fire on all day.
examples.chinese: 到了冬天，我就把煤气取暖器整天开着。

examples.english: A green light shows that the computer is switched on.
examples.chinese: 绿灯亮着表示电脑开着。

examples.english: I don't think he has his cell phone switched on.
examples.chinese: 我想，他没开手机。

examples.english: Have you checked that the power is switched on?
examples.chinese: 你检查过电源是开着吗？

examples.english: If the boiler fails to light, first check that the gas is turned on.
examples.chinese: 锅炉如果点不着火的话，首先要检查煤气是否开着。

examples.english: Is the switch turned on?
examples.chinese: 开关开着吗？

examples.english: Do not touch the machine while it is running.
examples.chinese: 机器在运转的时候不要去碰它。

examples.english: Nick left the engine running to warm it up, while he buckled the children into their car seats.
examples.chinese: 尼克把引擎开着让它预热起来，然后趁这个时候把孩子们安顿在车座上系好安全带。

examples.english: The clock stopped during the night, but it's going again now.
examples.chinese: 时钟夜里停了，但是现在又在运行。

examples.english: The washing machine's going, I can hear it.
examples.chinese: 我听到洗衣机在转。

examples.english: You mustn't open the lid while the sterilizer is working.
examples.chinese: 消毒器在运作的时候，不可以把盖子打开。

examples.english: You can hear the pump in the refrigerator when it's working.
examples.chinese: 冰箱运转的时候可以听到电泵的声音。

examples.english: The plane's engines were ticking over just enough to hold position in the air.
examples.chinese: 飞机的引擎以刚好让飞机保持在空中的低速运转着。

examples.english: He left the car ticking over while he dashed into the house.
examples.chinese: 他让汽车空转着，自己冲进屋里去。

centers.english: when something is switched off

examples.english: Is the cooker off? I can smell gas.
examples.chinese: 炉子关了吗？我闻到煤气了。

examples.english: I don't think anyone's at home. All the lights are off.
examples.chinese: 我想没有人在家，所有灯都关了。

examples.english: Do you mean the alarm was switched off all night?
examples.chinese: 你是说警报器整夜都关着？

examples.english: He didn't realize the microphone was switched off.
examples.chinese: 他不知道麦克风是关着的。

examples.english: Make sure everything's turned off before you leave the house.
examples.chinese: 离家前一定要把各样东西都关掉。

examples.english: The machine is only turned off for a few minutes at a time.
examples.chinese: 这台机器一次只关几分钟时间。

examples.english: She must have left the office as her light is out.
examples.chinese: 她肯定已经走了，因为她办公室的灯关了。

examples.english: All the lights in the house were out.
examples.chinese: 屋里所有的灯都熄灭了。

examples.english: That washing machine doesn't work. It's been disconnected.
examples.chinese: 那台洗衣机不运转，电源被切断了。

examples.english: When the power was disconnected, he carefully removed the back of the computer.
examples.chinese: 电源切断以后，他小心地把电脑的后部拆下来。

examples.english: I've been trying to call her for about half an hour. I think her phone must be off the hook.
examples.chinese: 我一直试着给她打了大约半小时的电话，肯定是她电话没有挂好。

expressions.text: take the phone off the hook

examples.english: I locked the door and took the phone off the hook so no one could disturb me.
examples.chinese: 我锁上门，摘下电话，这样就没有人能打扰我了。

centers.english: when a machine, light etc switches on or off automatically
centers.chinese: 机器、电灯等自动开或关

examples.english: The heating comes on automatically.
examples.chinese: 暖气会自动打开。

examples.english: Every so often, our TV just goes on by itself.
examples.chinese: 我们家的电视机有时会自己开起来。

examples.english: Clap your hands twice, and the light switches on.
examples.chinese: 拍两下手，灯就会亮。

examples.english: Water softening systems turn themselves on at night to clean the system.
examples.chinese: 夜间，软水器会自动开启清洗系统。

examples.english: Suddenly all the lights went out.
examples.chinese: 突然，所有的灯都熄灭了。

examples.english: The street lights have a light sensor that makes them automatically go out at dawn.
examples.chinese: 路灯上装有感光器，黎明时分自动熄灭。

examples.english: The heating goes off at 10:30.
examples.chinese: 暖气在10点30分停止供应。

examples.english: After the 30-car train passed, the flashing red lights went off and the arms blocking traffic went up.
examples.chinese: 这列有30节车厢的火车驶过以后，闪烁的红灯灭了，封锁交通的护栏升了起来。

examples.english: The pump automatically shuts down when no water is flowing.
examples.chinese: 如果没有水流动，水泵就会自动关闭。

examples.english: Something caused the computer to switch itself off.
examples.chinese: 有什么问题导致电脑自动关机了。

examples.english: The bulb inside the refrigerator switches off when you close the door.
examples.chinese: 冰箱关门后，里面的灯就会熄灭。

examples.english: If an automatic can opener fails to turn itself off, food is probably jamming the cutter.
examples.chinese: 自动开罐器如果不能自动关上，可能是食物卡住了刀口。

centers.english: to talk or write about a subject for the first time
centers.chinese: 首次谈及或写到某一主题

examples.english: The first chapter introduces the terms and ideas which will be studied.
examples.chinese: 第一章介绍了以后要学习的术语和观点。

examples.english: Einstein introduced his theory of relativity in 1915, in a scientific paper.
examples.chinese: 爱因斯坦在1915年的一篇科学论文中发表了他的相对论。

examples.english: Her lecture began with a talk about her own experience, leading into a more general discussion.
examples.chinese: 她以自己的亲身经历作为演讲的开始，并以此引入更有普遍性的讨论。

examples.english: Each Olympic event will be led into by sports experts explaining the format.
examples.chinese: 每个奥运会项目开始前都先由体育专家讲解它的形式。

examples.english: He prefaced his criticisms by saying there was much to admire in the work.
examples.chinese: 他开始批评这部作品之前先表示它有许多值得称赞之处。

examples.english: There are 45 tours, each prefaced with a historical overview of the area.
examples.chinese: 共有45条旅游路线，每一条都先有对该地区历史的概况介绍。

centers.english: speech or writing that introduces something
centers.chinese: 介绍某事物的演讲或文章

examples.english: After a brief introduction by the Chairman, the meeting began.
examples.chinese: 主席简短地介绍了一下以后会议便开始了。

examples.english: Powell wrote in the introduction that all the armed forces must work together as a team.
examples.chinese: 鲍威尔在序言中写道，所有武装部队必须要作为一个团队协同行动。

examples.english: I'll start off with a brief intro.
examples.chinese: 我先简单介绍一下就开始。

examples.english: In his intro he said he didn't know much about language teaching
examples.chinese: 他在开场白中说他对语言教学了解得不多。

examples.english: The lead-in to a news item should only take a few seconds.
examples.chinese: 一条新闻的引子应当只占用几秒钟时间。

examples.english: The last sentence in a paragraph should form a lead-in to what is to be discussed in the next paragraph.
examples.chinese: 一段的最后一句应承上启下，引入下一段要讨论的内容。

examples.english: Wilson will give the introductory speech at the education conference.
examples.chinese: 威尔逊将于教育大会上致开幕词。

examples.english: an introductory course on American literature
examples.chinese: 美国文学入门课程

examples.english: In his preface, the author sums up what he has learned from two years of observing political life.
examples.chinese: 作者在序言中对他两年中所观察到的政治生活进行了总结。

examples.english: According to the foreword, the cookbook aims to celebrate the rich variety of Chinese food.
examples.chinese: 这本烹调书在引言中说此书的目的是要称颂中国菜的丰富多样。

examples.english: The play begins with a brief prologue.
examples.chinese: 该剧以一简短的序幕开场。

examples.english: the prologue to Shakespeare's Henry V
examples.chinese: 莎士比亚的《亨利五世》的序幕

centers.english: the last part of an event or period of time
centers.chinese: 事件或一段时间的末尾部分

examples.english: The end of the game was really exciting.
examples.chinese: 比赛的最后阶段非常令人兴奋。

examples.english: He left New York at the end of December.
examples.chinese: 他在12月底离开了纽约。

examples.english: Towards the end of the interview, I was beginning to feel that I might have got the job.
examples.chinese: 面试快结束时，我开始感到，也许我已经获得了这份工作。

examples.english: It was a difficult project and I was glad when it came to an end.
examples.chinese: 这个项目很难，我很高兴终于能结束了。

expressions.text: there's an end/no end in sight

examples.english: The current housing crisis is more serious than ever, and there doesn't seem to be an end in sight.
examples.chinese: 目前的住房危机比以往任何时候都更加严重，而且似乎还看不到尽头。

examples.english: She remained loyal to her unfaithful husband right to the bitter end.
examples.chinese: 她苦撑到底，坚持忠于她那个不忠的丈夫。

examples.english: The company's board vowed to fight to the bitter end, rather than submit to the takeover.
examples.chinese: 公司董事会发誓要苦战到底，不会任由别人接管。

examples.english: Introducing the changes has been a long process, and it's still nowhere near its conclusion.
examples.chinese: 推行改革已经历了一个长期的过程，现在离结束为时甚远。

examples.english: At the conclusion of the trial, the jury returned with a verdict of guilty.
examples.chinese: 在庭审的最后阶段，陪审团宣告了一个有罪的判决。

examples.english: The talks are aimed at bringing the dispute to a conclusion.
examples.chinese: 会谈旨在结束分歧。

examples.english: The beginning of April usually marks the close of the skiing season.
examples.chinese: 4月的开始通常标志着滑雪季节的结束。

examples.english: At the close of trading on the stock market, Ciena shares were up to $37.
examples.chinese: 到股市收盘时，讯远通信公司的股票涨到了37美元。

examples.english: Several hours later, the meeting drew to a close and the board members filed out of the room.
examples.chinese: 数小时后会议结束，董事会成员从会议室里鱼贯而出。

centers.english: the last part of a book, film, show etc
centers.chinese: 书、电影、演出的最后部分

examples.english: It was such a terrible movie, half the audience walked out before the end.
examples.chinese: 这是一部很糟糕的电影，一半的观众在完场前就退席了。

examples.english: The end of the book was so sad that I almost cried.
examples.chinese: 书的结局非常悲惨，我差点儿就哭了。

examples.english: By the end of the play, the main characters have all either died or gone mad.
examples.chinese: 到了戏剧的结尾，主要人物都非死即疯。

examples.english: You don't find out who the killer is until the very end.
examples.chinese: 到了最后结束时才知道谁是凶手。

examples.english: In the Spanish version of this story, the ending is completely different.
examples.chinese: 这个故事的西班牙版本结局完全不同。

examples.english: I love those old Hollywood movies with happy endings.
examples.chinese: 我喜欢那些有美满结局的好莱坞老片。

examples.english: The finale of Beethoven's ninth symphony is really magnificent.
examples.chinese: 贝多芬《第九交响曲》的末乐章气势非常恢宏。

examples.english: For the grand finale there was a marching band and fireworks.
examples.chinese: 仪式在行进乐队的演奏和烟火表演中隆重结束。

examples.english: At the conclusion of the book, the reader is still not certain whether Markham is guilty or not.
examples.chinese: 在书的结尾，读者仍不能肯定马卡姆是否有罪。

examples.english: There was complete silence in the room as the play came to its tragic conclusion.
examples.chinese: 戏剧以悲剧收场，房间里鸦雀无声。

centers.english: happening at the end of an event, time, book, film etc
centers.chinese: 在某一事件、某段时间、某本书、某部影片的结尾发生

examples.english: If you have any questions, can you ask them at the end, please.
examples.chinese: 如果有任何问题，请在最后提问。

examples.english: At the end of the first half, the score was 2-0.
examples.chinese: 上半场结束时，比分为2比0。

examples.english: In his closing speech, he thanked the organizers of the conference.
examples.chinese: 在闭幕词中，他对会议的组织者表示了感谢。

examples.english: In the closing shot of the film, Hoffman walks away from the camera without looking back.
examples.chinese: 影片的最后一幕是霍夫曼头也不回地从摄影机前走远。

examples.english: The closing paragraphs were very moving.
examples.chinese: 结尾的几段非常感人。

expressions.text: the closing stages/minutes etc of something

examples.english: I turned on the TV just in time to catch the closing minutes of the race.
examples.chinese: 我打开电视正好赶上比赛的最后几分钟。

examples.english: The war reached its final stages in July.
examples.chinese: 战争在7月份进入了最后阶段。

examples.english: The Board is expected to make its final decision on the merger by August 12th.
examples.chinese: 预计董事会将在8月12日之前就合并事宜作出最后的决定。

examples.english: Can you come round late morning - at about 11.30?
examples.chinese: 你可以在上午晚些时候，11：30左右过来吗?

examples.english: It was late summer, and the evenings were already getting cooler.
examples.chinese: 那时候是夏末，晚上已经开始变得凉快了。

examples.english: We don't usually expect to have a frost so late in the spring.
examples.chinese: 晚春的这个时候，这里一般不会有霜冻。

examples.english: This point is explained more fully in the latter part of the chapter.
examples.chinese: 这一点在本章的后面部分有更详尽的解释。

examples.english: The house became known as Fields Mill during the latter half of the 17th century.
examples.chinese: 这房子在17世纪后半叶被称为“田园磨坊”。

examples.english: The concert ended with a laser light show.
examples.chinese: 音乐会在激光表演中结束。

examples.english: The coming term finishes with an exhibition in the Arts Centre on December 12th.
examples.chinese: 下个学期以12月12日在艺术中心举办的一场展览作为结束。

centers.english: the part at the end of an object

examples.english: She chewed the end of her pencil thoughtfully.
examples.chinese: 她咬着铅笔头，若有所思。

expressions.text: the end of the road/street/passage etc

examples.english: Go to the end of the street and turn left.
examples.chinese: 这条街走到尽头然后左转。

examples.english: You need a long pole with a hook at one end.
examples.chinese: 你需要一根一头有钩子的长竿。

examples.english: We sat in silence at either end of a long wooden table.
examples.chinese: 我们默不作声地坐在一张长木桌的两端。

examples.english: Ben carved his name in the tree trunk, using the point of his knife.
examples.chinese: 本用刀尖在树干上刻下自己的名字。

examples.english: the point of a needle
examples.chinese: 针尖

examples.english: It has small white flowers, and leaves that taper to a sharp point.
examples.chinese: 它开着白色的小花，叶子一头逐渐变细，收成一个尖端。

examples.english: Doctor Gordon felt my neck with the tips of his fingers.
examples.chinese: 戈登医生用指尖触摸我的颈部。

examples.english: The village is on the southern tip of the island.
examples.chinese: 这个村子在小岛的南端。

examples.english: The nose of the plane dipped as we came in to land.
examples.chinese: 我们开始准备着陆时，飞机机头向下倾斜。

examples.english: Each missile carries 150 kilos of high explosive in its nose.
examples.chinese: 每一枚导弹的弹头上都装载着150公斤烈性炸药。

examples.english: There was a hole in the pipe, no bigger than the head of a matchstick.
examples.chinese: 管子上有个火柴头大小的洞。

examples.english: I picked up a hammer and hit the head of the nail as hard as I could.
examples.chinese: 我拿起一把锤子拼命地敲打钉头。

centers.english: to finish doing something or making something

examples.english: Have you finished your homework yet?
examples.chinese: 你的家庭作业做完了吗?

examples.english: He was in London at the time, finishing a degree in economics.
examples.chinese: 他当时在伦敦读经济学学位。

examples.english: The builders say they should have finished by Friday.
examples.chinese: 建筑工人说到星期五他们应该完工了。

examples.english: ‘To think you might have been ...’ Carlos didn't finish his sentence.
examples.chinese: “想想你有可能…”卡洛斯没把话说完。

examples.english: Give me a call when you've finished unpacking.
examples.chinese: 打开行李后打电话给我。

examples.english: Let me finish washing the dishes, then I can help you.
examples.chinese: 我洗完碗碟，就能帮你了。

examples.english: Ask Jane if she's done that essay yet.
examples.chinese: 问问简文章写好了没有。

examples.english: I've done all the painting. Now all that's left is to put the pictures back up.
examples.chinese: 我已全部粉刷好了，余下的事就是把画重新挂上去。

examples.english: The building is likely to be completed in two years’ time.
examples.chinese: 这幢建筑可能在两年时间内完成。

examples.english: The novel wasn't published until 40 years after it was completed.
examples.chinese: 这部小说完成后过了40年才得以出版。

examples.english: On completion of the course you will be given a certificate of competence.
examples.chinese: 完成这个课程时，你将获得一张资格证书。

examples.english: I'd like to get all these letters finished so I can mail them on my way home.
examples.chinese: 我想把这些信写完，这样我回家的路上就可以寄了。

examples.english: He doesn't think he's going to get the baby's room finished before it's born.
examples.chinese: 他觉得自己不能在小孩出生前把婴儿室装修完毕。

examples.english: We thought it would only take a few minutes to put up the tent but by the time we were through it was dark.
examples.chinese: 我们以为搭帐篷几分钟时间就够了，可我们搭好时天都已经黑了。

examples.english: The mechanic thought he'd be through with the car at about four.
examples.chinese: 修车工认为到大约4点钟他就可以修好车子。

examples.english: By July the research team had concluded the main part of its work.
examples.chinese: 7月份之前研究小组已经完成了大部分工作。

examples.english: The police have now concluded their investigations.
examples.chinese: 警方现在已经完成调查。

centers.english: to have nearly finished something

expressions.text: be nearly finished/done/through / be almost finished/done/through

examples.english: I'm nearly finished -- I just want to put these files away.
examples.chinese: 我快好了—只想把这些文件放好。

examples.english: Are you almost done in the shower?
examples.chinese: 你快洗完澡了吗？

examples.english: Just give me a couple more minutes. I'm nearly through.
examples.chinese: 只要再给我几分钟时间就行。我快做完了。

expressions.text: be on the last lap/in the home stretch

examples.english: The election campaign is now on its last lap.
examples.chinese: 竞选活动已经到了最后阶段。

examples.english: The regular season is in the home stretch, and the playoffs will soon begin.
examples.chinese: 常规赛季已接近尾声，季后赛即将开始。

expressions.text: put the finishing touch/touches to/ put the finishing touch/touches on

examples.english: Sue's just putting the finishing touches to her make-up.
examples.chinese: 休化妆化得差不多了，正在做最后的修饰。

examples.english: Painters are putting the finishing touches on the baseboards and railings.
examples.chinese: 油漆工正在最后油漆踢脚板和栏杆。

examples.english: The team are busy putting the finishing touches to the new design.
examples.chinese: 该小组正忙着这一新设计的结尾工作。

examples.english: Your patio is lovely. Those tubs of flowers are the perfect finishing touch.
examples.chinese: 你家的中庭漂亮极了。那几盆花更是完美的画龙点睛。

examples.english: I came to work early this morning to finish off some urgent work for the boss.
examples.chinese: 今天我一早就来上班给老板把一些紧要的工作完成了。

examples.english: I usually partially cook steaks in the microwave and then finish them off on the grill.
examples.chinese: 我通常用微波炉把牛排烧得半熟，然后在烤架上把它烧熟。

examples.english: If we can tie up the loose ends in the next ten minutes, we'll break for an early lunch.
examples.chinese: 如果下面十分钟内我们能处理完零星事务，我们就停下来提早吃午饭。

examples.english: Apart from a few loose ends that need to be tied up, everything has gone according to schedule.
examples.chinese: 除了还有一些零星事务要处理，一切都如期进行。

examples.english: Mr Samuels is flying to Detroit to finalize the details and sign the contract.
examples.chinese: 塞缪尔斯先生将飞往底特律去把最后的细节问题定下来并签署合同。

examples.english: The meeting has been postponed until travel arrangements can be finalized.
examples.chinese: 会议被推迟到旅行计划能够定下来再开。

centers.english: to finish using something

expressions.gram: transitive phrasal verb not in passive

examples.english: Can you pass me the scissors when you've finished with them, please?
examples.chinese: 你用完剪刀递给我好吗?

examples.english: ‘Have you finished with the Monopoly game?’ ‘Yes.’ ‘Well put it away then.’
examples.chinese: “你玩完‘大富翁’了吗？”“玩完了。”“那就把它放回去。”

examples.english: I need to use the computer. Can you let me know when you're done?
examples.chinese: 我要用一下电脑，你用完了告诉我好吗?

examples.english: Aren't you through with the phone yet?
examples.chinese: 你电话快打完了吗？

centers.english: to finish a performance, speech, meeting etc
centers.chinese: 结束某一表演、讲话、会议等

examples.english: When Sir George finished his speech there was a moment's silence.
examples.chinese: 乔治爵士演说完了之后，出现了片刻的静默。

examples.english: The priest ended the service by saying a short prayer.
examples.chinese: 牧师作了个短短的祷告结束了礼拜。

examples.english: She ended her recital with an old French song.
examples.chinese: 她唱了一首法文老歌，以此结束独唱会。

examples.english: About 40 minutes after the interview starts, the interviewer will signal to you to start winding up.
examples.chinese: 面试开始约40分钟以后，面试官会示意你可以结束了。

examples.english: He was fired and the board would give him no more time to wind up his affairs.
examples.chinese: 他被炒了鱿鱼，董事会不会再给他时间把事情结束掉。

examples.english: Mark wanted to wind the meeting up quickly because he had a plane to catch.
examples.chinese: 马克想快点结束会议，因为他还得去赶飞机。

expressions.text: bring something to an end/to a close

examples.english: The committee has brought to a close one of the longest-running Senate investigations ever.
examples.chinese: 委员会已结束了参议院有史以来历时最久的调查之一。

examples.english: Efforts must continue to bring the conflict to an end.
examples.chinese: 为结束冲突必须继续努力。

examples.english: This year, Muslims will conclude Ramadan with the usual festivities on April 15 or 16.
examples.chinese: 今年穆斯林将在4月15或16日以传统的庆祝活动来结束斋月。

examples.english: He concluded with an appeal to everyone to support the campaign.
examples.chinese: 他以呼吁大家都来支持这次运动作为结束。

examples.english: I'd like to conclude by thanking everyone who has worked so hard to make this conference possible.
examples.chinese: 最后我想感谢诸位，正是由于诸位的努力会议才得以进行。

examples.english: To round off National Peace Week, a concert was organized in the park.
examples.chinese: 在公园里组织了一场音乐会来圆满结束全国和平周活动。

examples.english: She served us a splendid dinner, rounded off with a marvellous orange mousse.
examples.chinese: 她给我们做了一顿丰盛的晚餐，结束时上了一道美味的橘子奶油冻。

examples.english: They rounded off the day with a barbecue at the beach.
examples.chinese: 他们在海滩上举行烧烤结束了一天的活动。

examples.english: Please finish off now. The test will end in three minutes.
examples.chinese: 现在请完成考卷，考试三分钟后结束。

examples.english: You can finish up the puzzle tomorrow morning. It's time for bed now.
examples.chinese: 你到明早再完成拼图游戏吧，现在该上床睡觉了。

expressions.text: finish off/up something by doing something

examples.english: We finished off our vacation by spending two days in Santa Fe.
examples.chinese: 我们在圣菲待了两天后就结束了假期。

examples.english: Dan finished up his act with a joke about the Vice-President.
examples.chinese: 丹拿副总统开了个玩笑来结束他的表演。

centers.english: something that is finished

examples.english: All the packing was finished and we were ready to leave.
examples.chinese: 行李都打点好了，我们准备好离开了。

examples.english: A young couple moved into the house three weeks after it was finished.
examples.chinese: 房子完工三个星期后搬进了一对年轻夫妇。

examples.english: The finished book was more of a personal diary than an autobiography.
examples.chinese: 这本写完的书与其说是自传，倒不如说是私人日记。

examples.english: Looking at the finished product, you wouldn't know it was made from paper.
examples.chinese: 看看这制成品，你看不出是纸做的。

examples.english: Mom won't let me watch television till all my homework's done.
examples.chinese: 我做完了所有家庭作业后妈妈才会让我看电视。

examples.english: We'll send you a bill when the repairs are done.
examples.chinese: 修理好了我们会寄账单给你。

examples.english: We'll only get paid if the job is satisfactorily completed.
examples.chinese: 工作圆满完成了之后，我们才能得到工钱。

examples.english: At last all the arrangements were completed and we waited impatiently for the big day to arrive.
examples.chinese: 所有的准备工作终于都做好了，我们焦急地等候着这个重大日子到来。
