examples.english: Never in my whole life have I felt so angry.
examples.chinese: 我这辈子都没有这么生气过。

examples.english: I haven't ever had champagne before.
examples.chinese: 我以前从未喝过香槟。

examples.english: Don't ever do that again.
examples.chinese: 再也不要干那种事了。

examples.english: ‘I won't leave you,’ she said, ‘not ever.’
examples.chinese: “我不会离开你的，”她说，“永远不会。

examples.english: You must never ever tell anyone what you heard tonight.
examples.chinese: 你今天晚上所听到的，千万千万不能告诉任何人。

examples.english: I'll never, never stop loving you.
examples.chinese: 我将永远永远爱你。

examples.english: You won't get Kieran to agree - not in a million years!
examples.chinese: 你不可能说服基兰同意的一—永远也不可能！

examples.english: After all the help I've given her, she's never said thank you - not once!
examples.chinese: 我帮了她那么多，她却从没说声谢谢—一次都没有！

examples.english: My father hasn't once come here to see us, even though he lives quite nearby.
examples.chinese: 我父亲一次都没来看过我们，虽然他住得很近。

expressions.text: not once have I/did they etc

examples.english: Not once did she think of giving up, despite all the problems involved.
examples.chinese: 尽管困难重重，她从未想过要放弃。

expressions.text: never for a moment/not for a moment

examples.english: I always knew that I would be famous one day. I never for a moment doubted it at all.
examples.chinese: 我一直认为我会有成名的一天。我从来没有怀疑过。

examples.english: ‘Did you ever suspect that he was cheating on you?’ ‘No, not for one moment.’
examples.chinese: “你怀疑过他欺骗你吗？”“没有，从没怀疑过。”

expressions.text: never for a moment have I/did they etc

examples.english: Never for a moment did it enter my mind that something was wrong.
examples.chinese: 我从来没想过会出问题。

expressions.text: at no time have I/did they etc

examples.english: At no time did anyone suggest that the drug was dangerous.
examples.chinese: 从没有人说过这药不安全。！

examples.english: Despite what I'd been told about the local people's attitude to strangers, at no time did I encounter any rudeness.
examples.chinese: 尽管我听说过那里的人对待陌生人态度不好，但是我从未碰到过有人对我无礼。

expressions.text: have never been known to do something

examples.english: We were very worried -- Peter had never been known to disappear from home before.
examples.chinese: 我们非常担心—彼得以前从来没有离家出走过。

examples.english: Killer whales have never been known to attack humans without the provocation of a bullet or harpoon.
examples.chinese: 虎鲸如果没有子弹或捕鲸叉惹它，是不会袭击人类的。

centers.english: sometimes

examples.english: Sometimes I drive to work and sometimes I walk.
examples.chinese: 我有时候开车去上班，有时候走路去上班。

examples.english: Traffic noise is sometimes a problem.
examples.chinese: 交通噪音有时候是一个问题。

examples.english: The journey takes about an hour, sometimes even longer.
examples.chinese: 这个旅程要花一个小时，有时甚至更长。

examples.english: Injuries of this type sometimes take a long time to heal.
examples.chinese: 这类伤口有时要很久才能愈合。

examples.english: Occasionally we go out to restaurants, but mostly we eat at home.
examples.chinese: 我们偶尔也出去上餐馆吃饭，但多数时候都在家里吃。

examples.english: Kay's moods sometimes made life difficult, and occasionally impossible.
examples.chinese: 凯的这种情绪使得日子很不好过，有时简直过不下去。

examples.english: He lives in Australia now, so we only see him very occasionally.
examples.chinese: 他现在住在澳大利亚，所以我们很少看到他。

examples.english: Every so often, Frank looked up at me and smiled.
examples.chinese: 埃夫里偶尔抬起头朝我笑笑。

examples.english: The silence was broken every so often by the sound of guns in the distance.
examples.chinese: 偶尔，远处传来几声枪响打破了沉寂。

examples.english: It would be nice if we could see each other once in a while.
examples.chinese: 我们要是偶尔能见见面就好了。

examples.english: Teaching art can be fairly dull, but once in a while I come across a talent that really excites me.
examples.chinese: 教艺术会很沉闷，但偶尔我也能碰到个天才，那真的叫我兴奋。

examples.english: Every once in a while he disappears for days on end.
examples.chinese: 他偶尔会一连几天不见踪影。

examples.english: In a job like this, you're bound to feel a little stressed at times.
examples.chinese: 做这样的工作，有时候肯定会感到有点压力。

examples.english: At times even the most talented athletes lose their motivation.
examples.chinese: 有时就连最有天赋的运动员也会失去动力。

examples.english: I wear hats now and again, but they don't really suit me.
examples.chinese: 我偶尔也会戴帽子，但我不是很适合戴帽子。

examples.english: Now and then she would check on the baby sleeping in the next room.
examples.chinese: 她不时会去看看睡在隔壁房间的宝宝。

expressions.text: every now and again/every now and then

examples.english: Every now and again a passenger would pass through the carriage on the way to the bathroom.
examples.chinese: 偶尔会有乘客穿过车厢去洗手间。

examples.english: This is the kind of problem that we all have from time to time.
examples.chinese: 我们有时都会遇到这类问题。

examples.english: From time to time a helicopter flew by, but mostly the sky remained clear.
examples.chinese: 偶尔会有一架直升机飞过，但大部分时间天空一片明净。

examples.english: We've been going out together for five years, off and on.
examples.chinese: 我们断断续续地交往了五年。

examples.english: I worked in bars on and off for two years before I decided to go back to college.
examples.chinese: 我断断续续地在酒吧里干了两年，最后还是决定回大学读书。

centers.english: words for describing something that happens sometimes but not often
centers.chinese: 描述事情有时发生但不经常发生的词语

examples.english: The prisoners are allowed occasional visits from their relatives.
examples.chinese: 犯人获准偶尔让亲人来探视。

examples.english: The street was silent except for the occasional burst of laughter from one of the workmen.
examples.chinese: 街上静悄悄的，偶尔传来某个工人的大笑声。

examples.english: I didn't know Terry very well, but we went out for an occasional drink together.
examples.chinese: 我对特里了解不多，不过我们偶尔也一块出去喝一杯。

examples.english: Jim and I have the odd game of cards together.
examples.chinese: 我和吉姆偶尔一起玩玩纸牌。

examples.english: We get the odd complaint from customers, but mostly they're very satisfied.
examples.chinese: 我们偶尔也收到客户投诉，但是他们大都很满意。

examples.english: She is the sort of person who works with intense but intermittent effort.
examples.chinese: 她就是那种人，有时候工作会很努力，但也是间歇性的。

examples.english: The afternoon will be warm but unsettled, with intermittent light rain.
examples.chinese: 下午天气暖和但是不稳定，有间歇小雨。

examples.english: She suffered periodic bouts of depression.
examples.chinese: 她患了定期发作的抑郁症。

examples.english: Periodic failures of the olive crop kept the country in constant debt.
examples.chinese: 橄榄周期性歉收使得国家常年负债。

examples.english: The budget is subject to periodic review.
examples.chinese: 预算须定期审查。

examples.english: Children's homes are periodically inspected by government inspectors.
examples.chinese: 政府视察员定期巡视儿童收容所。

examples.english: Casual users of the library may not realize that they now need a computerized ticket.
examples.chinese: 不常去图书馆的人可能不知道现在他们需要一张电脑化的借书证。

examples.english: Charging an entry fee for museums will not affect the casual visitor very much.
examples.chinese: 博物馆收取入场费不大会影响到偶尔来参观的人。

centers.english: when something happens many times

examples.english: I often see her walking past with the children on the way to school.
examples.chinese: 我上学途中经常看到她带着孩子经过。

examples.english: Dad wasn't often angry so I knew something terrible must have happened.
examples.chinese: 爸爸不常生气，所以我知道肯定是发生什么糟糕的事情了。

examples.english: ‘Have you ever been to the China Moon Café?’ ‘Yes -- we go there quite often.’
examples.chinese: “你去过汉月咖啡厅吗？”“去过—我们经常去那儿。”

examples.english: I have a cell phone, but I don't use it very often.
examples.chinese: 我有个手机，但我不常用。

expressions.text: it's not often (that) somebody does something

examples.english: It's not often that you see a grass snake these days -- they've become quite rare.
examples.chinese: 现在游蛇不大常见—它们已变得很稀有了。

examples.english: It's not often I get the chance to go to the movies.
examples.chinese: 我不是经常有机会去看电影的。

examples.english: It's nice to meet you. Wendy's talked about you a lot.
examples.chinese: 很高兴认识你，温迪经常讲起你。

examples.english: I used to walk a lot, but I've been very lazy recently.
examples.chinese: 以前我走很多路，但是最近懒了。

examples.english: She goes abroad on business quite a lot.
examples.chinese: 她经常到国外出差。

examples.english: Passengers complain that trains are frequently cancelled.
examples.chinese: 旅客投诉火车经常取消。

examples.english: You have to be willing to change jobs frequently if you want to get to the top in business.
examples.chinese: 如果你想事业有成，就必须愿意频繁地换工作。

examples.english: Frequently, she would find herself gazing out of the window lost in thought.
examples.chinese: 她时常会凝视着窗外，陷入沉思。

examples.english: Graham's doctor had repeatedly warned him not to work so hard.
examples.chinese: 格雷厄姆的医生一再告诫他工作不要太累。

examples.english: Max was punched and kicked repeatedly as he lay on the ground.
examples.chinese: 马克斯躺在地上不断遭到拳打脚踢。

examples.english: Al Gore has stated repeatedly, that the American economy is dependent upon a healthy environment.
examples.chinese: 戈尔一再指出，美国经济依赖于良好的环境。

examples.english: She kept asking the same question again and again.
examples.chinese: 她再三地问相同的问题。

examples.english: Again and again I was thrown upwards from my bunk as the ship battled through the storm.
examples.chinese: 船在暴风雨中行驶，我一次又一次地从床铺上被抛起来。

examples.english: It was a sound he'd heard thousands of times before.
examples.chinese: 那个声音他以前听到过无数次。

examples.english: My grandmother must have spoken to him hundreds of times but, surprisingly, she didn't know his name.
examples.chinese: 我祖母一定跟他说过许多次话了，可她竟然不知道他的名字。

examples.english: I've been rejected hundreds of times, but if you don't try you never will get a job, will you?
examples.chinese: 我被拒绝了无数次，但是如果你不去尝试，就永远找不到工作，你说呢？

expressions.text: somebody has/had done something many times (before)

examples.english: I had walked down this road many times before, but somehow today it seemed different.
examples.chinese: 这条路我以前走过许多次，但今天不知何故好像不一样了。

expressions.text: as somebody has/had done many times (before)

examples.english: The woman upstairs shouted down at us and threatened to call the police, as she had done many times before.
examples.chinese: 楼上的女人探下头呼喝我们，威胁要报警，像她以前多次干过的那样。

examples.english: I looked down at the town, as I had done so many times as a young man, and remembered the people I had known there.
examples.chinese: 我俯瞰着小镇，像我年轻时常常做的一样，想起了以前在那里认识的人。

centers.english: too often, in a way that is annoying

examples.english: Dad, Bobby keeps hitting me!
examples.chinese: 爸爸，博比老是打我！

examples.english: How can I explain if you keep on interrupting me?
examples.chinese: 你老是打断我，我怎么解释呢?

examples.english: I keep forgetting to mail this letter.
examples.chinese: 我老是忘了寄这封信。

examples.english: I'm sick of Harold, he's always telling me what to do.
examples.chinese: 我讨厌哈罗德，他老是对我指手画脚。

examples.english: What do you mean you never see me? You're at my house all the time.
examples.chinese: 你说从未见过我，这是什么意思？你一直在我家。

examples.english: I don't know about you, but I'm always having arguments with people!
examples.chinese: 我不了解你，但我是经常会和人家争吵的！

examples.english: He was always trying to persuade me to go out drinking with him.
examples.chinese: 他老是想法子说服我和他一起出去喝酒。

examples.english: They seemed to be continually arguing.
examples.chinese: 他们好像不停地吵架。

examples.english: I wish you'd clean up your room without having to be constantly reminded.
examples.chinese: 我希望你自己会打扫房间，不要老要人家提醒。

examples.english: He never does his homework on time and is forever getting into trouble at school.
examples.chinese: 他从来不准时做功课，在学校里老是惹麻烦。

examples.english: We bought a new washing machine. The old one was forever breaking down.
examples.chinese: 我们买了一台新的洗衣机，旧的那台老是坏掉。

examples.english: Half the time the managers don't know what's going on.
examples.chinese: 那些经理大部分时间都不知道发生了什么事。

examples.english: I never know where he is - half the time he doesn't return my phone calls.
examples.chinese: 我从来都不知道他在哪里一他经常不回我的电话。

examples.english: I've told you time and time again not to play with matches - it's dangerous.
examples.chinese: 我一次又一次地告诉过你不要玩火柴—那是很危险的。

examples.english: I see people making the same mistakes, time and time again.
examples.chinese: 我看到人们一再犯同样的错误。

examples.english: Yes, the bank keep piling these outrageous charges on my bank account with great regularity.
examples.chinese: 是呀，银行经常会从我的账户里收取这些骇人的费用。

examples.english: With great regularity, wasps would fly in through the open window and get trapped behind the glass.
examples.chinese: 经常会有黄蜂从开着的窗户飞进来，被困在玻璃后面飞不出去。

examples.english: We've discussed this ad nauseam, and I don't see the point of going over the same ground again.
examples.chinese: 这事我们已经讨论得够多了，我看没有必要再老话重提了。

examples.english: He goes on ad nauseam about how much better everything was in the old days.
examples.chinese: 他喋喋不休地讲过去的一切是多么多么的美好。

centers.english: ways of saying what someone or something often does
centers.chinese: 某人经常做某事或某事物经常发生的说法

examples.english: Dave tends to arrive late, so don't worry yet.
examples.chinese: 戴夫经常迟到，所以现在还不用担心。

examples.english: The problem with this model of car is that the gearbox tends to seize up.
examples.chinese: 这种型号的汽车缺点是变速箱容易卡住。

examples.english: My father tends to interfere too much in other people's business.
examples.chinese: 我父亲经常对其他人的事情干涉太多。

examples.english: Recent studies show that girls tend to be better at languages than boys.
examples.chinese: 最近的一些研究显示，女孩子在语言方面往往强于男孩子。

expressions.text: have a tendency to do something

examples.english: Divorced people have a tendency to live with new partners rather than marry again.
examples.chinese: 离过婚的人往往倾向于和新的伴侣同居，而不是再婚。

examples.english: It's poor quality cloth, with a tendency to shrink.
examples.chinese: 这布料质量很差；容易缩水。

examples.english: Eliott's family has a tendency to put on weight, and so his parents enrolled him in a special gym for kids.
examples.chinese: 埃利奥特的家族有肥胖的倾向，所以他父母给他报名参加了一个儿童特别健身班。

examples.english: Victor is inclined to be somewhat domineering.
examples.chinese: 维克托总是有点专横。

examples.english: Middle-class victims of crime are more inclined to contact the police.
examples.chinese: 中产阶级的罪案受害人更倾向于与警察联系。

expressions.text: have a habit of doing something

examples.english: Be careful not to annoy the boss. He has a habit of losing his temper.
examples.chinese: 小心别去惹老板，他爱发脾气。

examples.english: We shouldn't rule out a Democrat victory yet. These things have a habit of changing just when you least expect it.
examples.chinese: 我们现在还不能排除民主党获胜的可能，这种事情经常在你最意想不到的时候发生变化。

examples.english: He was apt to get very upset when things went wrong.
examples.chinese: 出了差错时他很容易就会变得很心烦意乱。

examples.english: The pond was apt to dry up during summer.
examples.chinese: 那个池塘在夏天经常干涸。

centers.english: often happening or often done

examples.english: His job involved making frequent trips to Saudi Arabia.
examples.chinese: 他的工作要经常出差到沙特阿拉伯。

examples.english: The doctor recommended frequent salt baths to help the wound heal.
examples.chinese: 医生建议经常洗盐水浴，以促进伤口愈合。

examples.english: My duties brought me into frequent contact with Captain Nagumo.
examples.chinese: 我的工作使我经常要接触南云上校。

examples.english: As the treatment began to take effect, her headaches became less frequent.
examples.chinese: 治疗开始见效，她头痛的次数渐渐减少了。

examples.english: As a frequent business traveler, I have spent many nights in bland hotel rooms.
examples.chinese: 我经常出差，很多个晚上都是在冷冰冰的旅馆客房里度过。

examples.english: Simmons is a frequent guest on daytime TV talk shows.
examples.chinese: 西蒙斯是日间电视访谈节目的常客。

examples.english: The torture involved repeated beatings and electric shock treatment.
examples.chinese: 酷刑包括反复拷打和电击虐待。

examples.english: There have been repeated requests for the United Nations to send peace-keeping forces to the area.
examples.chinese: 他们已一再要求联合国派遣维和部队到该地区去。

examples.english: Massieu remains a free man, despite repeated attempts to arrest him on murder and drug charges.
examples.chinese: 马西厄依然是个自由人，尽管警方多次试图以谋杀和吸毒的罪名逮捕他。

examples.english: Ingrained attitudes and habitual ways of thinking are very difficult to change.
examples.chinese: 根深蒂固的处世之道和惯常的思维方式是很难改变的。

examples.english: Tony's habitual laziness became even more extreme in winter, and he would sometimes stay in bed until mid afternoon.
examples.chinese: 托尼懒惰成性，到了冬天更加变本加厉，有时候会在床上待到下午很晚的时候。

examples.english: My father was a habitual gambler, until my mother packed her bags and threatened to leave.
examples.chinese: 我爸爸曾经嗜赌成性，直到妈妈收拾行李威胁要离开他。

examples.english: It is estimated that as many as half the young men in the community are habitual drug users.
examples.chinese: 估计该社区有多达一半的年轻男人经常吸毒。

examples.english: Bernstein habitually arrived at airports just moments before departure.
examples.chinese: 伯恩斯坦总是在飞机就要起飞的时候才赶到机场。

examples.english: It's impossible to work with these constant interruptions.
examples.chinese: 这些无休止的干扰令人没法工作。

examples.english: We've had continual problems with the computer system ever since it was installed.
examples.chinese: 我们的电脑系统安装了之后一直出现问题。

centers.english: how often something happens

examples.english: How often do you see your parents?
examples.chinese: 你多久去看一次父母？

examples.english: What should you feed a puppy and how often?
examples.chinese: 你要用什么东西喂小狗，多久喂一次?

examples.english: When Peter said I was heartless it made me wonder how often I'd shown my lack of sympathy.
examples.chinese: 彼得说我无情的时候，我就想，我是不是经常这样冷漠。

examples.english: It's amazing how often this kind of thing happens.
examples.chinese: 奇怪呀，这种事情竟然经常发生。

examples.english: How many times has she been married?
examples.chinese: 她结过几次婚了?

examples.english: I can't remember how many times I've been to New York.
examples.chinese: 我记不起去过纽约多少次了。

expressions.text: how many times a day/week/year etc

examples.english: How many times a week do you go swimming?
examples.chinese: 你一星期去游几次泳？

examples.english: The doctor wanted to know how many times I went to the toilet in a day.
examples.chinese: 医生想知道我一天上几次厕所。

examples.english: The computer can tell you the number of times a word occurs in a piece of writing such as a book.
examples.chinese: 电脑能告诉你某个单词在一本书等的文字作品中出现的次数。

examples.english: The rate of respiration is the number of times the patient breathes in and out during a given period.
examples.chinese: 呼吸率是指病人在一定时间内吸气和呼气的次数。

examples.english: Try to increase the number of times you exercise per week.
examples.chinese: 尽量增加你每周的锻炼次数。

examples.english: The frequency of mining accidents has steadily decreased over the past 20 years.
examples.chinese: 在过去的20年里，采矿事故发生的频率已稳步下降。

examples.english: The high frequency of cases of diarrhoea is attributable to poor food hygiene.
examples.chinese: 腹泻病例频繁出现是因为食物不卫生。

examples.english: Crimes of this type are happening with increasing frequency.
examples.chinese: 这一类罪案发生得越来越频繁。

centers.english: at or immediately after the time when something happens
centers.chinese: 在某事发生之时或刚发生之后

examples.english: When I give the signal, turn on the engine.
examples.chinese: 我发出信号就启动引擎。

examples.english: When I told her what had happened she was really shocked.
examples.chinese: 我告诉她发生的事时，她十分震惊。

examples.english: We'd only just finished cleaning the house when our guests arrived.
examples.chinese: 客人到的时候，我们刚把房子打扫干净。

examples.english: You can change when you get back.
examples.chinese: 你回来后可以换一下衣服。

examples.english: I quit school when I was 15.
examples.chinese: 我15岁时辍学。

examples.english: When completed, the road will link four major cities.
examples.chinese: 完工后，这条公路将连接四座大城市。

centers.english: when one thing always happens when another thing happens
centers.chinese: 一事总是在另一事发生之时发生

examples.english: Her nose crinkles up when she laughs.
examples.chinese: 她笑的时候鼻子会皱起来。

examples.english: When the demand for a product is high, its price tends to rise.
examples.chinese: 当产品需求大时，价格就会上涨。

examples.english: I can't understand you when you mumble.
examples.chinese: 你咕咕哝哝地说，我听不懂你的话。

examples.english: You know what she's like when she's on the phone.
examples.chinese: 你知道她打电话的时候是什么样子。

examples.english: Whenever we come here, we see somebody we know.
examples.chinese: 我们每次到这里来，都看见熟人。

examples.english: You should come and talk to me whenever you have a problem.
examples.chinese: 只要你有问题就应该来跟我说。

examples.english: I try to rest whenever I can.
examples.chinese: 我尽量能休息就休息。

examples.english: He still visited her whenever he could.
examples.chinese: 他还是一有时间就去看她。

examples.english: She still visits her old schoolfriends whenever possible.
examples.chinese: 她还是一有机会就去看老同学。

examples.english: Every time she sees me she says looks away.
examples.chinese: 她每次看见我都把目光移向别处。

examples.english: Every time it rains we get a flood in the bedroom.
examples.chinese: 每次下雨我们的卧室都进水。

examples.english: Don't ask me for money every time you want to buy a drink.
examples.chinese: 别每次想喝酒时都问我要钱。

centers.english: when one thing happens during the time that something else is happening
centers.chinese: 在某事发生期间发生另一事

examples.english: I hurt my shoulder when I was playing football.
examples.chinese: 我踢足球的时候伤了肩膀。

examples.english: All this happened when we were living abroad.
examples.chinese: 这一切发生在我们旅居国外的时候。

examples.english: When she was at college she wrote for a student newspaper.
examples.chinese: 她读大学的时候为一家学生报撰稿。

examples.english: Why were you downstairs when everyone else was in bed?
examples.chinese: 别人都在睡觉，你为什么在楼下？

examples.english: My car was stolen while I was on holiday.
examples.chinese: 我度假的时候车子被偷了。

examples.english: While she was out of the room, he took a quick look at the papers on her desk.
examples.chinese: 趁她离开房间时，他看了一眼她桌上的文件。

examples.english: While Sandy was filling out the forms, I called Jimmy from the airport.
examples.chinese: 桑迪在填写表格的时候，我从机场打电话给吉米。

examples.english: Patients often comment upon lack of sleep whilst in hospital.
examples.chinese: 病人常常埋怨说住院时睡眠不足。

examples.english: They keep the animals under sedation whilst they're being transported.
examples.chinese: 在运送过程中他们给动物用了镇静剂。

examples.english: They were killed while attempting to reach the summit.
examples.chinese: 他们在冲顶时丧生。

centers.english: ways of asking or mentioning when something happened or will happen
centers.chinese: 某事何时发生的问法或说法

examples.english: When are you leaving for Norway?
examples.chinese: 你什么时候动身去挪威？

examples.english: When did you last have something to eat?
examples.chinese: 你最后一次吃东西是在什么时候?

examples.english: Do you know when the concert will be held?
examples.chinese: 你知道音乐会什么时候举行吗？

examples.english: I can't remember when I bought this.
examples.chinese: 我不记得这东西是什么时候买的。

examples.english: What time is dinner?
examples.chinese: 几点吃晚饭?

examples.english: What time did you get in last night?
examples.chinese: 你昨天晚上是什么时候到家的?

examples.english: What time do you usually get home from work?
examples.chinese: 你通常几点下班回到家？

examples.english: I don't know what time she's coming home.
examples.chinese: 我不知道她会几点回家。

examples.english: At what point did you try to stop the fight?
examples.chinese: 你是什么时候去劝架的?

examples.english: At what point did you realize that your book would never be published?
examples.chinese: 你是在什么时候意识到你的书永远不会出版的？

examples.english: I'm not sure at what point he began to suspect the truth.
examples.chinese: 我不确定他是在什么时候开始怀疑事情的真相。

centers.english: after a particular time or event
centers.chinese: 在某一时间或某事发生以后

examples.english: We went for a walk after lunch.
examples.chinese: 午饭后我们去散了步。

examples.english: Gingrich won election to the House in 1978, after Flynt retired.
examples.chinese: 1978年弗林特退休之后，金里奇当选进了众议院。

examples.english: What did you do after leaving school?
examples.chinese: 你离开学校之后干了些什么?

expressions.text: an hour/two days/a year etc after

examples.english: My father died two days after I was born.
examples.chinese: 我出生两天后，父亲就去世了。

examples.english: He joined the army in 1914, and soon after was promoted to the rank of captain.
examples.chinese: 他于1914年入伍，不久以后就被提升为上尉。

examples.english: Paul rushed home right after school.
examples.chinese: 保罗一放学就直奔家里。

examples.english: My sister and her husband moved west just after their wedding.
examples.chinese: 我姐姐和她丈夫结婚不久就搬到西部去住了。

examples.english: The Pirates jumped to a 22-2 lead, and Georgetown never got closer than 16 points after that.
examples.chinese: 海盗队的比分一下子升到以22比2领先，之后乔治敦队就再也没有将比分拉近到16分之内。

examples.english: It was terrible: first the gearbox seized and after that the radiator burst.
examples.chinese: 那真是糟透了：先是变速箱被卡住，然后散热器又爆裂了。

examples.english: The operation was rather painful, but I felt a lot better afterwards.
examples.chinese: 手术的时候很痛，但过后我感觉好多了。

examples.english: Afterward, Nick said he'd never been so nervous in his life.
examples.chinese: 后来尼克说，他这一生从没这么紧张过。

examples.english: A couple of years afterwards I bumped into her in a supermarket.
examples.chinese: 几年以后我在一家超级市场里碰见了她。

examples.english: His wife fell ill in June and died soon afterwards.
examples.chinese: 6月份他的妻子病倒了，之后不久便离开了人世。

examples.english: Can you remember what happened next?
examples.chinese: 你记得接着发生了什么事吗?

examples.english: First, chop up two large onions. Next, fry them until they are golden brown.
examples.chinese: 先将两只大洋葱剁碎，然后把它们炸成金黄色。

examples.english: I'll tell you about it later when I'm not so busy.
examples.chinese: 以后不太忙的时候我会把这件事告诉你的。

examples.english: Ronald Reagan joined the Republican Party in 1962 and later became Governor of California.
examples.chinese: 罗纳德·里根于1962年加入共和党，后来成了加利福尼亚州的州长。

expressions.text: three months/two years/ten days etc later

examples.english: A couple of days later I saw her in a downtown bar.
examples.chinese: 几天之后我在镇上的一个酒吧里见到了她。

examples.english: The first part of the film is really boring but it gets better later on.
examples.chinese: 这部电影的开头部分真的很枯燥无味，但后来就有意思多了。

examples.english: I found out much later that some of the children I taught had become teachers themselves.
examples.chinese: 很久以后，我发现我教过的一些孩子自己也当了老师。

examples.english: Later that month we got another letter from them asking for more money.
examples.chinese: 那个月的后半月，我们收到了他们的另一封信，说再要些钱。

examples.english: First we played tennis, and then we went swimming.
examples.chinese: 我们先是打网球，然后去游了泳。

examples.english: Add a cup of sugar. Then beat in three eggs.
examples.chinese: 加一杯糖，然后打入三个蛋。

examples.english: The book was published in 1954 and was subsequently translated into fifteen languages.
examples.chinese: 这本书于1954年出版，后来被译成15种语言。

examples.english: The six men were subsequently acquitted of all charges, but only after they had served 17 years in prison.
examples.chinese: 这六个男人后来都被无罪释放，但那时他们已坐了17年的牢。

centers.english: after a particular period of time has passed
centers.chinese: 在某一段时间过去之后

expressions.text: after a week/several hours/a long time/a while etc

examples.english: After half an hour we got tired of waiting and went home.
examples.chinese: 半小时后，我们等得不耐烦，于是回家了。

examples.english: At first I was very nervous, but after a while I began to feel more confident.
examples.chinese: 起初我非常紧张，但过了一会就开始更有信心了。

expressions.text: after a week/a year etc of (doing) something

examples.english: The war ended after another six months of fighting.
examples.chinese: 战争又打了半年以后结束了。

expressions.text: within a month/two weeks/a year etc

examples.english: He was bitten by a snake. Within three hours he was dead.
examples.chinese: 他被蛇咬了，不到三小时就死了。

examples.english: The fire alarm went off and within minutes the building had been cleared.
examples.chinese: 火警警报响起来后，没过几分钟大楼里的人就全都撤离了。

expressions.text: within a month/a few days etc of doing something

examples.english: Within six years of joining the company he was Managing Director.
examples.chinese: 他进公司不到六年就当了总经理。

examples.english: Within a short space of time, Gerry had managed to offend everyone in the group.
examples.chinese: 还没多久，格里就把组里的每个人都得罪了。

expressions.text: in a minute/a few hours/a month etc

examples.english: She'll be here in a few minutes.
examples.chinese: 她过几分钟就来。

examples.english: I'll see you again in a day or two.
examples.chinese: 我一两天以后再来看你。

expressions.text: in an hour's time/a few minutes’ etc time

examples.english: In a few weeks’ time I'll be off to university.
examples.chinese: 几个星期以后，我就要去上大学了。

expressions.text: 24 hours/a week/six months/100 years from now

examples.english: A week from now we will be in Paris.
examples.chinese: 一星期以后我们将在巴黎了。

examples.english: What do you think you'll be doing six months from now?
examples.chinese: 六个月以后你说你会在干什么?

examples.english: Four hundred years from now people will still be listening to Mozart .
examples.chinese: 400年以后人们还是会听莫扎特的音乐。

centers.english: starting to happen after now or after a particular time
centers.chinese: 从现在起或从某一时间起开始发生

expressions.text: from now on/ from here on (out)

examples.english: You'll be working with me from now on.
examples.chinese: 从现在起你就跟我一起工作了。

examples.english: From here on out I'll come to every meeting, I promise.
examples.chinese: 我保证从今以后每一次会议我都来参加。

examples.english: From now on Neil is responsible for publicity and marketing.
examples.chinese: 从现在起由尼尔负责推广和营销。

examples.english: As of the first of July, all back seat passengers must wear seat belts.
examples.chinese: 自7月1日起，后座的乘客都必须系安全带。

examples.english: The new timetable will come into effect from January 2003.
examples.chinese: 新的时间表将于2003年1月起开始实行。

examples.english: Starting today Miss Carey will be in charge of the Sales Department.
examples.chinese: 从今天起凯里小姐将负责销售部。

examples.english: I'm busy right now. Could you come back sometime after 4 o'clock?
examples.chinese: 我现在正忙，你4点钟以后再来好吗？

examples.english: After 1800, more and more people worked in factories.
examples.chinese: 1800年以后，越来越多人在工厂工作。

examples.english: If they left just after twelve, they should be here soon.
examples.chinese: 他们如果是12点刚过走的，应该马上就回来了。

examples.english: He found out that I had lied to him, and after that he never trusted me again.
examples.chinese: 他发现我对他撒了谎，从那以后他就再也不相信我了。

examples.english: The company started a big new advertising campaign, and business really improved after that.
examples.chinese: 这家公司开始了一场大规模的新广告宣传活动，从那以后，生意真的好起来了。

examples.english: I'm going to help you for the first two weeks, but after that you'll be working on your own.
examples.chinese: 前两个星期我会帮你，但以后你就要靠自己了。

examples.english: The latest sunrise of the winter is Friday; from then on, the dark winter mornings get brighter earlier .
examples.chinese: 今冬最晚的日出在周五，此后漆黑的冬日早晨会早点亮起来。

examples.english: He went to his first football game when he was four, and from then on he was crazy about it.
examples.chinese: 他四岁的时候第一次去看足球比赛，从那以后就迷上了。

examples.english: The plants should be watered every day for the first week and twice a week thereafter.
examples.chinese: 这些植物第一个星期应该天天浇水，以后一周浇两次。

examples.english: On retirement each employee will receive a lump sum of £10,000 and a regular annual pension thereafter.
examples.chinese: 每位雇员退休时可以一次性得到一万英镑，此后定期领取年金。

examples.english: When we got home it was gone midnight.
examples.chinese: 我们到家时已经过了半夜。

examples.english: We have to get you home. It's past your bedtime.
examples.chinese: 我们得带你回家，你的上床时间都已过了。

centers.english: coming after someone or something else

examples.english: Who was the next president of the United States after Ronald Reagan?
examples.chinese: 继罗纳德·里根之后的下一位美国总统是谁？

examples.english: When's the next flight to Miami?
examples.chinese: 飞往迈阿密的下一个航班是几点?

examples.english: I finished my classes on the 5th, and the next day I went home to Cleveland.
examples.chinese: 我5号上完课，第二天就回了克利夫兰的家。

examples.english: Next week I'm going on a training course in Seaford.
examples.chinese: 下个星期我要去锡福德上培训班。

expressions.text: the day after/the Saturday after/the week after etc

examples.english: Helen arrived on July 20th and I arrived the week after.
examples.chinese: 海伦是7月20日到达的，而我是在后一个星期到达的。

examples.english: The party's not this Thursday but the Thursday after.
examples.chinese: 那聚会不是在这个星期四，而是在下个星期四。

examples.english: The weather changed the morning after we arrived.
examples.chinese: 我们到达后的第二天早晨天气就变了。

examples.english: I felt rather tired the day after the party.
examples.chinese: 聚会的第二天我感到很累。

examples.english: If we miss the ten o'clock train we'll just have to catch the one after.
examples.chinese: 如果我们赶不上10点的火车，就只好赶下一班了。

examples.english: The following day she woke up with a splitting headache.
examples.chinese: 第二天她醒来时头痛欲裂。

examples.english: They agreed to meet the following week in the Cafe Rouge.
examples.chinese: 他们同意下周在红房子咖啡馆见面。

examples.english: We can sort out the final details at a later stage.
examples.chinese: 我们可以以后才处理这些细枝末节。

examples.english: In later centuries Venice lost its former importance and began to go into decline.
examples.chinese: 在之后的几个世纪里，威尼斯失去原有的重要地位，开始衰落。

examples.english: These skills were then handed down to subsequent generations of craftsmen.
examples.chinese: 后来这些技术就传给了后代的工匠。

examples.english: Many of Marx's theories were disproved by subsequent events.
examples.chinese: 后来的事件证明他的许多理论是不正确的。

examples.english: The first meeting will be in the City Hall, but all subsequent meetings will be held in the school.
examples.chinese: 第一次会议将在市政府大楼召开，但以后的各次会议均会在学校举行。

examples.english: The government started to borrow money in 1961, and the national debt has steadily increased with each succeeding year.
examples.chinese: 政府于1961年开始借钱，之后国债逐年递增。

examples.english: The effects of exposure to atomic radiation at Hiroshima have been passed on to succeeding generations.
examples.chinese: 原子弹辐射的影响祸及了以后好几代广岛居民。

examples.english: Someone shouted ‘Fire!’ and in the ensuing panic several people were injured.
examples.chinese: 有人大喊“着火了！”，在随后的慌乱之中，几个人受伤了。

examples.english: They met each other several times over the ensuing six months.
examples.chinese: 接下去的6个月内，他们彼此见了几次面。

examples.english: Once you have installed solar heating you will receive regular follow-up visits from our experts.
examples.chinese: 你一旦安装了太阳能供暖装置，就会有专家定期到访跟进。

examples.english: After each training programme everyone has a follow-up interview with their manager.
examples.chinese: 每次培训课程结束后，每个人都要跟经理作一次跟进的面谈。

examples.english: It is our duty to preserve our culture for future generations.
examples.chinese: 为子孙后代保护我们的文化是我们的职责。

examples.english: In future years some of you will regret the decision you have made today.
examples.chinese: 将来你们有些人会对自己今天所作的决定感到后悔。

examples.english: The company is building apartment buildings for future employees.
examples.chinese: 这家公司正在建公寓大楼给未来的雇员居住。

centers.english: to happen after something else

examples.english: We saw each other a lot in the months that followed.
examples.chinese: 后来的几个月里我们经常见面。

examples.english: the long period of stability that followed the war
examples.chinese: 战后的长期稳定

examples.english: The wedding was followed by a big party at the Chelsea Hotel.
examples.chinese: 婚礼之后在切尔西酒店举行了一场大型聚会。

examples.english: Suddenly there was a shout from above, immediately followed by a loud bang.
examples.chinese: 突然上面传来一声叫喊，紧接着的是砰的一声巨响。

examples.english: China's first nuclear test in October 1964 was closely followed by a second in May 1965.
examples.chinese: 中国在1964年10月进行了第一次核试验之后，很快又在1965年5月做了第二次核试验。

examples.english: There then followed a long and painful silence.
examples.chinese: 接着就是好一阵令人难受的沉默。

examples.english: The agreement came after six months of negotiations.
examples.chinese: 这个协议是经过6个月的谈判之后达成的。

examples.english: The Napoleonic Wars came after the French Revolution.
examples.chinese: 法国大革命之后发生了拿破仑战争。

expressions.text: come three weeks/five days etc after something

examples.english: My first chance to talk to her came three days after our quarrel.
examples.chinese: 我们吵了架三天之后，我才有机会第一次和她说话。

examples.english: The New Stone Age lasted about 1200 years in Britain. The period which came after is known as the Bronze Age.
examples.chinese: 新石器时代在英国大约延续了1,200年，在这之后的一段时期称青铜时代。

examples.english: I objected to what he had just said and a heated argument then ensued.
examples.chinese: 我反对他刚才所说的话，接着就发生了激烈的争论。

examples.english: The police were called in to quell the riot that ensued.
examples.chinese: 警察被召来平息之后发生的暴乱。

expressions.text: come on the heels of something

examples.english: The news comes on the heels of the FBI's announcement that last week's crash was caused by mechanical failure.
examples.chinese: 联邦调查局宣布上周的坠机事件是由机械故障所致，跟着就传来了这个消息。

expressions.text: hot/hard on the heels of something

examples.english: Tuesday's victory came hard on the heels of last week's shock defeat by Manchester United.
examples.chinese: 上周意外败于曼联队之后，紧接着在星期二就迎来了一场胜利。

examples.english: In the wake of Thailand's economic troubles, Malaysia's currency also sank.
examples.chinese: 泰国的经济危机余波未平，马来西亚的货币也出现贬值。

centers.english: the person who does a particular job after someone else

examples.english: In January 1947, Secretary of State Byrnes resigned; his successor was General Marshall.
examples.chinese: 1947年1月，国务卿伯恩斯辞去职务，他的继任者是马歇尔将军。

examples.english: Two weeks after the death of Pope John Paul, the cardinals met to elect his successor.
examples.chinese: 约翰·保罗教皇逝世两个星期以后，红衣主教们聚集起来选举他的继任人。

examples.english: Many people regard him as a likely successor to the current managing director.
examples.chinese: 许多人都认为他有可能接替现任总经理的职位。

examples.english: Eisenhower was succeeded by John F. Kennedy.
examples.chinese: 艾森豪威尔之后由约翰·F.肯尼迪继任。

expressions.text: succeed to the throne/the presidency/the championship etc

examples.english: Louis XIII succeeded to the throne when he was only nine years old.
examples.chinese: 路易十三继承王位的时候只有九岁。

expressions.text: succeed somebody as King/President/Secretary General etc

examples.english: Bailey will succeed Fuller as Director of Operations.
examples.chinese: 贝利将接替富勒任营运董事。

examples.english: Who is next in line when the current leader of North Korea dies?
examples.chinese: 现任领导人去世之后将会由谁继任?

examples.english: Tom's next in line for the boss's job.
examples.chinese: 下一个被提拔当老板的将是汤姆。

expressions.text: next in line to the throne

examples.english: Edward VIII was succeeded by his younger brother, who was next in line to the throne.
examples.chinese: 爱德华八世之后，下一个王位继承人是他弟弟。

examples.english: Who do you think will be the next prime minister?
examples.chinese: 你认为下一任总理会是谁?

examples.english: The next boss was better than the old one.
examples.chinese: 后来的老板比以前的一位好。

centers.english: a book, film etc that comes after an earlier one

examples.english: ‘Batman 2’ was a rare example of a sequel being better than the original.
examples.chinese: 《蝙蝠侠2》是少有的比第一集拍得好的续集。

examples.english: After the unexpected success of his first film, Rodriguez is making plans for a sequel.
examples.chinese: 在第一部影片获得意想不到的成功之后，罗德里格斯打算拍一部续集。

examples.english: the sequel to ‘Gone with the Wind’
examples.chinese: 《乱世佳人》的续集

centers.english: after someone or something in a list, series, line of people etc
centers.chinese: 在名单或清单、系列、一排人等中位于某人或某事物之后

examples.english: My name is after yours on the list.
examples.chinese: 在名单上我的名字排在你的后面。

examples.english: You'll find a reference number after each item in the catalogue.
examples.chinese: 产品目录上每件商品之后都有一个编号。

examples.english: There were several people after me who didn't manage to get into the game.
examples.chinese: 在我之后还有好几个人也没法进去看比赛。

examples.english: Could you ask the next patient to come in, please?
examples.chinese: 请你叫下一个病人进来好吗?

examples.english: Look at the diagram on the next page.
examples.chinese: 见下页的图表。

examples.english: Turn left at the next traffic light.
examples.chinese: 在下一个红绿灯向左拐。

examples.english: This topic will be discussed more fully in a later chapter.
examples.chinese: 这个主题在后面的一章里有更详细的讨论。

centers.english: to be the next person or thing after another in a list, series etc
centers.chinese: 在名单或清单、系列等中位于某人或某物之后

expressions.gram: verb phrase not in progressive or passive

examples.english: My name should be after yours if the list is alphabetical.
examples.chinese: 如果名单是按字母顺序排列的，我的名字应该排在你的后面。

examples.english: In British and American addresses, the name of the town always comes after the name of the street.
examples.chinese: 在英美地址里，镇的名字总是写在街道名字后面。

examples.english: The first line of the poem is ‘I wandered lonely as a cloud’. What comes after that?
examples.chinese: 这首诗的第一句是“独自漫游似浮云”，后面是什么？

examples.english: This book's called ‘The Third Dimension’. Which book comes next in the series?
examples.chinese: 这本书叫《第三维》，这个系列的下一本是什么？

examples.english: The nurse came out of her office and called out, ‘Who's next?’
examples.chinese: 护士从办公室里走出来喊道：“下一位是谁？”

examples.english: The first three sections of the course are just an introduction. Next comes the difficult bit.
examples.chinese: 这个课程的前面三个部分只是个介绍，接下去的内容便有些难度了。

examples.english: Taylor explains his theory in the pages that follow.
examples.chinese: 泰勒在随后的几页里对他的理论作了解释。

examples.english: In English the letter Q is always followed by a U.
examples.chinese: 英语里字母Q的后面总是跟着一个U。

examples.english: Each chapter is followed by a set of exercises.
examples.chinese: 每一章后面都有一套练习。

centers.english: immediately

examples.english: If your baby has a fever you should call the doctor immediately.
examples.chinese: 如果孩子发烧，你应立即打电话给医生。

examples.english: When mother saw my face, she knew immediately that something was wrong.
examples.chinese: 母亲看到我的脸色，马上就知道出事情了。

examples.english: Knowing the case was urgent, I replied to her lawyer's email immediately.
examples.chinese: 我知道此案很急迫，就立刻回复了她律师的电子邮件。

examples.english: The principal wants to see you at once.
examples.chinese: 校长要马上见你。

examples.english: We're in love and we want to get married right away.
examples.chinese: 我们相爱，想马上结婚。

examples.english: You said it was important so I came straight away.
examples.chinese: 你说这事很重要，所以我立刻就来了。

examples.english: Katie, put that down this minute, or you'll go straight to bed.
examples.chinese: 凯蒂，快把那东西放下，要不现在就上床睡觉去。

examples.english: Tell Mick that I want to see him in my office, right now.
examples.chinese: 告诉米克我要立刻在我办公室见他。

examples.english: If you lose your passport, you should contact the embassy without delay.
examples.chinese: 如果你遗失了护照，应该立即与大使馆联系。

examples.english: The crew and passengers were keen to get airborne without further delay.
examples.chinese: 机组人员与乘客急着要立即起飞。

centers.english: immediately after something else happens
centers.chinese: 其他事发生后随即发生

examples.english: We met at a friend's party, and immediately became friends.
examples.chinese: 我们在一位朋友的聚会认识后，立刻成了朋友。

examples.english: There was a loud explosion in the engine-room, and almost immediately a fire broke out.
examples.chinese: 发动机房里发生了爆炸，一声巨响后随即起了大火。

examples.english: I'll call you immediately we hear any news about the baby.
examples.chinese: 我们若得知有关宝宝的任何消息，我就立刻打电话给你。

examples.english: We'll have to leave immediately after the meeting.
examples.chinese: 会议结束后我们得马上离开。

examples.english: Mrs Smith was admitted to hospital at 10 o'clock, but died immediately afterwards.
examples.chinese: 史密斯夫人10点钟被送进医院，但随后很快就去世了。

examples.english: As soon as Stephen felt well again, he returned to work.
examples.chinese: 斯蒂芬一恢复健康就回来工作了。

examples.english: I will pay you back, I promise, the moment I get paid.
examples.chinese: 我保证一发工资就还钱给你。

examples.english: Honey, I swear, I'll phone you the moment I get to New York.
examples.chinese: 宝贝，我保证一到纽约就给你打电话。

examples.english: No sooner had they sat down to eat than the phone rang.
examples.chinese: 他们一坐下吃东西，电话铃就响起来了。

examples.english: No sooner had he arrived in the city than his wallet was stolen.
examples.chinese: 他刚到市里，钱包就被偷了。

examples.english: No sooner were the words out of her mouth than she regretted them.
examples.chinese: 她话一出口就后悔了。

examples.english: I'd done food shopping and had barely gotten to the door, when Debbie asked if I'd been listening to the radio.
examples.chinese: 我去商店买好食品刚到家门口，戴比就问我是不是听了收音机广播。

examples.english: Hardly had the film reached our screens last July than it was plagued by troubles and controversy.
examples.chinese: 去年7月份这部电影刚在我们的电影院上映就引来了麻烦和争议。

expressions.text: lose no time in doing something

examples.english: When the new manager was appointed, he lost no time in reorganizing the office.
examples.chinese: 新经理受命上任后立即就着手重组办公室。

examples.english: Murdock lost no time in setting out for London to find work.
examples.chinese: 默多克马上就去伦敦找工作。

examples.english: Sea snakes inject a poison so strong that it kills a fish instantly.
examples.chinese: 海蛇射出的毒液毒性很强，可把一条鱼当场杀死。

examples.english: It was a head-on crash and both drivers died instantly.
examples.chinese: 这次是迎头相撞，两名驾驶者当场丧生。

examples.english: He was killed outright when his car crashed at high speed.
examples.chinese: 他撞车时车速很快，当场死亡。

centers.english: when you do something immediately

examples.english: I could see at a glance that the situation was serious.
examples.chinese: 我一眼就看出情况很严重。

examples.english: An expert can tell at a glance whether it's a real diamond or a fake.
examples.chinese: 专家一眼便可识别出钻石是真是假。

examples.english: I was so angry I almost resigned on the spot.
examples.chinese: 我非常恼火，差一点当场辞职。

examples.english: The police can fine motorists on the spot for driving offences.
examples.chinese: 警察可以给违反驾驶规定的驾驶者当场罚款。

examples.english: Look, I can't give you a decision on the spot. I'll have to talk to my boss first.
examples.chinese: 瞧，我不能马上决定，得先和老板商量一下。

examples.english: When Kate knew John was seeing someone else, she should have left him then and there.
examples.chinese: 当凯特知道约翰在和别人约会时，她本应该马上就离开他。

examples.english: This time I'd gone too far, and I quit drinking there and then.
examples.chinese: 这一次我是太过分了，于是我当即就戒了酒。

expressions.text: off the top of your head
expressions.gram: adverb phrase usually in questions or negative sentences

examples.english: ‘How old is Chris?’ ‘I don't know off the top of my head.’
examples.chinese: “克里斯多大岁数？”“我一时想不起来。”

examples.english: There are some good restaurants around here, but I can't tell you their names off the top of my head.
examples.chinese: 附近有几家不错的餐馆，可我一时说不上它们的名字。

examples.english: I couldn't think of a reply right off.
examples.chinese: 我一时想不出个答复。

examples.english: We let them know right off the bat that we weren't going with them.
examples.chinese: 我们不假思索地告诉他们，我们不打算和他们同去。

centers.english: happening or needing to be done immediately
centers.chinese: 立刻发生或需要立刻做

examples.english: My immediate reaction was shock and horror.
examples.chinese: 我的即时反应是震惊和恐怖。

examples.english: This letter requires your immediate attention.
examples.chinese: 这封信需要你立即处理。

examples.english: The immediate needs of the refugees are for warm clothing and clean drinking water.
examples.chinese: 难民迫切需要的是暖和的衣服和清洁的饮用水。

examples.english: One immediate worry is money.
examples.chinese: 当下担心的是钱。

examples.english: The workers are being threatened with instant dismissal.
examples.chinese: 工人们受到了立刻被解雇的威胁。

expressions.text: take an instant dislike to somebody

examples.english: He took an instant dislike to LeRoy.
examples.chinese: 他一见面就不喜欢勒罗伊这个人。

examples.english: Death from a massive heart attack was almost instantaneous.
examples.chinese: 严重的心脏病突发造成的死亡几乎是即时的。

examples.english: Lightning flashed, followed almost instantaneously by the sharp crack of thunder.
examples.chinese: 一阵闪电，随后几乎是同时爆发出一声炸雷。

centers.english: at a later time

examples.english: Sorry, I'm busy right now - I'll speak to you later.
examples.chinese: 对不起，我现在正忙—过会和你再谈。

examples.english: We heard later that he had gone back to Japan.
examples.chinese: 我们后来听说他回日本去了。

expressions.text: a month/two weeks/three years etc later

examples.english: She became ill in 1993, and died two years later.
examples.chinese: 她在1993年生病，两年以后过世了。

examples.english: I didn't find out the truth until much later.
examples.chinese: 我很久以后才知道真相。

examples.english: Later that afternoon, Anna came to see me.
examples.chinese: 那天下午稍晚些时间，安娜来看我了。

examples.english: We are developing a training course to run later in the year.
examples.chinese: 我们正在设计一门培训课程，准备今年稍后开办。

examples.english: Dixon pleaded guilty to all the charges and will be sentenced at a later date.
examples.chinese: 对于所有的指控狄克逊都认罪了，他将于稍后的某个日期被判刑。

examples.english: In a later speech, the minister admitted he had been wrong.
examples.chinese: 在后来的一次讲话中，部长承认自己错了。

examples.english: Label the pipes you will be working on to avoid confusion later on.
examples.chinese: 把要处理的管子贴上标签，免得以后弄混淆了。

examples.english: Later on, I'll be interviewing the Prime Minister, but first here is a summary of the news.
examples.chinese: 稍后我将采访首相，现在先来看新闻摘要。

expressions.text: in a minute/24 hours/a week etc

examples.english: I'll be back in a couple of days.
examples.chinese: 我过几天就回来。

examples.english: The doctor would like to see you again in two weeks.
examples.chinese: 医生想两个星期以后再见你。

expressions.text: in an hour's time/a few minutes’ time etc

examples.english: Just think, in a few hours’ time we'll be in Seattle.
examples.chinese: 你想啊，几个小时以后我们就在西雅图了。

expressions.text: 24 hours/a week/100 years etc from now

examples.english: Three weeks from now the exams will be over.
examples.chinese: 三个星期以后考试就结束了。

examples.english: A hundred years from now there may be no rainforest left.
examples.chinese: 一百年以后可能就没有热带雨林了。

examples.english: After a few minutes, she fell asleep.
examples.chinese: 几分钟以后她睡着了。

examples.english: After ten days, their supplies of water were running low.
examples.chinese: 十天之后，他们的用水供应不足了。

examples.english: After a while, we got tired of waiting and went home.
examples.chinese: 过了一会儿，我们等得累了，就回家了。

examples.english: The decision was subsequently reversed on appeal.
examples.chinese: 这个判决随后在上诉中被撤销了。

examples.english: He was savagely attacked and sustained severe injuries from which he subsequently died.
examples.chinese: 他遭到残暴的攻击受了重伤，不久就离世。

centers.english: to arrange to do something at a later time
centers.chinese: 安排在稍后的某个时间做某事

examples.english: Several of today's football games have been postponed because of heavy snow.
examples.chinese: 今天有几场足球赛因为下大雪延期了。

examples.english: They decided to postpone the wedding until Pam's mother was out of the hospital.
examples.chinese: 他们决定把婚礼推迟到帕姆的母亲出院以后才举行。

expressions.text: postpone something for two days/three weeks etc

examples.english: In 1968, the Oscar ceremony was postponed for two days, following the assassination of Martin Luther King.
examples.chinese: 1968年，因为马丁·路德·金遇刺，奥斯卡颁奖仪式推迟了两天。

examples.english: I really should go to the dentist, but I keep putting it off.
examples.chinese: 我实在应该去看看牙医，但我总是一拖再拖。

examples.english: The concert's been put off till next week.
examples.chinese: 音乐会推迟到下个星期了。

examples.english: The committee decided to put off making any decision until the new year.
examples.chinese: 委员会决定新年过后再作决策。

examples.english: He decided to delay his departure until after he'd seen the Director.
examples.chinese: 他决定等到见过董事才走。

examples.english: The police delayed making any announcement until the girl's relatives had been contacted.
examples.chinese: 警方等联系到那女孩的家人之后才发出通告。

examples.english: The meeting has been put back to next Thursday.
examples.chinese: 会议已被推迟到下个星期四举行。

expressions.text: put something on ice/put something on the back burner

examples.english: We're going to have to put our plans on ice until we can raise some more money.
examples.chinese: 我们只好推迟我们的计划，等到多筹些钱再说。

examples.english: I've put my acting career on the back burner for a while, while I concentrate on my writing.
examples.chinese: 我已经暂时不考虑我的演艺事业，而先集中精力写作。

examples.english: He hesitated and procrastinated for weeks before he finally told her he wanted their relationship to end.
examples.chinese: 他犹犹豫豫，拖了好几个星期，后来终于告诉她，他希望结束他们之间的关系。

examples.english: Certain players are procrastinating over their contracts in order to see how much money they can squeeze out of their clubs.
examples.chinese: 有些球员签约一拖再拖，想看看能从俱乐部榨出多少钱来。

examples.english: She finally agreed to take the job after months of procrastination.
examples.chinese: 她拖了几个月之后终于答应接受这份工作。

centers.english: in a later place in a book, list etc
centers.chinese: 在书、名单等中较后面的位置

examples.english: Later in the poem there is a reference to the poet's unhappy childhood.
examples.chinese: 诗中后面有地方提到诗人不幸的童年。

examples.english: The author returns to the same subject in a later section of the book.
examples.chinese: 在书中较后面的一部分中，作者又回到了原来的这个主题上。

examples.english: I will explain how to deal with this problem in a later chapter.
examples.chinese: 在稍后的一章里我将解释如何处理这个问题。

examples.english: Later on in the article he repeats this statement.
examples.chinese: 他在文章的后面部分重复了这一声明。

examples.english: Further on in the book we find a full description of the system.
examples.chinese: 在书中的后面部分，我们可以看到有关这个体系的详细介绍。

centers.english: continuing to a particular time or event and then stopping

examples.english: My father worked as a teacher until 1989.
examples.chinese: 在1989年之前父亲一直是个教师。

examples.english: I'll be at home until 5:30, if you want to phone me.
examples.chinese: 5点半之前我都在家，你可以打电话给我。

examples.english: Vicky polished the car until it shone.
examples.chinese: 维基把车擦得闪闪发亮。

examples.english: David continued living at home until he was 26.
examples.chinese: 戴维一直在家住到了26岁。

examples.english: The library's only open till five on Saturdays.
examples.chinese: 图书馆周六只开到5点钟。

examples.english: Just wait till I've finished my coffee. Then we can go.
examples.chinese: 等我喝完咖啡，然后一起走。

examples.english: Up until the nineteenth century wood was commonly used for buildings.
examples.chinese: 19世纪之前木材一直普遍用于建筑。

examples.english: She continued to write poetry, up to the day she died.
examples.chinese: 直到去世那天她都一直在写诗。

examples.english: Things had been going very well up until then.
examples.chinese: 直到那时事情都进行得非常顺利。

examples.english: Right up until the last minute she had hoped that Peter would change his mind.
examples.chinese: 直到最后一分钟她还是希望彼得会改变主意。

examples.english: We worked from seven in the morning until late at night.
examples.chinese: 我们从早上7点一直工作到深夜。

examples.english: Max edited the paper from 1950 until he retired in 1989.
examples.chinese: 马克斯从1950年起一直任那份报纸的编辑，直到1989年退休。

expressions.text: from May to September/from 9am to 5pm etc

examples.english: The hotel is only open during the main tourist season, from March to October.
examples.chinese: 这间酒店只在3月至10月的旅游旺季才营业。

examples.english: Eisenhower was President from 1952 to 1956.
examples.chinese: 艾森豪威尔是1953年至1961年的美国总统。

expressions.text: May through September/Monday through Friday etc

examples.english: The store is open Monday through Saturday.
examples.chinese: 这家商店从星期一开到星期六。

examples.english: Prices are generally lowest from January through March and highest June through August.
examples.chinese: 一般来说，1月至3月价格最低，6月至8月价格最高。

examples.english: Visit the exhibition of modern art, open every day, 9:30-6:00.
examples.chinese: 参观当代艺术展览，每天开放，时间9:30-6:00。

examples.english: A special fishing licence is required for the season (May-September).
examples.chinese: 5月至9月垂钓季节需持专门的垂钓许可证。

centers.english: towards

examples.english: If you walk along the river bank towards Skipton you come to a bridge.
examples.chinese: 如果你沿着河边朝斯基普顿方向走去，你会走到一座桥边。

examples.english: She stood with her back toward the window.
examples.chinese: 她背对窗站着。

examples.english: Glancing towards me, he started to laugh.
examples.chinese: 他向我一瞥，开始笑起来。

examples.english: He looked toward the ceiling of his tiny apartment.
examples.chinese: 他往自己小套间的天花板看去。

examples.english: The plane banked and turned toward the mountains.
examples.chinese: 飞机倾斜机身朝山那边转过去。

examples.english: He slid the plate of cookies toward her.
examples.chinese: 他把一盘小甜饼朝她滑过去。

examples.english: Tyler strode off in the direction of Foxwood.
examples.chinese: 泰勒大踏步朝福克斯伍德的方向走去。

examples.english: People were making a move in the direction of the dining room.
examples.chinese: 人们朝饭厅方向走去。

examples.english: I glanced in her direction and our eyes met.
examples.chinese: 我朝着她的方向瞥了一眼，结果我们四目相对。

expressions.text: in the general direction of something

examples.english: He waved a hand in the general direction of the bar. ‘Another drink?’
examples.chinese: 他向吧台的大致方向挥了挥手。“再来一杯吗？”

examples.english: We set off for Boston at daybreak.
examples.chinese: 我们黎明时出发去波士顿。

examples.english: When it started to rain we headed for the trees as fast as we could.
examples.chinese: 天开始下雨，我们以最快的速度向树林走去。

examples.english: It's getting dark -- we'd better make for home.
examples.chinese: 天快黑了，我们最好回家去。

examples.english: The London bound train leaves at 22.00 hours.
examples.chinese: 前往伦敦的火车将于22:00开出。

examples.english: There have been several delays to southbound rail services.
examples.chinese: 南行的火车有好几班误点。

examples.english: We were travelling westbound on Interstate 90.
examples.chinese: 我们在90号州际公路上往西行驶。

examples.english: All inward bound flights are being cancelled due to heavy fog.
examples.chinese: 由于大雾，所有的返港航班都被取消了。

examples.english: The children stared at the eerie old house.
examples.chinese: 孩子们盯着那幢怪异的老房子看。

examples.english: Nick looked back and grinned at her.
examples.chinese: 尼克回过头向她咧着嘴笑。

examples.english: I threw the ball at Joe and hit him on the back of the neck.
examples.chinese: 我把球向乔扔去，打在他的后颈上。

centers.english: to go or come towards someone or something
centers.chinese: 往某人或某物走去或走来

examples.english: Go towards the church and take the first turning on your left.
examples.chinese: 朝教堂方向走，在第一个路口向左转。

examples.english: He watched her going toward West End Avenue.
examples.chinese: 他看着她朝西端大街走去。

examples.english: He looked up to see two policemen coming towards him.
examples.chinese: 他抬起头看见两个警察正朝他走来。

examples.english: As we came toward the house, the door flew open and out came Polly.
examples.chinese: 我们正向屋子走去时门突然打开，波莉从里面出来了。

examples.english: The bus was heading towards the Friedrich-Strasse railway station.
examples.chinese: 公共汽车正向腓特烈-施特拉斯火车站驶去。

examples.english: They saw the patrol boat turn and head towards them.
examples.chinese: 他们看见巡逻艇转了弯向他们直驶过来。

examples.english: As he made for the door he tripped and fell heavily.
examples.chinese: 他奔向门口时绊了一下，重重地摔在地上。

examples.english: We headed for the nearest island to try and repair our boat.
examples.chinese: 我们驶向最近的岛屿设法把船修复。

examples.english: I made a beeline for the bar and ordered myself a double whisky.
examples.chinese: 我迳直奔向酒吧，为自己要了杯双料威士忌。

examples.english: Jeff made a beeline for a table where two pretty Russian girls were sitting.
examples.chinese: 杰夫直奔向那张坐了两位漂亮的俄罗斯姑娘的桌子。

examples.english: In May 1681 a force of about 2500 troops advanced on Mondovi.
examples.chinese: 1681年5月，一支约有2，500名士兵的军队向蒙多维推进。

examples.english: He moved towards her as though he was advancing on a dangerous animal.
examples.chinese: 他向她靠近时就好像在逼近一头危险的野兽。

examples.english: As they approached the wood, a deer ran out of the trees.
examples.chinese: 他们走近树林的时候，一头鹿从树丛中跑了出来。

examples.english: We walked silently, so they would not hear us approach.
examples.chinese: 我们悄无声息地走着，因此他们听不到我们越走越近。

centers.english: to go towards someone because you want to speak to them
centers.chinese: 向某人走去想要跟他们说话

expressions.gram: transitive phrasal verb not in passive

examples.english: An old guy come up to me in the street and asked for a dime for coffee.
examples.chinese: 在街上有个老家伙走到我面前，求我给他一角钱买咖啡。·

examples.english: Total strangers used to come up to me and tell me how much they'd enjoyed the show.
examples.chinese: 以前常有完全不认识的人走过来对我说他们非常喜欢那次演出。

examples.english: A tourist approached us and asked us the way to the theatre.
examples.chinese: 一个游客走到我们身边问去剧院的路。

examples.english: Several people approached Fleming as he left the hall.
examples.chinese: 弗莱明离开大厅时，有几个人向他走去。

examples.english: She was approached by a waiter.
examples.chinese: 一位男服务员向她走来。

examples.english: I was sitting in the back row, when Harry sidled up and sat down next to me.
examples.chinese: 我坐在后排，突然哈里悄悄地走过来坐在我身边。

examples.english: He sidled up to me without a word and slipped a note in my pocket.
examples.chinese: 他一言不发地悄悄走到我身边，朝我衣袋里塞了张字条。

centers.english: during

examples.english: Terry's work has improved a lot during the last three months.
examples.chinese: 特里的工作在过去的三个月里进步了不少。

examples.english: Henry died during the night.
examples.chinese: 亨利在夜里去世了。

examples.english: During the summer we spend a lot more time out of doors.
examples.chinese: 夏天我们在户外的时间比起其他季节要多得多。

examples.english: At some time during the weekend someone broke into the building.
examples.chinese: 周末的某个时候有人闯入了大楼内。

examples.english: This place was an air-raid shelter during the war.
examples.chinese: 这地方在战时是一个防空洞。

examples.english: The long vacation is in August.
examples.chinese: 长假是在8月份。

examples.english: In the last six years, Carol has moved three times.
examples.chinese: 过去六年时间里，卡罗尔搬了三次家。

examples.english: In 1982 Paget was living in Geneva.
examples.chinese: 1982年佩吉特住在日内瓦。

examples.english: I always feel drowsy early in the morning.
examples.chinese: 一大早我总感到昏昏欲睡。

examples.english: There have been five serious accidents within the last few days.
examples.chinese: 过去几天之内就发生了五起严重的事故。

examples.english: If we do not hear from you within 14 days, we will contact our solicitors.
examples.chinese: 如果14天之内没有你的回复，我们就会和我们的事务律师联系。

examples.english: The party continued through the night until dawn.
examples.chinese: 聚会彻夜进行，一直持续到黎明时分。

examples.english: We'll have to see how he copes through the next couple of months.
examples.chinese: 我们得看看在接下来的几个月里他会是怎么应付的。

examples.english: It's closed all through the winter, and opens again in April.
examples.chinese: 它整个冬天都关闭，到4月份再开放。

examples.english: Throughout her career she has worked hard and maintained high standards.
examples.chinese: 她在整个职业生涯都努力工作，并保持着高标准。

examples.english: She's been a great help to me over the past year.
examples.chinese: 在过去一年内她给了我极大的帮助。

examples.english: They plan to redecorate their house over the Christmas period.
examples.chinese: 他们打算在圣诞节期间重新装修一下房子。

examples.english: They met in 1962 and wrote each other several letters over the next few years.
examples.chinese: 他们于1962年相遇，此后几年内相互通了几封信。

expressions.text: in the course of/during the course of

examples.english: In the course of a few years, Lambert built up a highly successful export business.
examples.chinese: 兰伯特在几年间建立了一家非常成功的出口企业。

examples.english: We expect to see some important political developments during the course of the next week or two.
examples.chinese: 我们希望在接着的一两周内能看到政治上有些重要的进展。

examples.english: By day they relied on the sun for direction, and by night they followed the stars.
examples.chinese: 白天他们靠着太阳辨别方向，到了夜里就按星星的方位行动。

examples.english: By day he works at a construction company, but by night he's a bartender.
examples.chinese: 白天他在一家建筑公司上班，晚上他就当酒保。

examples.english: They attacked by night, hoping to surprise their enemy.
examples.chinese: 他们在夜里进攻，以期能使敌人猝不及防。

centers.english: happening during the time that something else happens
centers.chinese: 某事发生在其他事情发生期间

examples.english: I bought a magazine while I was waiting for the train.
examples.chinese: 我在等火车时买了本杂志。

examples.english: I'll just make a phone call while you finish the dishes.
examples.chinese: 你收拾碗碟时我去打个电话。

examples.english: They're still working on our bedroom. Meanwhile, we're sleeping out back in tents.
examples.chinese: 他们还在弄我们的卧室，这段期间，我们还是在外面的帐篷里睡觉。

examples.english: People keep complaining about the service at hospitals, and meanwhile more and more nurses are losing their jobs.
examples.chinese: 大家在不断抱怨医院的服务质量，而同时却有越来越多的护士失去工作。

examples.english: We'll meet again on April 21st, and in the meantime I'll collect some more information for you.
examples.chinese: 我们在4月21日再见面，在此期间我会给你们多收集一些信息。

examples.english: I came back to work after just a month, but in the meantime, all my things had been moved to a smaller office.
examples.chinese: 仅一个月后我就回去上班了，但在此期间我的所有东西都被人搬至一间小办公室里去了。

centers.english: almost a number, time, or amount
centers.chinese: 将近某个数字、时间或数量

examples.english: I've been a teacher for nearly 10 years now.
examples.chinese: 我当教师差不多快十年了。

examples.english: We had money and almost $1000 in traveler's checks.
examples.chinese: 我们有现金，还有将近1,000美元的旅行支票。

examples.english: John is three years old and Sally is almost six.
examples.chinese: 约翰三岁，萨莉快六岁了。

examples.english: It's very nearly time to go home.
examples.chinese: 马上就到回家的时候了。

examples.english: ‘Is he 60?’ ‘Not quite!’
examples.chinese: “他60岁了吗？”“还没呢！”

examples.english: It's not quite time to go yet.
examples.chinese: 还未到走的时候。

examples.english: The government spends close to $100 billion a year on education.
examples.chinese: 政府一年花在教育上的开支将近一千亿美元。

examples.english: There must be close to a hundred people in the hall.
examples.chinese: 礼堂里肯定有近百人。

examples.english: We drove close on 500 miles Saturday.
examples.chinese: 星期六我们开车开了将近500英里。

examples.english: It was close on midnight by the time they got home.
examples.chinese: 他们到家的时候都快半夜了。

examples.english: In the North East, the unemployment rate is now nearing 20 percent.
examples.chinese: 在东北部，失业率现已接近20%。

examples.english: By now the winter was fast approaching, and further travel would soon be inadvisable.
examples.chinese: 这时候，冬天即将来临，很快就不宜继续前行了。

examples.english: It's getting on for 10 years since we last saw each other.
examples.chinese: 自上次见面之后，我们快有十年没见面了。

examples.english: It must be getting on for lunch time. I'm starving.
examples.chinese: 准是快到午饭时间了，我饿极了。

expressions.text: somebody is getting on for 40, 50 etc

examples.english: ‘How old's Diane?’ ‘She must be getting on for 50.’
examples.chinese: “黛安娜多大年纪了？”“她肯定快50了吧。”

examples.english: Burt's probably pushing 50 by now.
examples.chinese: 伯特现在可能快50岁了。

centers.english: almost all

examples.english: Almost all the wine had been drunk.
examples.chinese: 几乎所有的酒都被喝完了。

examples.english: The burglars took nearly everything in the house that was of any value.
examples.chinese: 窃贼把屋里任何值钱不值钱的东西都几乎全偷走了。

examples.english: Almost everyone in the office has had the flu this year.
examples.chinese: 今年办公室里几乎每个人都得过流感。

examples.english: The frost killed practically every plant in the garden.
examples.chinese: 霜几乎把花园里的所有植物都冻死了。

examples.english: Virtually everyone had gone home.
examples.chinese: 几乎每个人都回家了。

examples.english: I've packed pretty much everything I need for the trip.
examples.chinese: 我把旅途中需要的东西差不多全都收拾好了。

examples.english: It rains more or less every day here in November.
examples.chinese: 到了11月，这里几乎天天下雨。

examples.english: Sonya knew more or less everyone at the party.
examples.chinese: 聚会上的人，索尼娅差不多每个都认识。

examples.english: She's travelled in just about every country in Europe.
examples.chinese: 欧洲各国她几乎全都游历过了。

examples.english: The second-hand shop on the corner sells just about anything.
examples.chinese: 街角的那个旧货店差不多什么都卖。

centers.english: when something is almost true

examples.english: His hair was almost white.
examples.chinese: 他的头发几乎全白了。

examples.english: The moon was almost full that night.
examples.chinese: 那晚，月亮几乎是满月。

examples.english: Persuading Paul to change his mind is nearly impossible.
examples.chinese: 要说服保罗改变主意几乎是不可能的。

examples.english: That's a good answer but it's not quite correct.
examples.chinese: 答得很好，但还没有完全答对。

examples.english: It's not quite red, it's more like a maroon color.
examples.chinese: 这不完全是红色，更有点像褐紫色。

examples.english: The orbits of the planets are almost circular, but not quite.
examples.chinese: 行星的轨道几乎是圆的，但不完全是。

examples.english: The cupboard was practically empty.
examples.chinese: 碗橱几乎空了。

examples.english: Carbon fibre tennis racquets are virtually unbreakable.
examples.chinese: 碳纤维网球拍几乎是牢不可破的。

examples.english: She looks practically the same as his last girlfriend.
examples.chinese: 她长得和他上个女友几乎一模一样。

examples.english: All the rooms are more or less the same size.
examples.chinese: 所有的房间都差不多大小。

examples.english: His jacket was pretty much the same colour as his trousers.
examples.chinese: 他的外套和裤子颜色差不多。

examples.english: Until the 18th century, the region remained more or less independent.
examples.chinese: 18世纪以前，这个地区几乎是独立的。

examples.english: ‘I reckon he's about 65.’ ‘You're close - he's 67.’
examples.chinese: “我猜他65岁左右。”“差不多一他67岁了。”〕

centers.english: when you have almost done something or something has almost happened
centers.chinese: 几乎做完某事，或某事差不多已发生

examples.english: I've almost finished reading the newspaper.
examples.chinese: 我快看完报纸了。

examples.english: It was early 1945, and the war had nearly ended.
examples.chinese: 那是1945年初，战争已接近尾声。

examples.english: Hanson's acting career appears to be pretty much over.
examples.chinese: 汉森的演艺生涯似乎差不多已走到尽头。

examples.english: I had more or less convinced her that I was telling the truth.
examples.chinese: 我差不多已经使她相信，我说的是真话。

examples.english: Communist parties have practically disappeared in Europe.
examples.chinese: 这些政党在欧洲几乎已销声匿迹。

examples.english: Mexico's rainforest has been virtually destroyed.
examples.chinese: 墨西哥的热带雨林几乎全被破坏了。

examples.english: She hasn't quite finished her homework yet.
examples.chinese: 她还没有完全做好功课。

examples.english: Give me five minutes - I'm not quite ready.
examples.chinese: 给我五分钟时间—我还没完全准备好呢。

examples.english: By now the war was all but over.
examples.chinese: 那个时候，战争即将结束。

examples.english: ‘Can we go home now?’ ‘Just one moment - I've all but finished my work.’
examples.chinese: “我们现在可以回家了吗？”“等一会儿—我马上就干完。”

examples.english: The police describe the situation as approaching crisis proportions.
examples.chinese: 警方说事态已几近危急。

examples.english: Dr Dunstable was in a state nearing nervous collapse.
examples.chinese: 邓斯特布尔博士快要精神崩溃了。

examples.english: I felt close to tears as I read Vera's letter.
examples.chinese: 我看着维拉的信，眼泪都快涌出来了。

examples.english: The two countries are on the brink of war.
examples.chinese: 两国之间战争一触即发。

expressions.text: be on the verge of tears/death/hysteria etc

examples.english: Kerry is on the verge of a nervous breakdown.
examples.chinese: 克里精神濒于崩溃。

centers.english: when something almost happens but does not
centers.chinese: 某事差一点发生，但并没有发生

examples.english: I was laughing so hard I almost fell out of my chair.
examples.chinese: 我笑得差一点从椅子上掉下来。

examples.english: The terrorists almost succeeded in blowing up the President's limousine.
examples.chinese: 恐怖分子差一点就炸掉了总统的轿车。

examples.english: She was very lucky. She very nearly lost her life.
examples.chinese: 她非常幸运，差一点就没命了。

examples.english: She was so angry that she came very close to walking out of the meeting.
examples.chinese: 她气得差点儿就走出会议室。

examples.english: Dad came near to changing his mind about lending me the car.
examples.chinese: 爸爸差一点就改变主意不借车给我了。

examples.english: The negotiations were very near to breaking down.
examples.chinese: 谈判几近破裂。

examples.english: Coulson came within inches of death on a climbing trip in the Himalayas last year.
examples.chinese: 库尔森去年攀登喜马拉雅山时险些送命。

expressions.text: come within an inch of doing something

examples.english: Manchester United came within an inch of losing the game.
examples.chinese: 曼彻斯特联队险些输掉比赛。

examples.english: Flying into the airport at Lima, we narrowly avoided a collision with another plane.
examples.chinese: 飞进利马机场时，我们差一点就撞上了另一架飞机。

examples.english: The article says Meyers narrowly escaped arrest in Rome last month.
examples.chinese: 这篇文章说，上个月迈耶斯在罗马差一点就落网。

examples.english: A woman and child had a very narrow escape they were almost killed when their car hit a tree near Hartford last night.
examples.chinese: 昨晚在哈特福德附近，一名女子和一名儿童死里逃生，当时他们的汽车撞到一棵树上。

examples.english: Mike's had two motorcycle accidents, plus a few other pretty close shaves.
examples.chinese: 迈克两次遭遇摩托车事故，另外还有几次死里逃生的经历。

examples.english: The Labour party won the election, but it was a very near thing.
examples.chinese: 工党当选，但是赢得真险。

examples.english: Security forces managed to free the hostages, but it was a very close thing.
examples.chinese: 安全部队设法救了人质，但那真是虎口余生啊。

examples.english: The guard began to search the pile of leaves where we were hiding, but then got distracted by a noise from the house. ‘Phew, that was close!’ said John. ‘C'mon, let's get out of here!’
examples.chinese: 卫兵开始搜查我们正躲着的那堆树叶，这时屋里的一个声音使他们分了神。“唷，好险啊！”约翰说道，“快，我们快离开这儿！”

centers.english: approximately a number or amount

examples.english: It should cost about $1500.
examples.chinese: 这得花1,500美元左右。

examples.english: The church is about a mile away.
examples.chinese: 教堂离这里大约有一英里。

examples.english: It's been about five years since I've seen Linda.
examples.chinese: 我有大概五年没见到琳达了。

examples.english: The chance of men being born colourblind is about 1 in 12.
examples.chinese: 男性生来色盲的可能性大概十二个里面有一个。

examples.english: Approximately 30% of the community is Polish.
examples.chinese: 该社区大约有30%的成员为波兰人。

examples.english: Each disk stores approximately 144 pages of text.
examples.chinese: 每张磁盘大约可以储存144页文本。

examples.english: A new kitchen would cost roughly $6,000.
examples.chinese: 一个新的厨房大概要花6,000美元。

examples.english: The man was roughly my own age.
examples.chinese: 那人大概和我一般年纪。

examples.english: There were roughly 50 people there.
examples.chinese: 那里大约有50个人。

expressions.text: 3 days/a minute/fifteen people etc or so

examples.english: The baby usually sleeps for an hour or so after breakfast.
examples.chinese: 这婴孩通常在早餐后要睡一小时左右。

examples.english: He suggested that I take a week or so off work.
examples.chinese: 他建议我休假一星期左右。

examples.english: There must be thirty people or so in the class.
examples.chinese: 班上肯定有三十人左右。

examples.english: ‘How old do you think he is?’ ‘Oh, I don't know. Seventy odd.’
examples.chinese: “你觉得他多大年纪了？”“噢，我不知道，七十多岁吧。”

examples.english: It's been 30 odd years since I last saw him.
examples.chinese: 自从我上次见过他之后，已经有三十多年没见面了。

expressions.text: give or take a few miles/a couple of minutes/a pound etc

examples.english: The village is about fifty miles north of here, give or take a few miles.
examples.chinese: 该村庄位于从这里往北大约五十英里的地方，上下相差不了几英里。

examples.english: He's said to be worth $26 million, give or take a few million.
examples.chinese: 据说他的身价为2,600万美元左右。

examples.english: ‘How long will the meeting last?’ ‘A couple of hours, give or take.’
examples.chinese: “会议要开多长时间？”“几个小时左右吧。”

examples.english: At a guess, I'd say around 3000 people took part in the demonstration.
examples.chinese: 我估计大约有3,000人参加了游行示威。

examples.english: It was a cold night. About two or three degrees at a guess.
examples.chinese: 那是一个寒冷的夜晚，气温估计只有两三度。

examples.english: ‘How much will it cost?’ ‘A hundred and twenty pounds, at a guess.’
examples.chinese: “这要多少钱？”“估计要120英镑。”

centers.english: approximately a large number or amount
centers.chinese: 大约为某个很大的数量

examples.english: The business is worth something in the region of $25m.
examples.chinese: 这笔生意价值2,500万美元左右。

examples.english: The universe is estimated to be somewhere in the region of eleven billion years old.
examples.chinese: 宇宙估计有大约110亿年的历史了。

examples.english: A typical price would be somewhere in the region of £2,500 per person.
examples.chinese: 价格通常为每人2,500英镑左右。

examples.english: Smith is already something like $10,000 in debt.
examples.chinese: 史密斯已欠了约莫一万美元的债务。

examples.english: In the USA something like 4000 such accidents occur each year.
examples.chinese: 这样的事故在美国每年要发生大约4,000起。

expressions.text: an estimated 3000 people/one million pounds/90% of profits etc

examples.english: The event was seen on television by an estimated 250 million people worldwide.
examples.chinese: 估计全世界有两亿五千万人通过电视看到了这一事件。

examples.english: An estimated 10% of new mothers suffer from severe depression.
examples.chinese: 估计有10%的新妈妈患有严重的抑郁症。

examples.english: By the end of the month an estimated 1000 people had been killed and 42,000 left homeless.
examples.chinese: 到月底估计有1,000人丧生，42,000人无家可归。

examples.english: Among the 11 factory sites across Europe, some 2,600 jobs are to be eliminated this year.
examples.chinese: 欧洲境内的11家工厂今年将减少大约2,600个职位。

examples.english: He lectured at the Institut Pasteur for some 50 years.
examples.chinese: 他在巴斯德研究所讲了50年左右的学。

examples.english: How can you be tired? You slept for ten hours or more last night.
examples.chinese: 你怎么会累呢？昨晚你可睡了十多个小时。

examples.english: There were a thousand or more fans at the airport to welcome the band.
examples.chinese: 有一千多名歌迷在机场迎接该乐队。

centers.english: at approximately a particular time or date
centers.chinese: 大约在某个时间或日期

examples.english: It's two-thirty. They should be arriving about now.
examples.chinese: 2点半了，现在他们差不多该到了。

examples.english: The cathedral was completed in about the middle of the 16th century.
examples.chinese: 大教堂于16世纪中叶前后建成。

examples.english: About six months ago he suffered a major heart attack.
examples.chinese: 大约六个月以前他心脏病严重发作了一次。

examples.english: I picked Sue up around eight o' clock.
examples.chinese: 我是大约8点钟去接休的。

examples.english: I don't remember the exact date of the party, but it must have been around the first of December.
examples.chinese: 我记不得聚会的确切日期，但肯定是在12月1日前后。

examples.english: He left the house round about four o'clock.
examples.chinese: 他4点钟左右离开那房子。

examples.english: The job should be finished round about March next year.
examples.chinese: 这项工作要在明年3月份左右完成。

examples.english: The gate will close approximately two minutes before the train leaves.
examples.chinese: 检票口在火车发车前两分钟左右关闭。

examples.english: Tours start approximately every 15-20 minutes in summer.
examples.chinese: 夏季大约每15至20分钟就有观光团出发。

examples.english: The burglary must have happened some time after 8:00 p.m.
examples.chinese: 入室盗窃案一定是在晚上8点以后的某个时间发生的。

examples.english: His third symphony was written some time between 1750 and 1753.
examples.chinese: 他的第三交响曲大约于1750至1753年间写成。

examples.english: The clinic was closed some time in the early nineties.
examples.chinese: 这个诊所在90年代初停业了。

expressions.text: at 10 o'clock or thereabouts/in the 1950s or thereabouts etc

examples.english: They're old apartments, built in the 1930s or thereabouts.
examples.chinese: 这些是旧公寓，建于20世纪30年代左右。

examples.english: The book will be published in May or thereabouts.
examples.chinese: 这本书将在5月份前后出版。

examples.english: The manuscripts date from circa 400 B.C.
examples.chinese: 这手稿可以追溯到大约公元前400年左右。

examples.english: a Robert Adam mansion, built circa 1778
examples.chinese: 罗伯特·亚当设计的宅第，约建于1778年

examples.english: The picture shows Tsar Nicholas, circa 1914.
examples.chinese: 画上是沙皇尼古拉，时间约在1914年。

centers.english: approximately correct

examples.english: Yes, that's roughly the right answer.
examples.chinese: 对，那样回答大致正确。

examples.english: As long as you know roughly how to do it, that's fine.
examples.chinese: 只要你大体上知道怎么做就行了。

examples.english: What she says is more or less true.
examples.chinese: 她说的大致都是真的。

examples.english: ‘Did they have what you were looking for at the hardware store?’ ‘Yes, more or less.’
examples.chinese: “五金店有你要买的东西吗？”“有，差不多都有吧。”

examples.english: It's kind of circular-shaped, but not exactly.
examples.chinese: 形状有点圆，但又不完全是圆的。

examples.english: ‘Did you finish your homework?’ ‘Well, sort of.’
examples.chinese: “你做完功课了吗？”“嗯，差不多吧。

examples.english: ‘I'd think a project like this would take at least five years to complete.’ ‘Not quite as long as that, but you're in the right ballpark.’
examples.chinese: “我想这样一个项目至少要五年才能完成吧。”“没那么长，不过也差不多吧。”

centers.english: a number or amount that is approximately right

examples.english: The measurements are approximate, but I think they'll do.
examples.chinese: 这是个大概的尺寸，但是我想可以了。

examples.english: An expert could give you the approximate value of the painting.
examples.chinese: 专家会给你这幅画作出大概的估价。

examples.english: Approximate journey time to London is four hours.
examples.chinese: 去伦敦的行程大约是四小时。

examples.english: The report should give you a rough indication of the company's stock market performance over the past year.
examples.chinese: 报告大概反映了公司过去一年里的股市表现。

examples.english: I'd say that the whole thing would cost you around $1000, but that's just a rough estimate.
examples.chinese: 我说全部加起来可能要1,000美元左右，不过这只是个大概的估计。

examples.english: A ballpark figure for the cost of the construction is $4.5 million.
examples.chinese: 建筑成本大概是450万美元。

examples.english: A firm price hasn't been set yet, but the ballpark figure under discussion is $3 million.
examples.chinese: 价格尚未确定，但在讨论的大概是300万美元。

examples.english: Could you give me a ballpark figure?
examples.chinese: 你能给我一个大概的数字吗？

examples.english: Five thousand dollars seems to be a reasonable approximation of the actual cost.
examples.chinese: 5,000美元看来是对实际费用比较合理的估计。

centers.english: again

examples.english: If you're late again we'll leave without you.
examples.chinese: 如果你再迟到，我们不等你就走了。

examples.english: The floor needs cleaning again.
examples.chinese: 地板又要打扫了。

examples.english: It was nice to see you again.
examples.chinese: 再见到你真好。

examples.english: Julie! It's your sister on the phone again.
examples.chinese: 朱莉！你姐姐又来电话了。

examples.english: The fresh mountain air soon made Jennifer feel strong again.
examples.chinese: 山上的新鲜空气很快就使珍妮弗感到自己的身体又强健起来了。

examples.english: When I was safely back in my apartment again, I took out the letter and read it.
examples.chinese: 我平安地又回到公寓后，就取出那封信来读。

examples.english: Once again, the French army were totally humiliated.
examples.chinese: 法军再一次遭到极大的羞辱。
